Insert coin. (Continue.)
Only the tension in his posture betrays his interest.
Technically the human beings *are* at the top of the food-chain, so...
Too bad no one was around to witness your triumph. It was a 'high score' after all.
The amount of focus it takes to predict when a ball-goat is in striking range is staggering, but it *can* be done. Focus!
Over there, in the corner?
Get the game on, finger-boy! Those flippers are *ready*.
Hit the pads as fast as you can, maximum attack -- not even a bullet can get through!
Cuno doesn't fucking care, he repeats, "That shit's nothing to Cuno."
The kid shrugs. He doesn't care.
The machine needs a good service before anyone else can even attempt it again.
*That's* what they should've turned into a pinball game.
Why do they even make these if it's impossible to win?! [Give up, winning is too stressful.]
Mhmh, I've been wondering the same.
Tiny hammer shatters something inside the machine. Something glass.
Inspect the playing field.
“Wait, but what about Smolka, Jandiroba, Stirneman, and Schmelling?”
It's time to move on. [Leave.]
Feels like a *lot*. Too much to play it again.
There goes nothing, finger-boy.

Actor: Cornelius Gurdi and the Mountain Goats
En: So violent is it's descent it immediately knocks the last goat into the abyss, then proceeds to bounce all across the board as if carried by some demonic other-wordly inertia.
The pinball machine?
You're in a perfectly balanced system, where neither you nor the pale is able to get the upper hand. This could go on forever.

Actor: Cornelius Gurdi and the Mountain Goats
En: Suddenly a special passage, leading to the summit, slides open at the top of the board. This is where the balls need to go.
This *can* be navigated. The balls leave an almost imperceptible 'disruptions' in the fog. Use them to calculate where they hit next.

Actor: Cornelius Gurdi and the Mountain Goats
En: Maneuvering a goat-ball into a position for a perfect hit isn't easy. More fall to their deaths, but finally the opportunity presents itself. One of them gets through.

Actor: Cornelius Gurdi and the Mountain Goats
En: Areas around the playfield represent Gurdi’s climb: Places he was said to have camped, which the goats can discover. Get them to the summit!
Indeed. Think of them as balls.
Pig's got mad timing. Fast-twitch fiber shit, interceptor shit. Animal instinct-style.
Congratulations. The lieutentant nods. "This is where the game ends. It's a cheap way of getting more money out of the players. A stupid, nihilistic finale."
That is *not* cool.

Mesque culture holds a special place for the mountain goat, especially those on Corpus Mundi -- the only creature fearless and agile enough to climb the mountain. They are often associated with Gurdi.
The kid doesn't reply. He's squinting, eyes desperately trying to pierce the relentless fog.
Ignore the machine.
How, if you can't even see it?
Despite the colorful images on the backglass, there's definitely something sinister about the guy.
Life has left it, unlikely to ever return.
Not just any pinball machine. This is the pinnacle of pinball. 'Cornelius Gurdi and the Mountain Goats'. It's lit. You can't wait to get your hands on it.
Of course. He nods. "I still think you earned the right to be at least a little proud."
Hello again, officer.
Zh: “又见面了，警官。”

En: The Return, well... It's part urban myth -- part political science. It's a fool's hope, sir, and it's also all I've got. She smiles. "They say there will be an *event*. That it will happen somewhere here..."
Zh: 她笑了。“归来部分算是都市神话——部分算是政治学。它是一个愚蠢的希望，先生，而且它也是我所拥有的一切。他们说会发生一件*大事*。就在这里的某个地方……”

En: A warm column of air rises around you -- up to the falling rain -- encircling the building you stand on. You feel its twisting shape, bits of paper and birds on the *thermal*. Along with several million litres of water. All rising back into the great Insulindian sky.\n
Zh: 你周围升腾起一阵暖流——直至空中的落雨——将你脚下的建筑团团围住。你能感到它那扭曲的形状，纸屑和小鸟就乘在*热气流*之上。还有数百万升的海水。全都升向伟大的伊苏林迪的天空。

En: A column of air rises around you, encircling the building you stand on. You feel its twisting shape, bits of paper and birds -- on the *thermal*. Along with several million litres of water as the snow melts on the ground. All rising up into the great Insulindian sky.\n
Zh: 你周围升腾起一阵暖流，将你脚下的建筑团团围住。你能感到它那扭曲的形状，纸屑还有小鸟——就在*热气流*之上。还有数百万升的海水。全都升向伟大的伊苏林迪的天空。

En: A warm column of air rises around you, encircling the building you stand on. You feel its twisting shape, bits of paper and birds -- on the *thermal*. Along with several million litres of water. All rising up into the great Insulindian sky.
Zh: 你周围升腾起一阵暖流，将你脚下的建筑团团围住。你能感到它那扭曲的形状，纸屑还有小鸟——就在*热气流*之上。还有数百万升的海水。全都升向伟大的伊苏林迪的天空。

En: Nothing happens -- absolutely nothing. A mass of droplets above the planetary body, colourless, merging into another, raining down on you.
Zh: 什么也没有发生——完全没有。这颗行星之上有大量的液滴，色彩苍白，彼此交融，滴落在你身上。

En: Nothing happens -- absolutely nothing. A mass of droplets above the planetary body, colourless, merging into one another -- scattering snow over the city and the sea.
Zh: 什么也没有发生——完全没有。这颗行星之上有大量的液滴，色彩苍白，彼此交融——雪花飘散在城市和海面上。

En: Nothing happens -- there is a mass of droplets above the planetary body, colourless, merging into one another...
Zh: 什么也没有发生——这颗行星之上有大量的液滴，色彩苍白，彼此交融……

En: The sky is more black than grey; you hear the distant murmur of motor-connections somewhere south of here. That is all.
Zh: 天空是比灰更浓的黑；你听见南部某个遥远的地方传来发动机低沉的声音。仅此而已。

En: So, officer... the young woman says with a sigh.
Zh: “所以，警官……”年轻女子叹了口气，说到。

En: So, officer, what were we talking about -- in the final hours of this twilight world?
Zh: “所以，警官，我们会说些什么呢——在这个黄昏世界的最后时刻？”

En: Silence, officer. Wouldn't have thought you the silent type... What were we talking about?
Zh: “沉默，警官。没想到你是那种沉默的人……我们说些什么呢？”

En: So, officer. Sad silence -- a lamentation? What were we talking about?
Zh: “所以，警官，悲伤的沉默——是在悲叹吗？我们说些什么呢？”

En: So, superstar law official -- what were we talking about?
Zh: “所以，超级明星执法人员——我们说些什么呢？”

En: The sky slowly slides above you, raining down. The young woman exhales, there's menthol tobacco in the air.
Zh: 天空慢慢从你的头顶滑过，雨滴随之降落。年轻女子呼出一口气，空气中弥漫着薄荷烟草的味道。

En: The sky slowly slides above you, snowing down. The young woman exhales, there's menthol tobacco in the snowy air.
Zh: 天空慢慢从你的头顶滑过，雪花片片飘落。年轻女子呼出一口气，空气中弥漫着薄荷烟草的味道。

En: Continuously nothing. The sky slowly slides above you. The young woman exhales, the scent of menthol tobacco. Sounds like a sigh.
Zh: 这份虚无绵延不断。天空慢慢从你的头顶滑过。年轻女子呼出一口气，散发出薄荷烟草的味道。听起来像是一声叹息。

En: The sky is low and cold. You hear the distant murmur of motor-connections somewhere south of here. That is all.
Zh: 天空低沉而阴冷。你听见南部某个遥远的地方传来发动机低沉的声音。除此之外，什么也没有发生。

En: From the vapour, a great cumulus is formed. A cloud castle one thousand metres high. A flock of seagulls screams, hitch-hiking on the thermal. They too are part of the procession, the honour watch.
Zh: 水蒸汽中形成了一团巨大的积雨云。那是一座几千米高空上的云之城堡。一群海鸥在尖声鸣叫，搭乘着热气流展翅翱翔。它们也是队伍的一部分，荣耀的守护者。

En: Shudder.
Zh: 颤抖。

En: Because it's warm -- from the gathering spring; from the oceanic current flowing into the bay; the people on the streets; and the machines that hum in the harbour. Heavy fuel oils too.
Zh: 因为很温暖——因为春意正在集聚；从洋流汇入海湾；街上的行人们；还有港口的机器传来阵阵轰鸣。重燃油也是一样。

En: The Return of *what* exactly?
Zh: “到底是*什么*的归来？”

En: Everyone is a guest on this isola. A guest of the air and the water, and the humidity between. You are as well, surely.
Zh: 在这个洲上，所有人都是客人。空气、海水、还有两者之间湿气的客人。肯定的是，你也一样。

En: Miss. What are you waiting for?
Zh: “小姐。你在等什么？”

En: I'm waiting for the miracle to happen. It'll take one to get me out of the mess I'm in.
Zh: “我在等待奇迹发生。只有它才能帮我摆脱现在的困境。”

En: Revert your eyes to the rooftop and shudder.
Zh: 让你的目光回到屋顶，微微颤抖。

En: Is she safe here?
Zh: 她在这里安全吗？

En: As safe as you keep her.
Zh: 就像你对她的保护一样安全。

En: Her thought trails off. The wind picks up again. Above her, a great cumulus cloud rises -- ready to fall down as precipitation. April will come, then May; a month, maybe two from now... you shudder. The feeling dissipates. The thought ends.
Zh: 她的思绪开始飘散。又起风了。在她上面，一团巨大的积雨云渐渐升起——准备像雨滴一样落下。四月会来的，然后是五月；从现在算起，一个月，也许是两个月……你颤抖着。这种感觉正在消散。想法也跟着结束了。

En: Of the king? Of the nation? Of communism? Return on investment? She tilts her head. "I don't know exactly. It's meant to be vague, as promises generally are."
Zh: “国王？国家？康米主义？投资回报率？”她歪起脑袋。“我也不太清楚。它注定是模糊的，承诺通常就是这样。”

En: What is the Return?
Zh: “这个归来是什么？”

En: Don't you know, sir? I thought you were from around here. You look like *Mr. Revachol*.
Zh: “你不知道吗，先生？我还以为你是本地人。你看起来很像一个*瑞瓦肖绅士*。”

En: The Return, well... It's part urban myth -- part political science. It's a fool's hope, sir, and it's also all I've got. They say there will be an *event*. That it will happen somewhere here... She looks around, then at the sky.
Zh: “归来，好吧……部分算是都市神话——部分算是政治学。它是一个愚蠢的希望，先生，而且它也是我所拥有的一切。他们说会发生一件*大事*。就在这里的某个地方……”

En: For whom?
Zh: 为了谁？

En: For her. The guest. Waiting.
Zh: 为了她。客人。等待。

En: What is she waiting for?
Zh: 她在等待什么？

En: For Gloria. Soon these clouds will all fall down as even greater rain. Spring will come. Two more months. Maybe less. It's time.
Zh: 荣耀。很快，这些云会化作更大的雨点滴落下来。春天会来的。最多两个月。也许更快。到时间了。

En: For Gloria. Soon these clouds will all fall down as rain. Spring will come. Two more months. Maybe less. It's time.
Zh: 荣耀。很快，这些云会化作雨点滴落下来。春天会来的。最多两个月。也许更快。到时间了。

En: You mean here -- in Martinaise? She takes a drag, slow and long...
Zh: “你是说这里——马丁内斯吗？”她抽了一口烟，缓慢而悠长……

En: Nod.
Zh: 点点头。

En: Never mind. Tell me something else.
Zh: “不要紧。跟我说点别的吧。”

En: Sure.
Zh: “当然。”

En: And what would that miracle be?
Zh: “那是什么样的奇迹呢？”

En: And what miracle would that be?
Zh: “那又是什么奇迹呢？”

En: The Return, of course. She smiles. "Now, I know I'm not from *around* here -- I would only be hitch-hiking. The Return is a big hit in the industrial espionage circles. A lot of desperate, seedy types there. All screwed in this unipolar world."
Zh: “当然是归来了。”她笑了。“我知道我不是*本地*人——我只是搭个顺风車。工业间谍圈子里，归来是大热的话题。那里有很多绝望又没精打采的人。全都在这个单极世界搞砸了。”

En: Humour me.
Zh: “满足一下我吧。”

En: Oh, you don't know? I drank so much I can't remember my entire life. Turns out that can happen to a human being.
Zh: “哦，你不知道吗？我喝了太多酒，把自己的整个人生都忘记了。事实证明，这种事会发生在人类身上。”

En: You know I'm a bit spotty -- about a great many *things*. Like being a cop for example.
Zh: “你知道的，我有点模糊——对很多*事情*都是。比如说，我是警察的事。”

En: As I said -- there's talk. In the competitive intelligence crowd. A lot of people, like me, who need... a new colour on the map. It's all blue, you see. And that blue doesn't like us.
Zh: “我说过了——有一种传闻。在竞争情报圈里。很多人，就像我，都需要……地图上出现一种新的颜色。你能看见，它全都是蓝色的。而且蓝色并不喜欢我们。”

En: *Le Retour...* the lieutenant says. His forehead furrowed, he puts his notes down.
Zh: “*归来*……”警督说到。他皱起眉头，记下笔记。

En: How soon will it happen?
Zh: “多久会发生？”

En: Is this why you said you would like to surrender in a *free* Revachol?
Zh: “这就是你说想向一个*自由的*瑞瓦肖自首的原因吗？”

En: Thank you -- one last question. Do you *really* think it will happen? (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢你——最后一个问题。你*真的*觉得它会发生吗？”（总结。）

En: Some political event? How do you know of this?
Zh: “某种政治活动？你怎么会知道的？”

En: Yeah... She looks at the sky, then at you. "I guess it is."
Zh: “是啊……”她看向天空，然后看着你。“我想是吧。”

En: So she thinks -- at least partly -- that this Return will feature a self-governing Revachol. She has already placed her bets.
Zh: 所以她觉得——至少部分如此——这个归来会让瑞瓦肖变成一个自治的地方。她已经下定了自己的赌注。

En: Soon. But it's been *soon* for almost half a century now, so don't hold your breath.
Zh: “很快。不过这个*很快*到现在几乎已经过去半个世纪了，所以也不要抱太大希望。”

En: It's an empty vessel, an amphora waiting to be filled. Something always *returns* from the past -- it's how the future happens. And when it does, many guesses turn out to have been right.
Zh: 它是一个空容器，一个等待被填满的双耳瓶。过去的某些东西总会*归来——未来就是这样发生的。而且，当它发生的时候，很多猜测最后都是正确的。

En: You came all this way because of a rumour?
Zh: “你就是因为一个传言大老远跑过来的？”

En: No, I came all this way on a false passport and some money. To hide, mostly. In the most volatile part of the Reál Belt, where it's easiest to disappear.
Zh: “不。我大老远跑来是因为一个假护照和钱。主要是为了藏身。雷亚尔货币体系中最不稳定的部分，就是就容易消失的地方。”

En: Where the walls are still porous and you can slip through. This is just... something to get me through the night you know. A little *espérance*.
Zh: “那里的墙壁上还有气孔，你可以从里面溜过去。这只是……某种能让我安然度过夜晚的东西。一个小小的*希望*。”

En: Still, the story influenced her choice of where to run. It must have some hidden layers...
Zh: 尽管如此，故事还是影响了她逃往何处的选择。它肯定还有一些隐藏的层面……

En: No need to worry, lieutenant. She turns to the lieutenant. "I'm sure the streets will not erupt in seditious violence just yet."
Zh: 她转向警督。“不用担心，警督。我肯定街头还不会爆发煽动性的暴力行为。”

En: These streets? He looks over the railing. "I'm not so sure, miss."
Zh: “这些街道？”他越过栏杆看去。“我不是很肯定，小姐。”

En: I do. She lights another one.
Zh: “我信。”她又点燃一支。

En: In Revachol. Revachol West. They say it will happen soon -- and that it will change everything.
Zh: 她看了看周围，接着抬头仰望天空，然后继续说到：“在瑞瓦肖。瑞瓦肖西部。他们说很快就会发生——而且它会改变一切。”

En: Why does it rise?
Zh: 它为什么会上升呢？

En: But at the same time, I mean -- things can't go on like this forever. Something will give. It always does.
Zh: “不过，与此同时，我是说——-事情也不会永远这样下去。要放弃一些东西。总是这样的。”

En: So do I.
Zh: “我也是。”

En: I don't.
Zh: “我不信。”

En: I agree, detective. The lieutenant looks to the sea. "Something is coming -- *trouble*. It will be a hard spring. I don't know what exactly, but..." He shakes his head.
Zh: “我同意，警探。”警探望向大海。“有什么事要发生了——*麻烦*。这将会是一个难熬的春季。我不知道具体是什么，不过……”他摇摇头。

En: Something is happening. In this city. She nods -- a puff of smoke. "I can feel it. When I'm out here at night..."
Zh: 她点点头。一缕轻烟升腾而起。“有事正在发生。在这座城市里。我能感觉到。每当晚上我走到这里的时候……”

En: Don't be so sure. The lieutenant looks to the sea. "Something is coming -- *trouble*. It will be a hard spring. I don't know what it is, exactly, but..." He shakes his head.
Zh: “别这么肯定。”警探望向大海。“有什么事要发生了——*麻烦*。这将会是一个难熬的春季。我不知道具体是什么，不过……”他摇摇头。

En: Something is happening -- in this city. I can feel it. When I'm out here at night...
Zh: 她摇摇头。“有事正在发生。在这座城市里。我能感觉到。每当晚上我走到这里的时候……”

En: Something is happening in this city. She nods -- a puff of smoke. "I can feel it. When I'm out here at night."
Zh: 她点点头。一缕轻烟升腾而起。“有事正在发生。在这座城市里。我能感觉到。每当晚上我走到这里的时候……”

En: She probably didn't give you her real name either -- why would she? Arrest her immediately, before she further entangles you in her *web of lies*!
Zh: 她可能不会把真名告诉您的——她为什么要这么做？马上逮捕她，在您被她编造的*谎言之网*继续纠缠之前！

En: She probably didn't make the god damn call either. And her name is not Klaasje Amandou -- why would she give her real name? Arrest her immediately!
Zh: 连那通该死的电话可能都不是她打的。而且她的名字也不叫卡拉洁·阿曼多——她为什么要说出真名呢？马上逮捕她！

En: Samaran boxing, or Sam Bo. Graceful martial arts stuff -- stealth, cleverness, and cool.
Zh: 萨马拉拳击，或者又称为萨拳。优雅的武术类的东西——潜行，机敏又镇静。

En: Wait! If you arrest her, Kim will have to transport her. You'd be without your partner for the rest of the day...
Zh: 等等！如果你逮捕她，金就要负责押送她。接下来的一天，你的搭档就不在身边了……

En: SHUT UP! He's making a decision and it's *his* to make.
Zh: 闭嘴！他正在做决定，而且这是*他的*决定。

En: In her bedroom, inside?
Zh: 在她的卧室里面？

En: Not now...
Zh: 现在不行……

En: Yes -- you see the glass sparkling out of the corner of your eye...
Zh: 是的——你能看见玻璃在你眼角的余光中闪闪发光……

En: Still you see the glass sparkling out of the corner of your eye...
Zh: 你还能看见玻璃在你眼角的余光中闪闪发光……

En: Where the cold oceanic expanse lies -- and behind it, another world.
Zh: 冰冷的海洋无尽延伸的地方——还有在那之后，是另一个世界。

En: Actually -- this murder did have *a little* to do with her.
Zh: 其实——这起凶杀案确实跟她*有一点*关系。

En: But she *really* destroyed them. She still feels it.
Zh: 但是她*真的*毁掉他们了。她还能感觉到。

En: Oops...
Zh: 哎呀……

En: She's getting scared now. Of you -- the downstairs people. All of it.
Zh: 现在她开始害怕了。害怕你——还有楼下那群人。所有一切。

En: True, sire. 'Tis true.
Zh: 是真的，大人。这是真的。

En: Which *is* an indicator of truth...
Zh: 意味着她说的*的确*是实话……

En: Very good, sire. She will never admit to it, but we can *see* that she didn't.
Zh: 非常好，大人。她永远不会承认的，不过我们能*看出*她没有做。

En: She fears an arrest right here and now. This has been an awful turn of events for her.
Zh: 她害怕被当场逮捕。对她来说，事情发生了一个可怕的变化。

En: (Now that Kim's here) "What if I told you you're under arrest, Katarzine Alasije?"
Zh: （既然金已经在这里了）“如果我说，你被逮捕了呢，卡特琳·艾拉洁？”

En: (Now that Kim's here) "What if I told you you're under arrest, Klaasje?"
Zh: （既然金已经在这里了）“如果我说，你被逮捕了呢，卡拉洁？”

En: (Now that Kim's here) "What if I told you you're under arrest, Miss Oranje Disco Dancer?"
Zh: （既然金已经在这里了）“如果我说，你被逮捕了呢，奥兰治迪斯科舞者小姐？”

En: (Now that Kim's here) "What if I told you you're under arrest, miss."
Zh: （既然金已经在这里了）“如果我说，你被逮捕了呢，小姐。”

En: You *are*. This is not the end of this.
Zh: 你*就是*。这还没有结束。

En: That is *a little* strange... people don't usually keep things together under stress like that.
Zh: 有*一点*奇怪……处于那种压力之下的时候，人们通常没办法保持镇定。

En: I promise I will be right here all the time -- nights too. I'll help you *any* way I can in this investigation and I *will* be at Station 41 in six weeks. She repeats. "I promise."
Zh: “我保证我会一直待在这里——晚上也是。在调查过程中，我会*尽我所能*的帮助你，而且六周之后，我*会*出现在41分局。”她重复到。“我保证。”

En: It's a shame we never got around to doing a thorough autopsy... He shakes his head. "And when was the tampering -- twenty minutes after death?"
Zh: “很遗憾，我们还没抽出时间来做一次全面的尸检……”他摇摇头。“破坏尸体是什么时候的事——死后20分钟吗？”

En: I understand. She puts her coffee mug on the table.
Zh: “我理解。”她把咖啡杯放在桌子上。

En: This is good. Clear the air first -- between you two -- then move on to questions.
Zh: 这样很好。先消除误会——你们俩之间的——然后再提问题。

En: No, it's not good. It's the opposite of that. This will let her dictate the terms of your...
Zh: 不，这样不好。正好相反。这会让她支配你的……

En: Whoah, whoah, whoah! *I* am The Law. The Law -- that's Me.
Zh: “哇，哇，哇！*我*才是执法人员。法律——就是我。”

En: The Hardie boys are *not* the law. We are.
Zh: “哈迪兄弟*不是*执法人员。我们才是。”

En: Hold on there. The Hardie boys are *not* the law -- I am.
Zh: “先停一下。哈迪兄弟*不是*执法人员——我才是。”

En: Cool. I'm satisfied with this explanation.
Zh: “行。我对这个解释挺满意的。”

En: Who among us, having been in love, can say they haven't *thought* about it?
Zh: “在所有那些有过恋爱经历的人中间，谁敢说自己没有*想过*呢？”

En: She definitely called the cops. That was *task complete*. It could not hath been a *lie*. That is impossible.
Zh: 她肯定打给警察了。那是*完成任务*。这绝对不是一个*谎言*。这不可能。

En: Oh god. That was a lie too. Who made the call then?
Zh: 天啦。如果那也是撒谎的话，那到底是谁报的警。

En: She did, of course.
Zh: 当然是她了。

En: Who... what? Dear lord, you've been lied to. She could have killed her lover and lied to everyone! She's not the most *faire and juste* at all, she's smoke and mirrors and will-o-wisps!
Zh: 谁……什么？天啦，您被骗了！她可能杀了自己的爱人，然后欺骗了所有人！她根本不是最*美丽和公正*的女人，她是烟雾，是镜子，是鬼火！

En: Who... what... Dear god, you've been lied to! She could have killed her lover and lied to everyone! She's not *candid* at all, she's smoke and mirrors and will-o-wisps!
Zh: 谁……什么……天啦，您被骗了！她可能杀了自己的爱人，然后欺骗了所有人！她完全没有*坦白*，她是烟雾，是镜子，是鬼火！

En: She probably didn't make the god damn call either -- why would she? She was too busy abusing morphine and *cocainum!* Arrest her immediately!
Zh: 她可能根本就没有打那通该死的电话——她为什么要这么做呢？她忙着滥用吗啡和*可卡因*！马上逮捕她！

En: Look at her! Her hands, her nails, the cigarette... Every second you stall, you become more entangled in this spider's *web of lies*. Arrest her immediately!
Zh: 看看她！她的手，她的指甲，还有烟……你每拖延一秒，就会越来越陷入这个*谎言*的蛛网。马上逮捕她！

En: She has this *thing* for me -- ever since I met her and the boys downstairs. She's been pretty frank about what she wants...
Zh: “她对我有些*好感*——自从我在楼下见到她和哈迪兄弟之后。她对自己想要什么一直很坦率……”

En: Okay, I'll keep you here, miss. For now. (Back off completely.)
Zh: “好吧。我需要你留在这里，小姐。暂时。”（完全退下。）

En: Who?
Zh: 谁？

En: That's all good, but I think we should still take you in -- just in case. (Step a little bit closer.)
Zh: “一切都挺好的，不过我想我们还是要把你拘留起来——以防万一。”（稍微靠近一步。）

En: That's all about Ruby for now.
Zh: “露比的事先说到这里。”

En: Okay. That's it for Ruby. (Wrap it up.)
Zh: “好吧。露比的事就到这里。”（总结。）

En: Okay? And WHAT?!?
Zh: 好吧？什么？！？

En: Arrest the liar! Now.
Zh: 逮捕那个骗子！现在。

En: Let's talk about you and Ruby again.
Zh: “再说说你和露比的事吧。”

En: Just like that. No resistance -- her shoulders are slouched, her feet long and straight.
Zh: 就像那样。没有抵抗——她耷拉着肩膀，脚伸的又长又直。

En: You sent us on a run-around.
Zh: “你带我们兜了个圈子。”

En: You *lied* to us, miss.
Zh: “你对我们*撒谎*了，小姐。”

En: You don't look surprised. You were expecting this.
Zh: “你看起来一点也不吃惊。你早就预料到了。”

En: I knew there was a chance you'd get them to tell you. It's what you do. You're the police.
Zh: “我知道你们有可能会从他们嘴里问出来。你就是干这个的。你是警察。”

En: Shush. I can't hear what she's saying.
Zh: 嘘。我听不见她说什么了。

En: Let the miss speak. She's tall and thin and tired -- a twig trying so hard not to break...
Zh: 让小姐说话。她又高又瘦，还这么累——就像一根努力不被折断的树枝……

En: Why *did* you waste our time then?
Zh: “那你为什么*要*浪费我们的时间呢？”

En: Why *did* you waste my time then? If you knew I would find out.
Zh: “那你为什么*要*浪费我的时间呢？如果你知道我会查出来的话。”

En: Because of the Hardies -- I couldn't just dispense with them, they were only trying to help me. She looks at her feet. "Out of the shit I'd gotten into."
Zh: “因为哈迪兄弟——我不能不管他们，他们只是想帮我。”她低头看着双脚。“帮我从这些破事里脱身。”

En: That's not a good enough reason.
Zh: “这个理由还不够好。”

En: I know that. But the people around here -- they don't see it that way. And if I am to stay here I need to get along with them.
Zh: “我知道。但是这里的人——他们不会这么看。而且如果我要待在这里，我就需要跟他们好好相处。”

En: Is it? Something is off here...
Zh: 是吗？好像有些不太对劲……

En: Is she implying the Hardie boys are THE LAW?!
Zh: 她是在暗示哈迪兄弟是执法人员吗？！

En: A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Grey and pale violet in the morning light...
Zh: 一大片水域从海湾一直延伸到地平线。灰色和淡紫色晕染在晨光之中……

En: A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Wine dark in the evening light...
Zh: 一大片水域从海湾一直延伸到地平线。黄昏的光线一片深红……

En: A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Golden grey in the noon light...
Zh: 一大片水域从海湾一直延伸到地平线。午后的阳光洒落下金灰色的光彩……

En: What lies beyond it?
Zh: 在它之后呢？

En: The pale. The Mundi isola. The Occident. And then -- *Oranje*. The Old Old World.
Zh: 灰域。蒙迪洲。奥西登国度。然后是——*奥兰冶*。旧旧世界。

En: Never mind...
Zh: 没关系……

En: Is that why you're looking over your shoulder? You're afraid of the Moralintern?
Zh: “这就是你一直回头眺望的原因吗？你害怕国际道德伦理委员会？”

En: Is this about the people you mentioned, the ones who are *angry* at you?
Zh: “是不是跟你提到的那些人有关，那些*生你气*的人？”

En: Has this got something to do with you being... Miss Oranje '37?
Zh: “这是不是跟你当选为……37年的奥兰治小姐有关？”

En: The conference centres... the people who are angry at her. Her past.
Zh: 会议中心……那些生她气的人。她的过去。

En: She smiles, cautiously. "I wish it did. But sadly no."
Zh: 她小心翼翼地笑了。“我也希望如此。可惜并不是。”

En: She winces. "For what it's worth, I'm sorry for wasting your time."
Zh: 她退缩了。“不管怎么说，很抱歉，我浪费了你的时间。”

En: It was a mistake. She winces. "I'm sorry for wasting your time."
Zh: “这是个错误。”她退缩了。“很抱歉，我浪费了你的时间。”

En: I didn't lie. Not a lot. I've done my absolute best *not* to. I just...
Zh: “我没有撒谎。没那么多。我已经很努力*不要*这样做了。我只是……”

En: 'Tis true, the laedy hath tried to avoid falsehoods.
Zh: 是真的，这位女士尽量在避免撒谎。

En: C'mon. She lied through her teeth. And you know it.
Zh: 拜托。她明明满口谎言。而且你是知道的。

En: I know. She looks down for a moment, hiding her eyes behind her bangs. "For what it's worth, I'm sorry. For all of this, for wasting your time."
Zh: “我知道。”她低下头看了一会儿，把眼睛藏在刘海后面。“不管怎么说，我很抱歉。这一切，还有你浪费掉的时间。”

En: I am not. There is more here, miss.
Zh: “我不觉得。不止这些吧，小姐。”

En: Briefly glancing over her shoulder to the sea -- as she's done time and time again.
Zh: 短暂地回头望了一眼大海——正如她一遍又一遍做的那样。

En: I sense you're being sarcastic. Okay. The thing is -- you're on the payroll of the Moralist International... She stops to fish a new cigarette out of the pack.
Zh: “我能感觉到你在挖苦我。没事的。问题是——你在国际道德伦理委员会的名单上……”她停下来，从盒子里抽出一根新的烟。

En: It is. She nods. "They're after me. And they have friends in the Moralintern -- some *are* Moralintern. If I show up in your records, officer, they will find me. They will..."
Zh: “是的。”她点点头。“他们在追踪我。而且他们有朋友在国际道德伦理委员会——有些本身*就是*国际道德伦理委员会的人。如果我出现在你的记录里，警官，他们就会找到我。他们会……”

En: You share a database with them. You send people to their courts. She lights the cigarette.
Zh: “你和他们共享同一个数据库。你会把人送上他们的法庭。”她点燃香烟。

En: What's the RCM's involvement with the Moralintern got to do with this?
Zh: “RCM跟国际道德伦理委员会的关联跟这件事有什么关系？”

En: I don't answer to *anyone*. Mother fucking anyone, butch.
Zh: “我不会听*任何人*的话。他妈的谁都不行，假小子。”

En: I think you meant to say *bitch* there.
Zh: “我觉得你是想说*婊子*吧。”

En: I thought I did.
Zh: “我想是的。”

En: I know what I meant -- I meant to say *butch*, you have a problem with it?
Zh: “我知道自己是什么意思——我想说的就是*假小子*，你觉得有什么问题吗？”

En: She nods. "You share a database with them. You send people to their courts." She lights the cigarette.
Zh: 她点点头。“你和他们共享同一个数据库。你会把人送上他们的法庭。”她点燃香烟。

En: What the fuck WAS that!?! You don't fucking answer to FUCKING ANYONE.
Zh: 这他妈又是怎么一回事？！你他妈才不会听任何人的话。

En: Officer. Regain control of yourself. He turns to the woman: "What does our *limited cooperation* with the Moralintern have to do with this?"
Zh: “警官。控制一下自己的情绪。”他转向女子：“我们跟国际道德伦理委员会之间*有限的合作关系*跟这个有什么关系吗？”

En: Wow. Someone has their testosterone cranked up to neolithic. Let me just forcefully re-rail this talk for you, okay? Are you okay with that? Here we go...
Zh: “哇哦。看来某人睾丸素都升高到新石器时代的水平了。让我来强力地重新帮你捋一遍，好吗？你有意见吗？那就开始吧……”

En: You didn't. There's a short pause.
Zh: “你没有。”她短暂的停顿了一下。

En: Until the lieutenant loses patience: "What does our *limited cooperation* with the MI have to do with this?"
Zh: 直到警督失去了耐性：“我们跟MI之间*有限的合作关系*跟这个有什么关系吗？”

En: Is this part of why Oranje is a *bad memory* for you?
Zh: “这就是奥兰治给你留下了*不好的回忆*的部分原因吗？”

En: What happened here -- the night he died?
Zh: “这里到底发生了什么事——就是他死的那晚？”

En: Did you kill Lely?
Zh: “你杀了莱利吗？”

En: Did you kill him?
Zh: “你杀了他吗？”

En: Why did you call the cops if you're hiding?
Zh: “如果你在躲藏，为什么要打给警察？”

En: I think we're done here -- for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我觉得这里已经差不多了——暂时而已。”（总结。）

En: What did you do to have these *people* after you?
Zh: “你做了什么，让*这些人*追踪你？”

En: Let's continue with what happened after you ran downstairs. What did you do after treating the body?\n
Zh: “继续说你跑下楼之后的事吧。处理了尸体之后，你做了什么？”

En: What happened after you ran downstairs?
Zh: “你跑下楼之后发生了什么？”

En: Run, woman! Run past them and out into the street where it's dark and people move -- to the lorries at the intersection. As far as you can...
Zh: 快跑，女人！跑过他们身旁，跑向那条黑暗而又熙熙攘攘的街道——去往十字路口的货車。能跑多远就多远……

En: I already *ran*. I ran from an entire isola. There is... I can't run any further. Not with these people. This is as far as it gets.
Zh: “我已经*逃跑*了。我从一个洲跑出来了。所以……我再也跑不动了。跟这些人一起不行。我只能跑这么远了。”

En: What happens if they do?
Zh: “如果他们找到你了会怎么样？”

En: They will kill me, sir. If you file my name -- take me in for questioning -- enter me into the Moralintern mill...
Zh: “他们会杀了我，先生。如果你记录下我的名字——把我逮捕进去审讯——就等于把我送到国际道德伦理委员会的手上……”

En: Why didn't you run away from here? As a matter of fact -- why are you here now?
Zh: “你为什么不从这里逃走？事实上——你为什么现在还在这里？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: She pulls on the cigarette, looking up at the sky. Her hair ripples in the breeze as she stands there, silent.
Zh: 她抽了一口烟，抬头望向天空。她沉默地站在原地，头发在微风中飘荡。

En: What's going on? What did you do?
Zh: “出什么事了？你做了什么？”

En: Just business -- but *bad* business for some people in the Moralintern. If I show up in your records, officer, they will find me. They will...
Zh: “只是生意而已——不过对于国际道德伦理委员会的某些人来说，是很*不好*的生意。如果我出现在你的记录里，警官，他们会找到我。他们会……”

En: It's not nice -- but it's not illegal, not here in Revachol. Or even in Oranje.
Zh: “不是好事——但是不违法，在瑞瓦肖不是。甚至在奥兰治也不是。”

En: Industrial espionage. I joined a business collective with the intention of betraying them. I did my job well enough to be asked to do it again.
Zh: “工业间谍。我加入了一个有意背叛他们的商业团体。我的工作表现很好，好到他们让我再做一次。”

En: With a bigger company -- the kind you really, really don't fuck with. I took their ledgers -- two decades' worth of accounting.
Zh: “一家更大的公司——你真的，真的不应该去招惹的那种。我拿走了他们的账本——20年的会计记录。”

En: What *exactly* did you do?
Zh: “你*到底*做了什么？”

En: What *exactly* did you do?
Zh: “你*到底*做了什么？”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: She takes a drag, then glances over her shoulder.
Zh: 她抽了一口烟，然后回头望了一眼。

En: I'm sure there are people who have done much worse than that.
Zh: “我肯定有人做过比这更糟糕的事情。”

En: That's a lot of shit you've gotten yourself into.
Zh: “你还真是给自己惹了不小的麻烦啊。”

En: You're right. This is bad. You've destroyed what people have built.
Zh: “你说得对。这真的挺糟糕的。你毁掉了别人建造的东西。”

En: Looskap -- these people engineer financial disasters in second world countries. The conglomerate also includes The Bank of Consecration, Eeirberg and the popular Pappa Lollo line of dairy products.
Zh: 洛斯凯普——这些人精心策划了第二世界国家的金融灾难。这个大集团还包括祝圣银行，埃尔博格，还有广受欢迎的洛洛爸爸乳制品公司。

En: Who did you do this to? And on whose behalf?
Zh: “你的目标是谁？代表的又是谁？”

En: I need the names of the companies involved. And who hired you. He taps on his notebook.
Zh: 他拍了拍笔记本。“我需要相关公司的名字。还有你雇主的名字。”

En: The job was Loosduin County Savings Bank -- they sound small but they're part of the Looskap conglomerate. That was the second job. The first was some printer company. You wouldn't know them.
Zh: “我为洛斯杜林国家储蓄银行工作——听起来规模很小，不过他们也是洛斯凯普企业集团的一部分。那是我的第二份工作。第一份是在一家打印机公司。你应该不知道。”

En: As to who hired me for the job -- I don't know. But *they're* after me too. Along with Looskap, and their friends in the MI. She breathes out, heavily. "Once you're done in the competitive intelligence circuit, you don't have allies. You're radioactive."
Zh: “至于是谁雇佣的我——我不知道。不过*他们*也在追踪我。跟洛斯凯普集团，还有他们在MI的朋友一起。”她重重地吐出一口气。“一旦你进入竞争情报圈，你就没有盟友了。你会变得像核辐射。”

En: Pappa Lollo!
Zh: “洛洛爸爸！”

En: Yeah. Pappa fucking Lollo wants to kill me. She smiles.
Zh: “是啊。该死的洛洛爸爸想杀了我。”她笑了。

En: Serves them right. When I am king, Pappa Lollo will be first against the wall.
Zh: “他们罪有应得。等我当上国王的时候，洛洛爸爸会成为第一个倒霉蛋。”

En: Serves them right. When I am king, this Savings Bank will be first against the wall.
Zh: “他们罪有应得。等我当上国王的时候，这家储蓄银行会成为第一个倒霉蛋。”

En: Sure. I'm not a war criminal -- but it was bad. People lost their jobs. Good people too, not just C-Suite... She shakes her head at the thought.
Zh: “当然。我不是战争罪犯——不过也挺糟糕的。很多人丢了工作。好人也是，不仅仅只有最高管理层……”一想到这里，她摇了摇头。

En: It is. Many people lost their jobs. Not just C-Suite. Ordinary people. What I did to get to accounting... She shakes her head at the thought.
Zh: “是的。很多人丢了工作。不仅仅是最高管理层。还有普通人。为了拿到会计账目，我做的那一切……”一想到这里，她摇了摇头。

En: Pappa Lollo is not real. He's a picture on a milk carton. Real people lost their jobs. Not C-Suite either -- good people. What I did to get to accounting... She shakes her head at the thought, then swallows.
Zh: “洛洛爸爸不是真人。他只是牛奶盒上的一张图片。活生生的人丢了工作。不仅仅是最高管理层——还有好人。为了拿到会计账目，我做的那一切……”一想到这里，她摇了摇头。

En: This was not a joke. Real people lost their jobs. Not just in the C-Suite either -- good people. What I did to get to accounting... She shakes her head at the thought.
Zh: “这可不是个笑话。活生生的人丢了工作。不仅仅是最高管理层——还有好人。为了拿到会计账目，我做的那一切……”一想到这里，她摇了摇头。

En: I am. I am a destroyer. People lost their jobs -- good people. Not just C-Suite assholes. And the way I got into accounting... She shakes her head at the thought.
Zh: “是的。我是个毁灭者。很多人丢了工作。好人也是，不仅仅是那些该死的最高管理层。还有我拿到会计账目的手段……”一想到这里，她摇了摇头。

En: She says it quickly. Like she's said it a hundred times -- to herself? To get used to the idea?
Zh: 她的语速很快。好像她已经说过了上百次——对她自己说的吗？为了能习惯这个想法？

En: Out of guilt.
Zh: 因为内疚。

En: That's bad. How do you live with yourself?
Zh: “真是挺糟糕的。那你是怎么活下去的？”

En: I don't. She shrugs. "How do *you* live with yourself?"
Zh: “我没有。”她耸耸肩。“那*你*又是怎么活下去的？”

En: I do drugs.
Zh: “我嗑药。”

En: I drink.
Zh: “我喝酒。”

En: I'm a cop. What I do is *right*.
Zh: “我是个警察。我做的事都是*对的*。”

En: By not remembering a single god damn thing.
Zh: “因为我他妈的一件该死的事都记不住。”

En: That would do the trick, yes. She has almost devoured the cigarette she lit. She looks at it sadly.
Zh: “那样确实能行，没错。”她几乎要把点燃的香烟吞掉了。她悲伤地望着它。

En: By bargaining then... She takes a drag. "I do that too. It's not all that effective."
Zh: “那就是靠讨价还价了……”她抽了一口烟。“我也会这么做。并不是很有效果。”

En: Drugs are good, she looks to the sea. "I also do those. I try to *bargain* with myself too."
Zh: “毒品很好，”她望向大海。“我也嗑药。我也试过跟我自己*讨价还价*。”

En: I just try to smoke as much as possible.
Zh: “我就是尽可能地抽烟。”

En: That doesn't really work, does it? She has almost devoured the cigarette she lit. She looks at it sadly. "Not without a drink to wash it down..."
Zh: “其实并没有什么用，不是吗？”她几乎要把点燃的香烟吞掉了。她悲伤地望着它。“没有酒把它冲下去可不行……”

En: I do that too, yes. It's unbelievably effective. Especially when combined with cigarettes. And drugs.
Zh: “没错，我也是。效果真是出奇的好。特别是跟烟组合在一起的时候。还有毒品。”

En: Around her -- a drop in atmospheric pressure. Not long from now it will get dark. And the air will start moving faster, circling the box she stands on...
Zh: 在她周围——气压下降了。不久之后，天就会变黑。空气会流动地更快，在她脚下的箱子周围盘旋……

En: We were there. She points to the window -- the silhouette of the bed is visible. "Together. In bed, I mean."
Zh: “我们曾经在那里待过。”她指着窗户——床的轮廓依稀可见。“在一起。我是说在床上。”

En: I turned back to him. She breathes out. A moment's silence. "His eyes were looking through me and his mouth was open, dumb... I could see... I could..."
Zh: “我转身面向他。”她呼出一口气，沉默了一阵子。“他的眼睛看穿了我，嘴巴张开，沉默无语……我可以看见……我可以……”

En: Tell me *exactly* what happened.
Zh: “告诉我*到底*发生了什么。”

En: Okay... She takes a deep breath. "He was in a kneeling position, he had just entered me. I was on my back, looking at him. I heard the window behind me shatter and I turned to look -- there was a hole in the glass..."
Zh: “好吧……”她深吸一口气。“他正半跪着，刚刚进入我的身体。我躺在床上，看着他。我听见身后的窗户碎裂的声音，转回头一看——玻璃上有个洞……”

En: I waited for the second shot to come -- for me. I thought there would be one. It never came.
Zh: 长时间的停顿。她就这么站在那里，胳膊垂放在身体两侧。然后她继续说到：“我等着第二枪的到来——冲我来的。我觉得它会来。但是没有。”

En: I knew he was dead. Before he fell down on top of me.
Zh: “我知道他死了。在他倒在我身上之前。”

En: Your cigarette, miss.
Zh: “你的烟，小姐。”

En: I'm sorry this happened to you.
Zh: “很抱歉你遇到了这种事。”

En: Are you sure you're not making this up?
Zh: “你确定这不是你编的吗？”

En: (Shake your head.) "That's a motherfucker."
Zh: （摇摇头。）“真是个混账。”

En: A great pain moves through her, a dark and indefinite wave. She continues in spite of it.
Zh: 一阵巨大的痛苦穿透她的身体，宛如一波漆黑又模糊的浪潮。尽管如此，她还在继续。

En: Sylvie was tending the bar. She looks down. "A lot of people were there. The Hardies were at the table in front of the stage, I think the Union box was full..."
Zh: “塞尔维在打理酒吧。”她低下头。“很多人都在那里。哈迪兄弟在舞台前的桌子旁边，我觉得工会包厢也是满的……”

En: Yeah, it was a real *motherfucker*. She immediately lights another one.
Zh: “是啊，是个货真价实的*混账*。”她随即又点燃了一支烟。

En: Yeah, it was a real *motherfucker*... ouch... She looks down. Her cigarette has singed her fingers.
Zh: “是啊，是个货真价实的*混账*……很疼……”她低下头，香烟烫伤了她的手指。

En: Then what happened?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: He was heavy. I pushed him off and he fell to the floor -- there. She points through the window. "He only had his boots on. I bit the pillow... not to scream, then ran downstairs..." There's a long pause. She just stands there, her arms at her sides.
Zh: “他很重。我把他推下去，他掉在了地板上——就在那里。”她指向窗户。“他只穿了靴子。我咬紧枕头……为了不让自己尖叫出声，然后跑下楼……”

En: She's forgotten about her cigarette. The butt has burned right down to her fingernails...
Zh: 她忘了手中的烟。烟蒂烧到了她的手指甲……

En: (Just nod.) "We're going to need more details."
Zh: （只是点头。）“我们需要更多细节。”

En: (Just nod.) "There are a few questions I have to ask for my report."
Zh: （只是点头）“为了报告，我还需要问一些问题。”

En: So am I. She immediately proceeds to light another one.
Zh: “我也是。”她随即又点燃了一支烟。

En: So am I. She looks down. Her cigarette has singed her fingers.
Zh: “我也是。”她低下头，香烟烫伤了她的手指。

En: I'm not lying to you... She immediately lights another one.
Zh: “我没有对你撒谎……”她随即又点燃了一支烟。

En: I'm not lying to you... She looks down, wincing. Her cigarette has singed her fingers.
Zh: “我没有对你撒谎……”她低下头，有些畏缩。香烟烫伤了她的手指。

En: Oh... She looks at it and quickly tosses the butt aside.
Zh: “哦……”她看着它，赶忙把烟蒂扔到了一旁。

En: She throws it away and immediately proceeds to light another one.
Zh: 她扔掉烟蒂，随即又点燃了一支烟。

En: Sure. She immediately lights another one.
Zh: “当然。”她随即又点燃了一支烟。

En: She looks down with a wince. Her cigarette has singed her fingers.
Zh: 她低下头，有些畏缩。香烟烫伤了她的手指。

En: What time was this? When did it happen -- it would help us if you could be as precise as possible.
Zh: “什么时候的事？事情是什么时候发生的——如果你能尽可能精确的话，对我们会很有帮助。”

En: 11.30 to 12.15. I don't know the exact time. Around midnight.
Zh: “晚上11:30到00:15。具体时间我不知道，大概在午夜吧。”

En: That's okay. He makes a note. "Were you inebriated?"
Zh: “没事。”他记下笔记。“你喝醉了吗？”

En: Not as much as usual. He'd done a line. Plus other things. I was drinking.
Zh: “没有平常那么多。他抽了点可卡因。还有别的东西。我在喝酒。”

En: What did you do then?
Zh: “然后你做了什么？”

En: Nothing. I was trapped. I was stuck in my room -- downstairs. I got some clothes on and crawled back up, drew the blinds. Blood was coming from his mouth -- not a lot. Just a little.
Zh: “没什么。我被陷入困境了。我被困在自己的房间里——在楼下。我穿上衣服，爬起来，拉上了百叶窗。血从他的嘴里流了出来——不是很多。只有一点。”

En: Could the people after you have killed him?
Zh: “会不会是追踪你的人杀了他？”

En: He was still on the floor. Slouched. I couldn't be there with him anymore. So I ran down -- and out of my room. Into the hallway. Down the stairs. I knew there would be people there.
Zh: “他还在地板上。耷拉着。我没办法跟他继续待在一起了。所以我跑下去——离开我的房间。穿过走廊。跑下楼梯。我知道那里会有人。”

En: What time was this?
Zh: “这是什么时候的事？”

En: Were you inebriated?
Zh: “你喝醉了吗？”

En: You *may* sometimes be bad at reading her, but this time, truly -- she's telling you how it really went down.
Zh: 有时候，您*可能*不善于读懂她，不过这一次，说真的——她在告诉您事情的真相。

En: Ruby was there too. They were having such a good time... She pauses. "I sat down and they all welcomed me. I didn't even have to say anything, Ruby *knew* something was wrong."
Zh: “露比也在那里。他们玩的真的很开心……”她停顿了一下。“我坐下来，他们全都很欢迎我。我甚至都不用开口，露比就*知道*出事了。”

En: What are you doing?
Zh: 你在做什么？

En: Interesting. Why did this Ruby go through so much trouble -- to hide something someone *else* did? Look into this later!
Zh: 有意思。为什么这个露比会费这么大的功夫——去隐藏*别人*做的事呢？之后要好好调查一下！

En: Coming up with a theory. She said Ruby *knew* something was wrong before she said anything. How come? It was loud downstairs, she couldn't have heard the shot...
Zh: 我有个理论。她说在她开口之前，露比就*知道*出事了。怎么会呢？楼下那么嘈杂，她不可能听得到枪声……

En: It is ominous -- you already coming up with theories that put the blame on someone other than Klaasje.
Zh: 这是个不祥的预兆——你已经想出一套责怪其他人而不是卡拉洁的理论了。

En: Before we continue -- who is Ruby?
Zh: “在我们继续之前——露比是谁？”

En: *Now* let's go on. What happened then?
Zh: “*现在*我们继续吧。之后发生了什么？”

En: Okay, let's go on. What then?
Zh: “好吧，我们继续。之后呢？”

En: Ruby. You know. The leader.
Zh: “露比。你知道的。那个头目。”

En: The leader? Of what?
Zh: “头目？什么头目？”

En: The Hardie boys, she says as if it's self-evident.
Zh: “哈迪兄弟，”她说的好像这是一件明摆着的事实。

En: This Ruby, in her phrasing, is entrusted with great power. She trusts her. So do the others.
Zh: 这个露比，在她的说法里，应该是被赋予了巨大的权利。她相信她。其他人也是。

En: Well. Nominally yes. Ruby's the one they go to when things happen. Like, things they need taken care of. She's the organizer.
Zh: “嗯。名义上是的。遇到事情的时候，他们会去找露比。比如说，那些需要他们去处理的事。她是组织者。”

En: Did you *party* with Ruby too?
Zh: “那你也跟露比一起*派对*过吗？”

En: Better not to.
Zh: 最好不要。

En: Does she also *party* with Ruby, however?
Zh: 可是，她也要跟露比一起*派对*吗？

En: Leader? Of what?
Zh: “头目？什么头目？”

En: I thought Hardie was the leader of the Hardie boys.
Zh: “我以为哈迪兄弟的头目是哈迪。”

En: No... She smiles nervously. The beads of her teeth shine.
Zh: “没有……”她紧张地笑了，牙珠闪闪发亮。

En: What are you trying to be -- tactful? She wouldn't mind. Go on then...
Zh: 你这是想干什么——装圆滑吗？她不介意的。继续吧……

En: Still -- there is something there. She won't tell you now.
Zh: 不过——还有些什么。她现在还不会告诉你。

En: Well, Ruby said let's talk upstairs. I showed her the room... I've known these people since December, they know my situation. That I can't leave a paper trail. Ruby was the first one I told...
Zh: “嗯，露比说我们楼上聊。我把房间指给她看……我12月份的时候就认识这些人了，他们知道我的处境——我不能留下书面记录。我第一个告诉的人就是露比……”

En: She said she'd take care of this. It's what she does, you know. Take care of things. I helped her get the body to the bathroom -- we used a belt to pull him up under the shower. To keep him upright.
Zh: “她说交给她处理。你知道的，她就是专门干这个的。处理各种事情。我帮她把尸体搬到浴室——我们用一条皮带把他拉到了花洒下面。为了把他直起来。”

En: To produce lividity matching a hanging?
Zh: “为了制造出跟绞刑一样的铁青色？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: This is concurrent with your doubts -- at the autopsy -- about the colouring being...
Zh: 这跟你的怀疑是一致的——在尸检的时候——关于那个颜色的问题……

En: You were right. He *did* enjoy the moment of his death.
Zh: 你是对的。他*确实*很享受死亡的那一刻。

En: I knew he enjoyed the moment he died. I could see it in him.
Zh: “我知道他很享受死亡的那一刻。我能从他身上看出来。”

En: So my feeling was right earlier. He *was* enjoying the moment of his death.
Zh: “所以我之前的感觉是对的。他*当时*是很享受死亡的那一刻。”

En: The occult vision was right. He was enjoying the moment of his death.
Zh: “那个神秘的幻象是对的。他在享受死亡的那一刻。”

En: (Better not to mention it.) "Then what happened?"
Zh: （最好还是别提了。）“之后发生了什么？”

En: How?
Zh: “怎么说？”

En: That's the first thing that went through my head, when I heard the glass break.
Zh: “当我听到玻璃碎裂的时候，这是我脑子里想到的第一件事。”

En: You asked her before if he enjoyed it! She avoided it. How did I not discern it was a lie?
Zh: 您之前问过她，他是不是很享受！她回避了这个问题。吾辈怎么没看出那是个谎言呢？

En: You *know* how.
Zh: 你*知道*是怎么回事。

En: Yes, but... How did you know that?
Zh: “是的，不过……你是怎么知道的？”

En: He just had a hunch. Detectives have those sometimes.
Zh: “这是他的一种直觉。有时候警探是会这样的。”

En: Another hit. The lieutenant looks at you -- in acknowledgement.
Zh: 又命中了。警督看着你——表示确认。

En: A direct hit. The lieutenant looks at you -- in acknowledgement."
Zh: 直接命中。警督看着你——表示确认：

En: Just a hunch -- then what?
Zh: “只是一种直觉——然后呢？”

En: I could see it in his eyes.
Zh: “我能从他的眼睛里看出来。”

En: She blinks. Everything is silent for a second.
Zh: 她眨眨眼。周围的一切都沉默了一秒钟。

En: I knew it -- the colouring in his lower limbs looked faint.
Zh: “我就知道——他下体的颜色看起来很模糊。”

En: Better not to remark.
Zh: 最好不要评论。

En: About half an hour had passed since the moment of death?
Zh: “他死之后的大概半个小时？”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: You were late. The marks would be faint by that time. He makes a small pointing motion with his pen -- it's meant for you.
Zh: “你晚了。那时候痕迹会变得模糊。”他用笔做了一个小小的指向动作——是给你看的。

En: In honour of another *precise* forensic observation.
Zh: 为了庆祝又一个*精准*的法医观察。

En: You have it in you to be a champion. Keep your head in the game now...
Zh: 你有成为冠军的资质。现在全身心投入到游戏中去吧……

En: In honour of that *precise* forensic remark, officer. A real hit.
Zh: 为了庆祝又一个*精准*的法医评论，警官。一次真正的成功。

En: You were tampering with the body.
Zh: “你是在破坏尸体。”

En: Yes. Ruby said the blood in him... would look like a hanging.
Zh: “是的。露比说他身体里的血液……会看起来像是绞死的。”

En: She's silent. Just looks at her cigarette.
Zh: 她沉默不语，只是看着手中的香烟。

En: This tactful approach will rob you the glory, but hey, all I care about is angles and timetables. The loss is yours.
Zh: 这种圆滑的方式会抢走你的荣耀，不过，我在乎的只有角度和时间表。损失是你自己的。

En: We *completely* missed the tampering. Looks like you got there in time. What was this -- twenty minutes after death?
Zh: “我们*完全*漏掉破坏的事了。看来你及时赶到了。是什么时候做的——死后20分钟吗？”

En: About twenty, yes. Ruby explained it would make the blood... you know what it does. She looks at the ground. Then raises her light brown eyes to meet yours.
Zh: “差不多是20分钟，没错。露比解释说这样血液就会……你应该知道是怎么回事。”她看向地面，然后抬起头，浅棕色的眼睛跟你的交遇。

En: Then what did you do?
Zh: “然后你做了什么？”

En: That's enough. I had another question.
Zh: “差不多够了。我还有别的问题。”

En: She blinks, without averting her gaze.
Zh: 她眨眨眼，没有转移视线。

En: Ruby went outside to talk to Titus and the boys. I was just... looking at Lely. In the bathroom. I had to put his clothes back on. His armour too. It was tough, but I've seen him take it off and put it on many times.
Zh: “露比出去跟提图斯和哈迪兄弟说话去了。我只是……看着莱利。在浴室里。我必须把他的衣服穿回去。还有他的盔甲。其实挺困难的，不过我已经看他穿脱过很多次了。”

En: It took Ruby maybe half an hour to come back with Titus. I'd gotten him ready by then, they carried him out. I knew what they were going to do -- make it look like a hanging. Ruby said they would.
Zh: “大概过了半个小时，露比和提图斯一起回来了。那时候我已经把他收拾好了，他们把他搬了出去。我知道他们要做什么——让他看起来像是被绞死的。露比说他们会处理好的。”

En: Wait-- the Hardie boys said she'd visited them last week. Glen and...
Zh: 等等——哈迪兄弟说她上周去见过他们。格伦还有……

En: What? She gathers the last vestiges of her strength. "Why would I put myself through this... insanity? Get myself cornered like this?" There's a silence.
Zh: “什么？”她积蓄起残存的一丝力气。“我为什么要让自己经受这种……疯狂的事？让自己陷入这样的困境？”她沉默了。

En: I'm sad to hear that. They must have said it in some fit of frustration, or under pressure. They couldn't have meant it. I've talked to them after it happened -- *no one* has implicated me.
Zh: “听到这个我很难过。他们肯定会说是处于挫败，或者压力之下吧。他们不是认真的。事情发生之后我跟他们聊过——*没有人*牵扯到我。”

En: Desires drive people *loco*...
Zh: “欲望会让人*发疯*……”

En: Downstairs people have this *crazy* idea that you killed him.
Zh: “楼下那群人有一个很*疯狂*的想法，觉得是你杀了他。”

En: She *has* the training for it. You've known it since she first greeted you. Here on this roof.
Zh: 她*肯定*接受过专门训练。从她第一次跟你打招呼的时候，你就知道了。就在这个屋顶上。

En: All kinds of *crazy* things happen when drugs are involved... sweet, sweet drugs.
Zh: 每当毒品卷入其中的时候，总会发生各种各样*疯狂*的事情……甜美又甜蜜的毒品啊。

En: He was a serviceman -- he must have had a gun. Somewhere, lying around. Close to her hand...
Zh: 他是一个军人——他肯定有一支枪。就在附近的什么地方。离她的手很近……

En: *Now* you guys suddenly have theories pouring out? When they're obviously just stabs in the dark...
Zh: *现在*你们这群家伙突然冒出很多理论了？当他们显然只是在黑暗中被刺伤的时候……

En: Drugs were an *integral* part of your relationship. Perhaps they contributed to its end?
Zh: “毒品是你们的关系中*不可或缺*的一部分。也许它们也促成了这个结果？”

En: What does that mean?
Zh: “这话是什么意思？”

En: Do drugs make you aggressive?
Zh: “毒品会让你变得很有攻击性吗？”

En: You know what I mean. Do drugs make you aggressive?
Zh: “你知道我是什么意思。毒品会让你变得很有攻击性吗？”

En: I thought officers in Revachol would be more enlightened about these things. She looks you in the eye. "Especially since you've admitted to taking drugs yourself."
Zh: “我觉得瑞瓦肖的警官对这种事情会更开明一些。”她看着你的眼睛。“特别是你自己承认吸毒的时候。”

En: What? Fucking themselves over for no reason? This is not what happened, officer.
Zh: “什么？毫无理由地给他们惹下一大堆麻烦？事情不是这样的，警官。”

En: He must have had a weapon nearby. Did you use that?
Zh: “他肯定有一支武器放在附近。你是用了那个吗？”

En: He was a serviceman. He must have had a gun lying around. Close to her hand -- a  military weapon using *jacketed ammunition*.
Zh: 他是一个军人——他肯定在附近放了一支武器。离她的手很近——一支使用*被甲弹*的军事武器。

En: The bullet in his head. It was jacketed. Military-grade. Who else here has a *military* rifle?
Zh: “他脑袋里那颗子弹。是一颗被甲弹。军事级的。这里谁还会这种*军事*步枪？”

En: You've lied to me about this bullet before. When I said he was *shot*? You said you're *confused*.
Zh: “你之前就在子弹的事情上跟我撒过谎。在我说他*中枪*的时候？你说你很*困惑*。”

En: I've said I'm sorry. What more do you want me to say? I did my best not to lie. It didn't always work.
Zh: “我说过了，我很抱歉。你还想让我说什么？我已经尽力不撒谎了。因为这样总是不管用。”

En: No. I specifically asked him not to carry firearms when he was with me. He only had his stupid armour...
Zh: “不。我明确跟他说过，跟我在一起的时候不要把武器带在身上。他只穿了那套愚蠢的盔甲……”

En: I don't know. His friends have rifles, maybe those psychos did it? Coalition military have rifles. I'm not a munitions expert -- and I did not shoot him.
Zh: “我不知道。他的朋友有步枪，也许是那群疯子做的？联盟军有步枪。我不是军火专家——而且我也没朝他开枪。”

En: She might have been a tad disingenuous when she avoided talking about the bullet in his head *before...*
Zh: *之前*她避而不谈脑子里那颗子弹的时候，可能有一点虚伪……

En: Better not start on this.
Zh: 最好先别说这个。

En: She simply shakes her head.
Zh: 她只是摇摇头。

En: The wind picks up. "He wouldn't have died if it weren't for me -- I know that. But I would *never* hurt him."
Zh: 起风了。“如果不是因为我，他也不会死——这一点我知道。但是我*永远不会*伤害他的。”

En: You *have* the training for it. I can see it in your posture, your shoulders...
Zh: “你*曾经*接受过训练。我能从你的姿势，还有你的肩膀看出来……”

En: The work I did -- they don't just send you in with no training. Corporate security is a thing. But I'm not a trained Sam Bo fighter, I've never needed it outside the gym.
Zh: “我的工作——他们不可能不经过训练就把你派出去的。企业安全是一回事。但我也不是训练过的萨拳拳手，在健身房之外的地方，我根本用不着它。”

En: She has the training for it. Two, maybe three years of self defence? Her body is hard and lean underneath that jump suit...
Zh: 她曾经接受过训练。两年，或者是三年的自我防身术？在那身连体裤下面，是一副结实又精瘦的身体……

En: Rage? Who knows the *dark currents* where lovers go?
Zh: 愤怒？谁知道恋人们会前往怎样的*暗流*？

En: She's fought outside that *gym* of hers. That much is clear. Moreover -- she would like to do it now: hit you. It's in her shoulders...
Zh: 她在*健身房*以外的地方打过架。这一点很明显。而且——她现在就想这么做：打你。她的肩膀已经出卖了她……

En: Do you want to hit me right now?
Zh: “你现在就想打我吗？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Because I'm under stress. Her mouth is tense, the muscles in her feet tighten.
Zh: “因为我现在很有压力。”她的嘴唇紧闭，脚上的肌肉也紧绷起来。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Because life is hell, officer, she finds refuge in her cigarette. It's thin, but it's something.
Zh: “因为生活就像地狱一样，警官，”她在香烟里寻找慰藉。虽然很微弱，不过总算是有的。

En: I thought Revachol would be more enlightened about this. She looks you in the eye. "Especially since you've taken them yourself."
Zh: “我觉得瑞瓦肖在这方面会更开明一些。”她看着你的眼睛。“特别是你自己也会吸毒。”

En: Of course. There's a pause. "I'm sorry. I withdraw my statement. I'm under stress here."
Zh: “当然。”她停顿了一下。“很抱歉。我收回我的话。我现在压力有点大。”

En: The lieutenant gives you a sideways glance.
Zh: 警督斜了你一眼。

En: It's not one of respect. You may want to add a clever *retort* to this one.
Zh: 这不是尊重。也许你想在这里加上一句聪明的*反驳*。

En: I thought you Revachol people would be more enlightened on this... She pauses. "I'm sorry, officer. I'm under some stress here."
Zh: “我觉得你们瑞瓦肖人在这方面会更开明一些……”她停了一下。“很抱歉，警官。我现在压力有点大。”

En: Clever retort.
Zh: “很聪明的反驳。”

En: I do drugs to keep working, cleaning up this *shit* you've created here.
Zh: “我吸毒是为了能继续工作，清理你留下的这堆*烂摊子*。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Me and my drugs are none of your business.
Zh: “我和毒品的事跟你无关。”

En: She looks at you, confused.
Zh: 她看着你，很是困惑。

En: The lieutenant shares her confusion. Respond with something professional, now...
Zh: 警督跟她一样困惑。想点更专业一些的回应，现在……

En: I'm sorry. There's a pause. "I withdraw what I said -- I didn't mean to judge anyone."
Zh: “很抱歉。”她停顿了一下。“我收回自己的话——我并不像评判任何人。”

En: She takes a healthy -- nay, *giant* healthy -- drag of her menthol cigarette, waiting for what's next to come.
Zh: 她抽了一口健康的——不，*非常*健康的——薄荷香烟，等待着接下来要发生的事。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Her shoulders do not shake, despite the strength it takes to keep herself from crying.
Zh: 她的肩膀没有颤抖，尽管她用了很大的力气才让自己不哭出来。

En: Okay. I'd like you to answer some other questions, miss. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。我希望你再回答一些问题，小姐。”（总结。）

En: Like what? She waits, her light brown eyes wandering... over the floor... over your face...
Zh: “比如什么？”她等待着，浅棕色的眼睛四处游移……掠过地板……飘过你的脸……

En: Look who's waking up from a thousand years of sleep!
Zh: 看看是谁从一千年的梦境之中醒过来了！

En: She pinches the filter of her cigarette, hard. Then looks at you.
Zh: 她重重地拧动着香烟的过滤嘴。然后看着你。

En: We can't go after Looskap. Not yet. There are other, saner leads.
Zh: “我们不能追查洛斯凯普。暂时不行。还有其他的，更清晰一些的线索。

En: And?
Zh: “还有呢？”

En: I thought they'd found me. They've killed him to punish me.... All last week I've tried not to talk to anyone, or be seen with anyone... She looks at her cigarette.
Zh: “我以为他们发现我了。他们杀了他来惩罚我……过去整整一周，我尽量不去跟任何人说话，或者被看见跟别人一起……”

En: So they wouldn't be hurt. I've come to understand, however -- this is paranoia. What happened didn't have anything to do with me.
Zh: 她看着手中的香烟。“这样他们就不会受伤。不过，我现在明白了——这是一种偏执的妄想。发生的事情跟我没有任何关系。”

En: But maybe it did?
Zh: “也许有呢？”

En: It probably didn't.
Zh: “可能没有。”

En: Either way -- something to consider.
Zh: “不管怎么说——都要考虑一下。”

En: I just don't know... She shakes her head. "I don't know anything."
Zh: “我真的不知道……”她摇摇头。“我什么也不知道。”

En: Or... I don't know anything anymore. All I do is wait here. She looks at the table.
Zh: “或者……我什么都不知道了。我唯一能做的就是在这里等着。”她看着桌子。

En: If there's one thing I know it's that you'll get *nothing* from Looskap. They don't fuck things like this up.
Zh: “如果说还有一件事我能确定，那就是你从洛斯凯普那里什么也*得不到*。他们可不会把事情搞成这个样子。”

En: I don't ask you to, lieutenant. If there's one thing I know it's that you'll get *nothing* from there.
Zh: “我没有要求你这么做，警督。如果说还有一件事我能确定，那就是你从洛斯凯普那里什么也*得不到*。”

En: *If they want to kill me, they'll kill me. If they want to toy with me they will*. She's come to this conclusion weeks ago.
Zh: *如果他们想杀我，他们就会杀了我。如果他们想玩弄我，那就会玩弄我*。几周之前，她得出了这个结论。

En: Because I'm an *idiot*. The answer comes fast.
Zh: “因为我是个*白痴*。”答案来的很快。

En: Idiot? She's nothing of the sort.
Zh: 白痴？她不是那种人。

En: So you would have us believe. But you're not.
Zh: “所以你想让我们相信。但是你没有。”

En: So you would have me believe. But you're not.
Zh: “所以你想让我相信。但是你没有。”

En: I don't think so. Why did you do it?
Zh: “我不这么认为。你为什么要这么做？”

En: I'm not gonna disagree with you.
Zh: “我不会反驳你的意见。”

En: You have to understand -- the people around here... No one was making the call, and he kept rotting. And then they picked his clothes off. And that little fucker threw stones at him...
Zh: “你必须理解——这里的人……没人打电话，而他还在继续腐烂。然后他们把他的衣服扒了下来。还有那个小混账朝他扔石头……”

En: Her jaw is clenched. Her throat moves. It takes all her strength not to cave in and sob... once... just one time...
Zh: 她的下巴紧绷。喉咙颤动着。她用尽全身的力量不让自己崩溃大哭……一次……只有这一次……

En: He kept throwing stones at him for *three days*. I could hear the *thud*, *thud*... She shakes her head. "So I called you. I hope with all my heart it's not the last thing I do in Revachol."
Zh: “他朝他身上扔石头已经整整*三天*了。我能听见那个*砰*，*砰*的声音……”她摇摇头。“所以我报了警。我真心希望这不是我在瑞瓦肖做的最后一件事。”

En: Don't be *paranoid*, man. It's unbecoming of a police officer -- and it makes you sweat.
Zh: 别这么*偏执*，老兄。这样不太符合你警官的身份——而且还会让你流汗。

En: She nods, silently. She doesn't even smoke, just picks up the cold coffee and holds it in her hands.
Zh: 她默默地点点头。她甚至都没有抽烟，只是端起那杯冷咖啡，捧在手中。

En: When was the window changed? (Point to it.)
Zh: “窗户是什么时候换的？”（指向它。）

En: Last week. Angus and Titus's brother -- I think he's called Tibbs -- took care of it. She takes a drag.
Zh: “上周。安格斯和提图斯的兄弟——好像是叫提布斯——处理的。”她抽了一口烟。

En: You should have a look at that window -- reconstruct the scene. It's right here, so near...
Zh: 你应该看看那扇窗户——重构现场。它就在这里，这么近……

En: You should have another look at that window after this -- reconstruct the scene. It's right there.
Zh: 之后你应该再看看窗户——重构现场。它就在那里。

En: Last week. Angus and Titus's brother -- I think he's called Tibbs -- took care of it. She takes a drag. "I told you I'm a horrible girl. I told you things break around me."
Zh: “上周。安格斯和提图斯的兄弟——好像是叫提布斯——处理的。”她抽了一口烟。“我告诉过你，我是个可怕的女孩。我告诉过你，我周围的东西都会被破坏。”

En: We are *still* wide awake and staring into an archipelago of truths! Scandalous facts she's shared, omitting nothing, even if it casts her as a demimonde with *sexually lax* morals...
Zh: 我们*仍然*很清醒，正凝望着真实的群岛！她分享了一些可耻的事实，没有任何遗漏，虽然这让她成为了在*两性关系*上很随便的放荡女子……

En: But we *are* awake, sire! She has been forthcoming -- with sordid details women usually conceal! Most *shocking* details of the sexual kind! We are a bulwark, un-breached...
Zh: 但吾辈*是*清醒的，大人！她一直都是有问必答——对女人通常会隐瞒的那些肮脏细节也一样！主要是跟性有关的那些*耸人听闻*的细节！吾辈是一座堡垒，无法攻破……

En: Who hath told you these *lies*? She was merely confused by the esoteric arts you deploy in policework.
Zh: 谁告诉您这些是*谎言*的？她只是被您在警察工作中使用的深奥艺术给弄糊涂了。

En: Who is this *personhood* picking these seditious options? Can't you see the detective grows weary of your malcontent?!
Zh: 是哪个*人格*挑出了这些煽动性的选项？难道看不出警探先生已经对你的反抗感到厌倦了吗？！

En: We will need to take this question to the Hardie boys.
Zh: “我们需要带着这些问题去找哈迪兄弟。”

En: This Ruby -- where is this Ruby now?
Zh: “这个露比——她现在在哪里？”

En: I don't know. I haven't seen her since.
Zh: “我不知道。从那之后我就没有见过她了。”

En: Uhm... The lieutenant glances at you -- then at the door.
Zh: “嗯……”警督撇了你一眼——然后看着门的方向。

En: He's thinking -- *are* we done here?
Zh: 他在想——我们在这里*完事*了吗？

En: He's thinking -- *are* we done here, or...?
Zh: 他在想——我们在这里*完事*了吗，还是……？

En: ...or should we arrest her? She's a flight risk and she lied to you -- she should be taken into custody.
Zh: ……或者我们应该逮捕她？她有潜逃的风险，而且还对你撒谎——她应该被拘留起来。

En: She nods silently, then picks up the cold coffee and holds it in her hands.
Zh: 她默默地点点头，然后端起那杯冷咖啡，捧在手中。

En: Okay. She puts her coffee mug on the table.
Zh: “好吧。”她把咖啡杯放在桌子上。

En: I don't... She squints. "How did you know that?"
Zh: “我没有……”她眯起眼睛。“你是怎么知道的？”

En: You've been breached, bulwark. You've been breached, like, a thousand times now!
Zh: 你被攻破了，堡垒。你被攻破了，怎么讲，到现在已经一千次了吧！

En: Get a hold of yourself just once, will you -- try and look past the *sexually* lax morals and do some police work.
Zh: 控制住自己，哪怕只有这一次，行吗——试着忽视*两性关系*上的放荡行为，做好你的警察工作。

En: She was *confused* when I asked about the bullet in his head. That was a lie.
Zh: 当我问起他脑子里那颗子弹的时候，她表现的很*困惑*。那是个谎言。

En: I remember doubting she made the call she said she made. To the police.
Zh: 她说那通电话是她打的，我记得曾经怀疑过这一点。就是打给警察的那通。

En: When I said the deceased *enjoyed* his moment of death, she knew it was true. But she said nothing.
Zh: 我说死者很*享受*死亡那一刻的时候，她知道这是事实。但她什么也没说。

En: Just a proposition: *Could* the lurid explicitness conceal something less sensational, but more... *illegal*?
Zh: 这只是一个主张：这么骇人的直白下面*是不是*隐瞒了某种不那么感性，但却更……*违法*的东西？

En: I got nothing. You're right, wise council. It is silly of me to imply she's lied.
Zh: 我什么也没明白。你是对的，聪明的顾问。暗示她在撒谎是我太傻了。

En: What was that about the semen sample? She didn't like that topic...
Zh: 精液样本是怎么回事？她不喜欢那个话题……

En: Seditious talk! That was not even Our Wise Council, it was this shithead! This one. The next one.
Zh: 煽动性言论！它根本就不是我们聪明的顾问，它是白痴！这个是。下一个也是。

En: Who, me? The lady is saintly, oh she sucks on the cigarette most elegantly. Stop staring, you look mentally handicapped.
Zh: 谁，我吗？这位女士是个圣人，她抽烟的样子是那么地高雅。别死盯着了，你看起来就像个智障。

En: There iseth no doubt! The laedy could not bear the sight of her lover splayed and disfigured. A horrible story.
Zh: 毋庸置疑，毫无疑问！这位女士不忍看到自己的爱人四仰八叉，容颜尽损。多可怕的故事啊。

En: Try not to sweat so profusely in her presence too. You'll blow your chances.
Zh: 她在场的时候，尽量不要这副大汗淋漓的样子。机会会从你身边溜走的。

En: *Laedyfolk* do not know of such things -- ballistics and weapons of war. She is too young and frail.
Zh: 这位*淑女*不懂这些——弹道学和战争武器什么的。她这么年轻，而且这么纤弱。

En: You should stop staring at her and say something. This is costing you *credibility and power* in her eyes.
Zh: 别再盯着她了，说点什么吧。在她的眼里，这会损伤你的*信誉和权威*。

En: Stop staring at her eyes and say something pertinent to the case! This is making you look like an *amateur*.
Zh: 别再盯着她了，说点什么跟案子有关的事吧！这样会让您看起来想个*外行*的。

En: Gosh, she's so young, isn't she? She would look spectacular in a *starring role*!
Zh: 天啦，她真年轻，不是吗？如果让她出演*主角*，看起来一定会光彩照人！

En: Be careful not to look too long into her soon-to-be-famous face, lest it make you look like an indecisive suitor.
Zh: 小心，不要长久地看着她那张*很快就会爆红*的脸，免得把自己弄得像个举棋不定的求婚者。

En: Stop staring and say something dramatic and professional!
Zh: 别再盯着她了，说点激动人心又专业的话吧！

En: Your paranoia is unflattering. She has been nothing but forthcoming and you're staring mutely into her face like a donkey!
Zh: 您的偏执并不吸引人。她一直都是有问必答，而您却像头蠢驴一言不发地盯着她的脸！

En: Take it easy. Don't overcompensate with this *course correction*. Ask questions first...
Zh: 放松一点。不要过度补偿这次的*航向修正*。先问问题……

En: You know -- I think you *didn't* make that call to the station.
Zh: “你知道吗——我觉得你*没有*打电话给警局。”

En: Your real name isn't Klaasje Amandou. You wouldn't give me your real name if you're on the run.
Zh: “你的真名不是卡拉洁·阿曼多。如果你在逃跑的话，你不会把真名说出来的。”

En: Your real name isn't Klaasje Amandou.
Zh: “你的真名不是卡拉洁·阿曼多。”

En: What if I told you you're under arrest, Katarzine Alasije?
Zh: “如果我告诉你，你被逮捕了呢，卡特琳·艾拉洁？”

En: What if I told you you're under arrest, Klaasje?
Zh: “如果我告诉你，你被逮捕了呢，卡拉洁？”

En: What if I told you you're under arrest, Miss Oranje Disco Dancer?
Zh: “如果我告诉你，你被逮捕了呢，奥兰治迪斯科舞者小姐？”

En: What if I told you you're under arrest, miss.
Zh: “如果我告诉你，你被逮捕了呢，小姐。”

En: Let's change the subject. For the time being. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我们换个话题吧，暂时。”（总结。）

En: I *did*. She takes a step forward. "What is this? I called your *desk*, or whatever it is. The numbers are all over town: 'Call 8-100-2 for Emergencies'."
Zh: “我*打了*。”她上前一步。“这是干什么？我打给你们的*呼叫台*，或者不管那是什么。城里到处都是那个号码：遇到紧急情况，请拨打8-100-2。”

En: That *is* the Emergencies Desk number...
Zh: 那*的确是*紧急呼叫台的号码……

En: Anyone could know that, sire. By looking around and calling the desk. I don't believe a single word she says!
Zh: 所有人都知道这个，大人。只要到处看看，然后打给呼叫台就行。她说的话，吾辈一个字都不信！

En: What time did you make the call?
Zh: “你是什么时候打的电话？”

En: Thursday night. It was late. Some time after twelve.
Zh: “周四晚上。很晚了。应该是12点之后。”

En: The lieutenant nods in your direction: "It checks out."
Zh: 警督朝你的方向点点头：“确认无误。”

En: Okay -- it's not.
Zh: “好吧——不是的。”

En: Like I lied before. Like I did at LCSB... I have to lie *all the time*. She looks at her hands, her fingernails are chipped white. "I'm so tired of it."
Zh: “就像我之前说谎一样。就像我在LCSB的时候……*一直以来*我都不得不撒谎……”她看着自己的双手，手指甲上是一片斑驳的白色。“我真的已经厌倦了。”

En: Like I lied before. Like I lied to my employers. I have to lie *all the time*... She looks at her hands, her fingernails are chipped white. "I'm so tired of it."
Zh: “就像我之前说谎一样。就像我对自己的雇主撒谎一样。我*一直以来*都在撒谎……”她看着自己的双手，手指甲上是一片斑驳的白色。“我真的已经厌倦了。”

En: Anyone could know the number. And that someone coughed -- it means nothing.
Zh: “任何人都可能知道那个号码。而且那个人还咳嗽了一声——这意味不了什么。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: But I know the *time* of the call too! She breathes in. "I know I have not been 100% truthful with you, officers. But I *am* now."
Zh: “但是我还知道打电话的*时间*啊！”她吸了一口气。“我知道自己之前对你没有100%的诚实，警官。但是现在我*会的*。”

En: You sense a little hesitation there -- or maybe even fear? The stress was on the wrong syllable.
Zh: 你感觉到一丝犹豫——或者甚至可以说是害怕？重音的音节不对。

En: Welcome to the wake-up club, brother.
Zh: 欢迎来到清醒俱乐部，老兄。

En: I can give you the *time* too, it was late. After midnight. 12:20. She breathes in. "I know I have not been 100% truthful with you. But I *am* now."
Zh: “我还可以告诉你*时间*。当时很晚，过了午夜。12点20分。”她吸了一口气。“我知道自己对你没有100%的诚实，警官。但是现在我*会的*。”

En: Why is she so *angry* then? If she's so sorry?
Zh: 那她为什么会这么*愤怒*？如果她感到抱歉的话？

En: (Nod.) "Okay."
Zh: （点点头。）“好吧。”

En: Okay, she repeats, her voice more to the side of a cry than a sigh.
Zh: “好吧，”她重复到，她的声音与其说是叹息，还不说是哭泣。

En: I *know* you didn't make the call.
Zh: “我*知道*电话不是你打的。”

En: I did, she replies. Her little fists tremble...
Zh: “我打了，”她回复到。她的小拳头在颤抖……

En: Was the passport bullshit too? That passport you keep hidden?
Zh: “那护照的事也是话说八道了？就是你一直藏起来的那个？”

En: You admit to it!
Zh: “你自己承认了！”

En: I knew it.
Zh: “我就知道。”

En: Good. You can tell me the truth.
Zh: “很好。你可以告诉我事实。”

En: She stands before you, holding her back very straight.
Zh: 她站在你面前，背挺得笔直。

En: Tighten it. You've got her.
Zh: 再收紧一点。你抓到她了。

En: All out of lies now, huh?
Zh: “现在全都是谎言了，是吗？”

En: Devil woman...
Zh: “恶毒的女人……”

En: What? Her nails tap on the steel railing, nervously. The sound of cold metal ringing under the wind.
Zh: “什么？”她的指甲紧张地敲击着铁护栏。冷金属的声音在风中回荡。

En: Her hand trembles. She's only pretending she didn't hear you say *devil woman*.
Zh: 她的手在颤抖。她假装没有听到你说的那句*恶毒的女人*。

En: There was an older woman on the other end. It sounded like she was smoking. She took my complaint. She coughed.
Zh: “另一边是一个老妇人。听起来她是在抽烟。她接受了我的投诉。她还咳嗽了一声。”

En: She sounds like a sixth grader, apologizing.
Zh: 她听起来像是一个六年级学生，正在道歉。

En: Okay. Her voice cracks suddenly -- like there's a garrotte around her neck.
Zh: “好吧。”她的声音突然变得沙哑——好像脖子上套了一根绳索。

En: 'Okay' what?
We don't need to do this. (Back off.)
Zh: “我们不用这么做的。”（后退。）

En: Thank you. She sounds grateful.
Zh: “谢谢你。”她听起来很感激。

En: Okay, yes -- it's not. She's cornered. "It's not my name."
Zh: “好吧，没错——不是的。”她被逼入了绝境。“那不是我的名字。”

En: She's not lying now. She really is very tired. Her metabolism is failing her, the afterglow of whatever narcotics she's been taking is wearing off.
Zh: 现在她没有撒谎。她真的很疲惫。她的新陈代谢正在衰退，不管她服用了什么麻醉剂，后劲也在渐渐消退。

En: Tell me your real name.
Zh: “把你的真名告诉我。”

En: You're probably not even Miss Oranje '37, are you?
Zh: “你可能根本不是37年的奥兰治小姐，对吗？”

En: Enough. (Conclude.)
Zh: “够了。”（总结。）

En: No, it's submerged in a plastic buoy on the coast, in the reeds. It just... doesn't say Klaasje Amandou. It says Annouk Meijer-Smit.
Zh: “不，它就藏在一个塑料浮标里，就在海岸边的芦苇丛。上面写的……并不是卡拉洁·阿曼多。而是安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特。”

En: Falsified documents?
Zh: “伪造文件？”

En: Passport and visa. She nods. "Given to me by my employer. I can't even use them. My employer probably leaked the name -- Meijer-Smit -- to hurt me."
Zh: “护照和签证。”她点点头。“我的雇主给的。我甚至都不能用它们。我的雇主可能泄露了那个名字——梅耶尔-斯密特——来伤害我。”

En: I didn't show up to a rendezvous. They don't take that *lightly*. She rushes to explain: "I didn't show up because I was afraid they'd... do something to me. The job was finished -- I'm just a liability now."
Zh: “我没有赴约。他们不会*掉以轻心*。”她急着解释到：“我没赴约是因为我怕他们会……对我做些什么。工作完成了——现在我变成了一个不利因素。”

En: Why would they do that?
Zh: “他们为什么要这么做？”

En: Where is this buoy?
Zh: “浮标在哪里？”

En: West of the boardwalk -- in the reeds -- on the coast there. She points toward a clump of  ruins on the western horizon. "I put it there when I first arrived. Haven't been there since. I'm not sure I could even find it now..."
Zh: “木板道西边——在芦苇丛里——就在那片海岸上。”她指向西边地平线上的一个废墟。“我一到这里就把它放在那边了。之后再也没去过。我都不确定自己现在还能不能找到……”

En: It's useless.
Zh: “它没用了。”

En: The wind rises. So do the hairs on your back. Somewhere West, small bubbles rise from a plastic ball floating in the water -- rusty, the colour of over-steeped tea.
Zh: 起风了。你后背的毛发也立了起来。在西边的某个地方，小气泡从漂浮在水中的塑料球中升起——那是锈蚀，在海中浸泡太久的颜色。

En: What's happening?
Zh: 出什么事了？

En: Shake your head.
Zh: 摇摇头。

En: Long, spindly arms are spinning the buoy around, turning it, inspecting it like a magic eight-ball, trying to find a way to snap it open...
Zh: 细长的胳膊转动着浮标，翻转着，像是在检查一颗神奇的八号球，正在试图寻找打开它的方法……

En: NOTHING IS USELESS.
Zh: 没有什么是没用的。

En: Long spindly arms spin the buoy... Oh, but it's all gone now...
Zh: 细长的胳膊转动着浮标……哦，但是现在全都没了……

En: It's fake too?
Zh: “它也是假的吗？”

En: She nods. "A fake passport and visa. Given to me by my employer. I can't even use them. My employer probably leaked the name to hurt me."
Zh: 她点点头。“一份假护照还有签证。我的雇主给的。我甚至都不能用它们。我的雇主可能泄露了那个名字来伤害我。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: It's Katarzine Alasije. The smile on her face is timid -- almost painfully so.
Zh: “是卡特琳·艾拉洁。”她脸上的笑容有些羞怯——几乎可以说是痛苦。

En: It's a Graad name -- Zsiemsk or Yugo-Graad in origin -- not Occidental at all. Smells of motor oil, taiga, economic desolation, and rock music infused alcoholism.
Zh: 这是个格拉德名字——起源于西姆斯科或者南格拉德——根本就不是奥西登人。机油的味道，针叶林，经济萧条，还有摇滚乐灌输的酗酒行为。

En: Wait, Klaasje is an abbreviation of Katarzine Alasije?
Zh: “等等，所以卡拉洁是卡特琳·艾拉洁的缩写吗？”

En: Hold on, Katarzine Alasije is not an Oranjese name, is it? It's not even Mundi. It's *Graad*.
Zh: “等等，卡特琳·艾拉洁不是一个奥兰治人的名字，对吗？甚至不是蒙迪的。而是*格拉德*。”

En: It also makes *Klaasje* almost an allacronym -- for Katarzine Alasije.
Zh: 这也让*卡拉洁*几乎成为了一个缩写词——代表卡特琳·艾拉洁。

En: Could it mean *Klaasje* is an allacronym? For Katarzine Alasije?
Zh: 这是不是意味着*卡拉洁*是一个缩写词？代表卡特琳·艾拉洁？

En: Finally we meet, Katarzine Alasije. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我们总算是见面了，卡特琳·艾拉洁。”（继续。）

En: My parents were Zsiemsk immigrants, but I'm nationalized. Oranjese. All I remember is Oranje. She's silent. "Alasije is my father's name."
Zh: “我的父母是西姆斯科移民，不过我是奥兰治人。我唯一记得的只有奥兰治。”她沉默不语。“艾拉洁是我父亲的名字。”

En: She nods. "It was a sentimental thing. I wanted to be more *me* here. This time. So I used my nickname."
Zh: 她点点头。“这是一件很感性的事。我希望这次在这里能更*自我*一些。所以我用了自己的昵称。”

En: Of course -- she doesn't look like an Oranjese woman at all. She's...
Zh: 当然了——她看起来完全不像是奥兰治女性。她……

En: I should have guessed. You don't look Oranjese.
Zh: “我早该猜到的。你看起来不像奥兰治人。”

En: I am. Her smile quivers. "They can never take my sash and my sceptre from me."
Zh: “我是的。”她的笑容有些颤抖。“他们永远也无法夺走我的饰带和权杖。”

En: She nods, her back straight -- ready for whatever is next.
Zh: 她点点头，挺直后背——不管接下来会发生什么，她都已经准备好了。

En: I've always had *that* going for me. Oranjese women do not win beauty pageants at 14.
Zh: “我身上一直都有*那种*东西。奥兰治女人可没办法在14岁赢得选美比赛。”

En: I've always had that going for me -- I only have the birthmarks of an Oranjese woman.
Zh: “我身上一直都有*那种*东西。我只有奥兰治女人的胎记。”

En: A nickname -- who gave you this nickname, Klaasje?
Zh: 昵称——谁给你这个昵称的，卡拉洁？

En: Who gave it to you?
Zh: “谁给你取的？”

En: I don't care.
Zh: 我不在乎。

En: Someone's angry... she's in trouble.
Zh: 有人很生气……她遇到麻烦了。

En: A teenage boy. A million years ago.
Zh: “一个小伙子。那已经是一百年前的事了。”

En: I'll keep calling you Klaasje. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我还会继续叫你卡拉洁。”（继续。）

En: I'll just call you Miss Oranje Disco Dancer. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我会叫你奥兰治迪斯科舞者小姐。”（继续。）

En: Say nothing. (Proceed.)
Zh: 什么也不说。（继续。）

En: That's cool... She looks at the ashtray. Then up at you.
Zh: “挺酷的……”她看着烟灰缸，然后抬眼看着你。

En: She nods. Her round eyes meet yours. They seem moist from the wind on the roof.
Zh: 她点点头。圆溜溜的大眼睛跟你的相遇。屋顶的轻风让它们显得有些氤氲。

En: You can call me whatever you want. She smiles, too tired to laugh.
Zh: “你想怎么称呼我都行。”她笑了，疲惫的有些笑不出来。

En: She doesn't feel like a Klaasje. She feels like nothing.
Zh: 她感觉自己不是卡拉洁。她什么也感觉不到。

En: Ready for the damage. She knows you're grilling her.
Zh: 准备好面对伤害。她知道你会拷问她。

En: I haven't said it aloud in such a long time... that name. She shakes her head.
Zh: “我已经很久没有大声说出来了……那个名字。”她摇摇头。

En: She's filling dead air. Can't stand the silence.
Zh: 她有意停顿了一下。这种尴尬的沉默让她难以忍受。

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Kim, why have we not *arrested* her yet?"
Zh: （转向警督。）“金，我们为什么还不*逮捕*她呢？”

En: There may be grounds here... At least for an extended detention.
Zh: “这里可能有些证据吧……至少可以延长拘留时间。”

En: This bastion of willpower here -- his watchmen have been sleeping too, you can be absolutely certain now. But he's coming around.
Zh: 这座意志力的堡垒——现在你完全可以肯定，他的看守人也睡着了。不过他正在苏醒。

En: A little whimper. The young woman hears you. She's looking around.
Zh: 一阵微弱的声响。年轻女子听见你了。她在四处张望。

En: But I haven't *done* anything... She backs up against the railing -- with a forced smile on her face. "Anything *illegal*."
Zh: “但是我*什么*也没做……”她背靠在栏杆上——脸上生硬地挤出了一点笑容。“没做*违法*的事。”

En: Klaasje... she repeats with a pained smile on her face, her back pressed against the railing.
Zh: “卡拉洁……”她重复到，脸上挂着悲伤的笑容，后背依靠在栏杆上。

En: It was my first real boyfriend who gave me that name. He never recovered from the shit we went through. Because of me.
Zh: “是我第一个真正意义上的男朋友给我起的名字。他永远也没能从我们经历的那些破事中恢复过来。全都因为我。”

En: You almost say the words, then stop -- the lieutenant is off somewhere, 28 kilometres from here, probably reading the *manual* for incarceration. The one he *knows how to use*. Come back and say this with Kim here; you can't handle it without him.
Zh: 你差点就要说出那个词，随即停住了——警督在离这里28公里远的某个地方，很可能正在阅读监禁*手册*。只有他*知道如何使用*。之后再回来，把这一切告诉金；没有他，你一个人处理不了。

En: So you say! But give us one example of deceit -- just *one* example.
Zh: 是您这么说的！不过请给我们举出一个撒谎的例子——只要*一个*。

En: She slowly-slowly lights another cigarette and steadies her breath. As if in the presence of some tiger.
Zh: 她慢慢地点燃又一支香烟，然后稳住呼吸。仿佛眼前出现一只老虎一样。

En: Who was he?
Zh: “他是谁？”

En: Cute. You're still under arrest.
Zh: “很可爱。不过还是要逮捕你。”

En: He was a writer, he made Oranjese lit -- and I destroyed him. She looks you in the eye, her mouth quivering.
Zh: “他是个作家，他创造了奥兰治文学——而我却毁了他。”她看着你的眼睛，嘴唇微微颤抖。

En: Who was he? A boy. A student. It was a good thing and I destroyed him. She looks you in the eye, her mouth quivering.
Zh: “他是谁？一个男孩。一个学生。这本来是一件好事，不过我却毁了他。”她看着你的眼睛，嘴唇微微颤抖。

En: Take it easy. Start at the top, choose at the bottom, it's how we've always done it. No rush...
Zh: 放松点。从头开始，到结尾再做选择，我们一直是这么做的。不要急……

En: Yes. Start at the top, choose at the bottom, it's how we've always done it. No rush...
Zh: 是的。从头开始，到结尾再做选择，我们一直是这么做的。不要急……

En: See?!
Zh: 看见没？！

En: ...but there's nowhere to go. A two-story drop to the plaza mosaic -- if she could, she would've run before.
Zh: ……但是根本无处可去。两层高的楼，下面就是广场的地砖——如果她可以，之前就已经跑了。

En: The lieutenant produces a pair of handcuffs...
Zh: 警督拿出一副手铐……

En: Please, no. Her eyes become round with fear. She tries to back further off...
Zh: “求你了，不要。”她吓得双眼圆睁，想要向后退去……

En: Failure to aid a police officer. You have wasted our time -- in a time-critical investigation.
Zh: “你没有协助警察。浪费了我们的时间——而且这是一次时间紧迫的调查案件。”

En: You misrepresented information about the case. Without the Hardies' confession we'd know nothing.
Zh: “你没有如实叙述跟案件有关的信息。如果没有哈迪兄弟的供认，我们什么也不会知道。”

En: You're a flight risk -- and I've already told you're a *suspect* in a murder investigation, remember?
Zh: “你有潜逃风险——而且我已经说过，你是凶杀案调查的*嫌疑人*，还记得吗？”

En: Fragile, unshielded, her voice is thin and tired.
Zh: 脆弱，毫无防备，她的声音微弱又疲惫。

En: You're a suspect in a murder investigation. You're a flight risk. That is all the reason I need.
Zh: “你是一起凶杀案调查的嫌疑人，有潜逃风险。这些理由对我来说已经够了。”

En: I know I'm shit -- I know. But I haven't done anything. At least nothing illegal.
Zh: “我知道自己是个混账——我知道。但是我什么也没做。至少没做违法的事。”

En: Industrial espionage. We uphold international law here in the Zone of Control.
Zh: “工业间谍。我们控制区是维护国际法的。”

En: Failure to aid a police investigation.
Zh: 没有协助警察调查。

En: Fucking *mind* games! Enough!
Zh: 该死的*心理*游戏！够了！

En: She purposefully misrepresented information crucial to the case.
Zh: 她没有如实叙述跟案件有关的关键信息，这是故意的。

En: That's right, gang. Stern and merciless now -- STERN AND MERCILESS as we reel her in.
Zh: 没错，伙计们。现在必须严厉又无情——吾辈要严厉又无情地把她送入虎口。

En: That's right. Stern and merciless now, STERN AND MERCILESS as we reel her in.
Zh: 没错。现在必须严厉又无情，吾辈要严厉又无情地把她送入虎口。

En: You're not playing these *mind games* with me anymore. (Wag your finger next to your head.) "You're coming in."
Zh: “你不能再跟我玩这些*心理游戏*了。”（在脑袋旁边摇晃你的手指。）“我要把你送进去。”

En: Okay, he's too busy doing something else now. I meant Rhetoric. Anyway, don't listen to that guy -- we're right, she's saintly. Carry on.
Zh: 好吧，现在他忙着做别的事去了。我是说修辞。不管怎么说，别听那个家伙的——我们是对的，她是圣洁的。继续。

En: I think I know who did it -- I know who shot Lely. You know it wasn't me, why would I do it? I have no motive...
Zh: “我想我知道是谁做的——我知道是谁开枪杀了莱利。你知道不是我做的，为什么还要这么做？我没有动机啊……”

En: I think I *know* who did it. Who shot Lely... I can tell you. I can help you.
Zh: “我想我*知道*是谁做的。是谁开枪杀了莱利……我可以告诉你。我可以帮你。”

En: What do you know? Who shot him?
Zh: “你知道些什么？是谁开枪杀了他？”

En: Kim, arrest this woman before she can say anything else. (Ignore her theory.)
Zh: “金，别让她在多说了，马上逮捕她。”（无视她的理论。）

En: She's silent for a second -- as if looking into herself for certainty. Then, in a hushed voice, she says...
Zh: 她沉默了一两秒——似乎在审视自己的确定性。然后，她低声说到……

En: Ruby.
Zh: “露比。”

En: Gearing up for this betrayal is hard for her.
Zh: 对于她来说，做好背叛的准备并不容易。

En: Why do you think it was her?
Zh: “为什么你觉得是她做的？”

En: And *how* could she have killed him?
Zh: “她*怎么*能杀掉他的呢？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: She nods, nervously. Then pulls on her cigarette.
Zh: 她紧张地点点头，然后抽了一口烟。

En: She nods, then nervously pulls on her cigarette.
Zh: 她紧张地点点头，然后抽了一口烟。

En: And what is that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: Sex -- and more. I made the mistake of confiding in her -- I told her I was on the run. She started *protecting* me. It became an unhealthy relationship.
Zh: “性——还有更多。向她吐露秘密是我的错——我告诉她自己在逃跑。她开始*保护*我。这变成了一段不健康的关系。”

En: When I started spending time with Lely, she says in a near-whisper, "she told me to end it. Said there would be *shit* if I didn't. It was not a good meeting. We stopped talking after that, but..."
Zh: “当我开始跟莱利在一起的时候，”她的声音几乎变成了低语，“她让我结束这段关系。还说如果我不这么做的话，后果*不堪设想*。那次会面很不愉快。在那之后我们就不再说话了，但是……”

En: Sex?
What she's saying is -- sex?
Zh: 她的意思是——性？

En: And more. She nods. "I made the mistake of confiding in her -- I told her I was on the run. She started *protecting* me. It became an unhealthy relationship."
Zh: “不止。”她点点头。“向她吐露秘密是我的错——我告诉她自己在逃跑。她开始*保护*我。这变成了一段不健康的关系。”

En: I don't understand. Ruby's a woman, right? It's a *woman's* name.
Zh: “我不明白。露比是个女人，不是吗？这是个*女人*的名字。”

En: She said she's in love with me. She even asked me to run away with her, when I told her I'm a fugitive. She started developing... *notions* about our relationship.
Zh: “她说她爱上我了。当我告诉她自己是个逃犯的时候，她甚至让我跟她一起走。她开始……*计划*我们的关系。”

En: I do not. He turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What exactly about your relationship with this person made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “我不明白。”他转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟这个人到底是什么关系，让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: And you led her on? The lieutenant narrows his eyes.
Zh: “你骗了她？”警督眯起眼睛。

En: A little. I was flattered, you know. But then I had to let her off and it was *not* easy. I came to regret being friendly with her. She looks at her feet. "We maybe kissed. Nothing more."
Zh: “有一点。你知道的，我有些受宠若惊。不过之后我不得不让她放手，而且真的很*不容易*。我有些后悔自己对她太友好了。”她看着自己的双脚。“我们也许是亲吻过。不过没有别的了。”

En: Yes. She nods.
Zh: “是的。”她点点头。

En: Okay, now I understand. Yes.
Zh: “好吧，现在我明白了。没错。”

En: But... you're *also* a woman.
Zh: “但是……你*也是*个女人。”

En: Women like women too, officer. Try not to fixate on this. Let's move on.
Zh: “女人也会喜欢女人的，警官。不要纠结这个问题。我们继续吧。”

En: What do you mean, *like*?
Zh: “你说*喜欢*，是什么意思？”

En: I'm fixating on it, Kim.
Zh: “我就要纠结，金。”

En: Okay, I won't fixate
Zh: “好吧，我不纠结了。”

En: Okay, I understand now. Just clearing it up.
Zh: “好吧，现在我明白了。只是想解释清楚。”

En: I mean romantically. He turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What exactly in your relationship with Ruby made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “我是指浪漫的那种。”他转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟露比到底是什么关系，让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: Don't, he says and turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What -- in your relationship -- made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “别，”他一边说，一边转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟这个人——到底是什么关系——才会让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: Good. He turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What -- in your relationship -- made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “很好。”他转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟这个人——到底是什么关系——才会让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: Actually -- I don't. He turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What exactly in your relationship made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “其实——我没有。”他转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟这个人到底是什么关系，让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: Yes, but what was their relationship *outside* this unequal power dynamic?
Zh: 是的，但是在这个不平等的权力力度*之外*，她们的关系又是什么呢？

En: I don't understand. The lieutenant sounds incredulous. He still has the cuffs in his hand. "What exactly in your relationship made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “我不明白。”警督有些怀疑，手铐还握在他的手里。“你跟这个人到底是什么关系，让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: What was your relationship like? Before this conflict?
Zh: “你们是怎样一种关系？在这次冲突之前？”

En: Sounds like she was fixated on you.
Zh: “听起来她像是看上你了。”

En: This is just sensationalism and guesses.
Zh: “这只是感觉和猜测。”

En: I don't know what to make of this.
Zh: “我不知道这是怎么一回事。”

En: We spent a lot of time together. Listening to the radio and such. She had great milieus. And she was good company. I was sorry it had to end... She pauses.
Zh: “我们在一起很长时间。听收音机啊，之类的。她的电台很棒。而且她是个很好的伙伴。很遗憾这段关系不得不结束……”她停了下来。

En: What was that question about? She's feeding her friend to the wolves here.
Zh: 那个问题是关于什么的？她是在把她的朋友往狼嘴里送。

En: It also has startlingly little to do with arresting her, you suddenly realize...
Zh: 你突然意识到，这也跟逮捕她几乎没有任何关系……

En: When Ruby said there would be 'shit' happening if you didn't end your relationship with the deceased -- was she threatening you?
Zh: “当露比说如果你不跟死者断绝关系，后果会‘不堪设想’的时候——她是在威胁你吗？”

En: She came over one night. Drunk. Said she'd turn my life into a *living hell*. I've been threatened before, so I can tell when someone *knows* how to do it.
Zh: “有天晚上她来找我。喝醉了。她说要把我的生活变成*人间炼狱*。我之前也被威胁过，所以我能分清这个人是不是*知道*该怎么做。”

En: And she's a pro. She must be. To keep the Hardies in line. I tried severing ties with her after that. I thought it had worked, but... She looks through the window of her room.
Zh: “而她是这方面的专家。一定没错。为了管束哈迪兄弟。在那之后，我试图断绝和她的关系。我觉得奏效了，不过……”她看向房间的窗户外面。

En: Somehow she *knew* Lely was dead. She wasn't surprised at all. When we came up here she was calm as a stone too. She cleaned it all up, like she had a... plan.
Zh: “不知怎么的，她*知道*莱利死了。她一点也不吃惊。我们上去的时候，她镇静的像块石头。她把一切都处理地干干净净，好像她有……计划一样。”

En: This is a familiar theory. You had it too, remember? Could it be that Ruby was covering up after herself? The lynching...
Zh: 这是一套很熟悉的理论。你也想过，还记得吗？会不会是露比在掩饰自己？那个私刑……

En: I know what it sounds like. That's why I didn't want to tell you before. She raises her eyes to meet yours. "But she *knew* what had happened -- when I came downstairs."
Zh: “我知道这听起来像什么。所以我之前不想告诉你。”她抬起头，眼神与你交汇。“但是她*知道*发生了什么——当我下楼的时候。”

En: Yet again you're coming up with this... The worst thing is -- it may be true?
Zh: 你再一次想到了这个……最糟糕的事情是——这也许是真的？

En: I know what this sounds like. That's why I didn't want to tell you before. She raises her eyes to meet yours. "But she *knew* what had happened -- when I came downstairs."
Zh: “我知道这听起来像什么。所以我之前不想告诉你。”她抬起头，眼神与你交汇。“但是她*知道*发生了什么——当我下楼的时候。”

En: This is just an assumption.
Zh: “这只是一种假设。”

En: Some of that fear is still with her. She exhales sharply...
Zh: 还有些恐惧依然伴随着她。她用力地呼着气……

En: What are you talking about? She's afraid you'll *arrest* her.
Zh: 您在说什么呢？她是害怕您会*逮捕*她。

En: Did you hear or see the shooter in the course of this?
Zh: “在整个过程中，你有听见或者看见枪手吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Interesting theory. Did she know that door exists? Had you been out here -- with her?
Zh: “很有意思的理论。她知道那扇门的存在吗？你来过这里吗——跟她一起？”

En: You said there was a secret route there, right? She points to the door that leads to the pinball workshop.
Zh: “你说过那里有一条秘密路线，是吧？”她指向通往弹球工作室的大门。

En: The peephole!
Zh: 那个偷窥孔！

En: What is it?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: You're thinking about the peep hole, right? The perforation in the pinball workshop wall.
Zh: “你在想偷窥孔，对吗？就是弹球工作室墙上的那个穿孔。”

En: The peephole. My bed is right next to... She's forgotten her cigarette in the ash tray. "She would have seen us."
Zh: “偷窥孔。我的床就在旁边……”她把香烟忘在了烟灰缸里。“她可能会看见我们。”

En: She would have seen us. My bed is right next to... She's forgotten her cigarette in the ash tray.
Zh: “她可能会看见我们。我的床就在那旁边……”她把香烟忘在了烟灰缸里。

En: No. She shrugs it off. "No -- why would she do that to herself? No, I just remembered you told me about it, and..."
Zh: “不。”她耸耸肩。“不——她为什么要这么对自己？不，我只记得你告诉过我，而且……”

En: You mean to suggest -- she made it?
Zh: “你是想暗示——是她做的？”
