En: She's not really sure. It *was* right above the bed -- she could have seen all sorts of things from there.
Zh: 她不是很确定。它*就在*床的上面——从那里可以看到各种各样的东西。

En: Don't let your imagination run wild here, sire. Keep it on track.
Zh: 别让您的想象力失控了，大人。让它保持正轨。

En: Then slipped back downstairs without anyone noticing? That *is* possible...
Zh: 然后在不被任何人发现的情况下偷偷溜回楼下？那*是*有点可能……

En: Her! Stop letting her distract you.
Zh: 是她！别再因为她分心了。

En: Okay. She looks relieved.
Zh: “好吧。”她像是松了一口气。

En: Thank you. She cradles herself for warmth. "Thank you. You won't regret it."
Zh: “谢谢你。”她摩擦着身体想要暖和一点。“谢谢你。你不会后悔的。”

En: Something tells you: you will.
Zh: 有什么东西告诉你：你会的。

En: *Still...* she says, her voice breaking. "After all this. Sir, please... it's a shitty world and I know I'm shitty too, I know..." Her hand turns into a fist, slowly, crushing the cigarette she's still holding.
Zh: “*不过*……”她的声音变得嘶哑。“虽然发生了这一切，长官，请求你……这是个卑劣世界，我知道自己也是个卑劣的人，我知道的……”她双手握拳，慢慢地，捏碎了还握在手里的香烟。

En: But I don't deserve to be sent to the Moralintern and ground into paste just because I disturbed the *sanctity* of accounting. At some multinational...
Zh: “但是不能只因为我扰乱了会计的*圣洁性*，就要把我交到国际道德伦理委员会手里，给碾成肉饼吧。有些跨国公司……”

En: What do you deserve then?
Zh: “那什么是你应得的呢？”

En: What *other* option do I have?
Zh: “你还有什么*其他*选择吗？”

En: I don't know... She throws away the cigarette. "To spend my days with smoke and drink and dance -- wallowing in shit. Just like everyone else."
Zh: “我不知道……”她扔掉香烟。“抽烟喝酒跳舞度日——在这个污秽的世界里浮浮沉沉。就像其他人一样。”

En: You have those *station calls*, right? Where I have to show up at the station or I'll become a fugitive. You can write me one. You don't have to take me in right now... I promise I won't go out anymore, at night. I'll be right here.
Zh: “你们有那些*警局传唤通知*，对吧？我会出现在警局里，或者会成为逃犯。你可以给我写一个。你不用现在就把我抓进去的……我保证以后晚上再也不出去了。我会待在这里。”

En: Kim, arrest her before this drags out any longer. (Arrest her.)
Zh: “金，别让她在多说了，马上逮捕她。”（逮捕她。）

En: I will escort you to the motor carriage outside, miss.
Zh: “我会护送你到外面的車里，小姐。”

En: I do have a form... He looks at his notes.
Zh: “我确实有张表格……”他看着自己的笔记。

En: You do have the form... He looks at your ledger.
Zh: “你确实有张表格……”他看着你的手册。

En: That's it. I'm calling it. Kim is beyond compromised.
Zh: 就这样吧。我来传唤。金是不会妥协的。

En: Definitely, sire.
Zh: 当然了，大人。

En: Let's write you a Station Call, Katarzine. If you miss it -- I can't help you anymore. (Write a Station Call.)
Zh: “我们给你写个警局传唤通知，卡特琳。如果你错过了——我再也帮不了你了。”（写一份警局传唤通知。）

En: Let's write you a Station Call, Klaasje. If you miss it -- I can't help you anymore. (Write a Station Call.)
Zh: “我们给你写个警局传唤通知，卡拉洁。如果你错过了——我再也帮不了你了。”（写一份警局传唤通知。）

En: Let's write you a Station Call, Miss Oranje. If you miss it -- I can't help you anymore. (Write a Station Call.)
Zh: “我们给你写个警局传唤通知，奥兰治小姐。如果你错过了——我再也帮不了你了。”（写一份警局传唤通知。）

En: Let's write you a Station Call. If you miss it -- I can't help you anymore. (Write a Station Call.)
Zh: “我们给你写个警局传唤通知。如果你错过了——我再也帮不了你了。”（写一份警局传唤通知。）

En: What will have changed in two months? Or one month?
Zh: “两个月后会有什么变化？或者一个月？”

En: *Everything* could change. She looks around. "This city, the extradition rules... The people after me could be in jail. Or maybe Revachol..." She falls silent.
Zh: “*一切*都会变。”她看了看周围。“这座城市，引渡法案……追踪我的人可能会进监狱。或者也许瑞瓦肖……”她陷入沉默。

En: I won't, sir... She exhales, shaking visibly. Then she collects herself.
Zh: “我不会的，先生……”她呼出一口气，明显地颤抖着。然后她重新恢复了镇定。

En: I'll let you off the hook -- for now. Don't make me regret it. (Don't arrest her.)
Zh: “我会放过你——不过是暂时的。别让我后悔。”（不要逮捕她。）

En: With a muffled whimper, she extends her wrists. They're white, bony -- exposed below the rolled-up sleeves of her jumpsuit.
Zh: 伴随着一声压抑的呜咽声，她伸出了手腕。它们很白，骨瘦嶙峋——暴露在连体裤卷起的袖子下面。

En: Waiting for your teeth and talons to cut into...
Zh: 等待你的牙齿和爪子咬上去……

En: He puts the handcuffs away. "These won't be necessary. I will take you to Station 57 myself -- and slow the extradition process as much as I can."
Zh: 他把手铐收了起来。“没这个必要。我亲自送你去57分局——还会尽我所能地拖延引渡进程。

En: It may be possible to stall it indefinitely. But you *will* be safer there for the purposes of this investigation. He turns to you.
Zh: “可能会无限期的拖延下去。不过出于调查的原因，你在那里*会*更安全一些。”他转向你。

En: I won't... She takes the slip, shaking visibly. Then she collects herself.
Zh: “我不会的……”她接过通知单，明显有些颤抖。然后她重新恢复了镇定。

En: This may have been a *grave* mistake, sire.
Zh: 这可能是个*严重的*错误，大人。

En: Maybe. Maybe not. Mercy is rarely a *complete* mistake.
Zh: 也许是，也许不是。仁慈很少是*完全*错误的。

En: By god, she's playing him too! Like a fiddle, sire... Who would have known?
Zh: 天啦，她也开始玩弄他了！就像摆弄一把小提琴，大人……谁又能知道呢？

En: I can do this on my own. She's not an immediate flight risk. See you tomorrow morning, officer. Downstairs, at the Whirling-in-Rags. Or outside your shack.\n
Zh: “我自己可以的。她不会马上潜逃的。我们明天早上再见了，警官。褴褛飞旋，楼下。或者在你的小屋外面。”

En: I can do this on my own. She's not an immediate flight risk. See you tomorrow morning, officer. Downstairs, at the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “我自己可以的。她不会马上潜逃的。我们明天早上再见了，警官。褴褛飞旋，楼下。”

En: Maybe Revachol could be free? I could be tried in a free Revachol? An independent state that doesn't hand its detainees over to the Moralintern.
Zh: “也许瑞瓦肖会重获自由呢？我可能会在一个自由的瑞瓦肖接受审讯？一个独立的国家是不会把在扣押人员交给国际道德伦理委员的。”

En: A free Revachol... There is a low, distant rumble on the motor tract. A great machine, shaking the pillars down south. Electricity runs up your spine.
Zh: 一个自由的瑞瓦肖……高速公路上响起低沉又遥远的轰隆声。一台伟大的机器，把柱子摇向南方。电流沿着你的脊柱一路向上窜。

En: The world can change. It's changed before. Her round light brown eyes shining with tears.
Zh: “这个世界可以改变。之前就变过。”她浅棕色的圆眼睛里闪烁着泪光。

En: Okay. Well... Her round light brown eyes shine with the tears.
Zh: “好吧。那……”她浅棕色的圆眼睛里闪烁着泪光。

En: Don't. The world can change. It has changed before... She looks at you, frozen to her spot.
Zh: “不。世界是会改变的。之前就变过……”她看着你，僵在原地。

En: That's not the answer she was hoping for. Thought that you were some kind of patriot, or communard, did she?
Zh: 这不是她期望的答案。她以为您是某种爱国主义者，或者公社社员，是吗？

En: When it happened -- did you hear a gunshot?
Zh: “那是什么时候发生的——你听见枪声了吗？”

En: When he was shot? She thinks. "I may have. I don't know. I couldn't hear anything over the glass exploding."
Zh: “他是什么时候中枪的？”她想了想。“我可能听见了。我不知道。除了玻璃爆裂的声音，我什么也听不见。”

En: The gunshot wasn't that loud? This is something to keep in mind when assessing the distance of the shot.
Zh: 枪声并不响？在评估射击距离的时候，要把这点记在心里。

En: I know you can do that -- just let me come in on my own. In two months. Or maybe even one month. That's all I need...
Zh: “我知道你可以这么做——让我自己去吧。两个月以内。或者甚至是一个月。我只要求这个就行了……”

En: There is desperation there -- in that silence. A cornered animal looking for a way out.
Zh: 那片沉默中——有一种绝望。一只被逼入绝境的动物在寻找逃出去的方法。

En: I also find it hard to believe.
Zh: “我也觉得难以置信。”

En: I do like the sound of that... another Revachol. Free. Independent.
Zh: “我的确喜欢这种说法……另一个瑞瓦肖。自由。独立。”

En: Not on my watch.
Zh: “不在我的管辖范围之内。”

En: This has got nothing to do with anything.
Zh: “这和任何事都没有关系。”

En: I find that hard to believe, miss. The handcuffs jingle in his hands...
Zh: “我觉得这很难让人相信，小姐。”手铐在他的手中叮当作响……

En: What was that feeling?
Zh: 这是什么感觉？

En: IT'S COMING.
Zh: 它来了。

En: The political gambit has failed.
Zh: 政治策略失败了。

En: Her political overture has succeeded, we see?
Zh: 她的政治提议成功了？

En: I know, but... She doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “我知道，但是……”她不知道该说些什么。

En: This was her last card. There is nothing more she can say. The final decision is yours -- you alone stand on the throne of your Mind.
Zh: 这是她的最后一张牌。她已经没什么可以说的了。最后的决定权在你手上——只有你一个人，站在心灵的王座上。

En: You said you studied *Oranjese lit*. What is with this fugitive stuff?
Zh: “你说你研究的是*奥兰治文学*。这跟逃亡有什么关系呢？”

En: I *did* -- I also had a side job selling insurance that I was really good at. Got picked up by a bank. *Competitive intelligence*, they called it.
Zh: “我真*学过*——我还做过卖保险的兼职，而且我真的挺擅长的。然后被银行挑中了。他们叫它，*竞争情报*。”

En: After that I sort of... She smiles. "Transitioned out of the whole culture scene."
Zh: “在那之后，我算是……”她笑了。“从整个文化背景中过渡出来了。”

En: The grand expanse of water is barely visible in the darkness of the night. The horizon is there, somewhere...
Zh: 在这个漆黑的夜里，几乎很难看见那片广阔的水域。地平线就在那里，某个地方……

En: The light refracts. She takes out her lighter.
Zh: 光线折射出去。她掏出自己的打火机。

En: West of the boardwalk, in the reeds? We have to check this buoy out. (Accept task.)
Zh: “木板道西边的芦苇丛里？我们需要去确认一下那个浮标。”（接受任务。）

En: West of the boardwalk, in the reeds? I need to check this buoy out. (Accept task.)
Zh: “木板道西边的芦苇丛里？我需要去确认一下那个浮标。”（接受任务。）

En: Fine. Tell me one more thing. (Move on.)
Zh: “好吧。再告诉我一件事。”（继续。）

En: Of course. She nods.
Zh: “当然可以。”她点点头。

En: The lieutenant makes a note of it.
Zh: 警督记下笔记。

En: You're welcome to it. It's in the reeds northwest of here, past a broken sewage pipe. Right near the waterline.
Zh: “你尽管去吧。它就在这里西北方向的芦苇丛里，经过一条坏掉的污水管。就在水线附近。”

En: You're welcome to it. It's in the reeds northwest of here, past a broken sewage pipe. Right near the waterline.
Zh: “你尽管去吧。它就在这里西北方向的芦苇丛里，经过一条坏掉的污水管。就在水线附近。”

En: If you have doubts, just give us *one* example of deceit! Just one, and we will gladly change our minds.
Zh: 如果你有疑问，那就给我们说*一个*欺骗的例子吧！只要一个，我们会很乐意改变主意的。

En: This guy won't budge. You have to wake  Multi-Face up -- forcefully -- if you want to continue pushing her.
Zh: 这家伙不听话。你快让这个多面鬼醒醒——用武力--如果你还想对她施加压力的话。

En: It looks like this one's not waking up. You'll have to do it without pushing her further. At least for now.
Zh: 看来这家伙没有醒来。你不能再继续逼迫她了。至少暂时不行。

En: This one's *still* not budging. You'll have to do it without pushing her further.
Zh: 这家伙*还是*没有顺从。你不能再继续逼迫她了。

En: You log your work *every week*. It's *all* transmitted to COMINSUR. I couldn't just beg you not to enter my name... So I lied.
Zh: “你*每周*都会记录你的工作。然后*全部*会传送给委员会。我不能就这么求你不要输入我的名字……所以我撒谎了。”

En: Yes they can! For *lying*!
Zh: 他们可以的！因为*撒谎*！

En: I agree. The lieutenant turns to her. "You wouldn't give us your real name -- not when people are after you."
Zh: “我同意。”警督转向她。“你不会告诉我们真名的——特别是有人在追踪你的时候。”

En: Wait, wait, everyone -- just *wait*. What is this? Derail this whole thing immediately and *investigate*.
Zh: 等等，等一下，大家——先*等等*。这是干什么？马上让整件事脱离轨道，继续*调查*吧。

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: Before we do... He turns to her, the cuffs still in his hand. "What exactly in your relationship made you think she's romantically interested in you?"
Zh: “在这之前……”他转向她，手铐还握在手里。“你跟这个人到底是什么关系，让你觉得她对你有好感呢？”

En: Well, I'll be damned! That's one more thing we know about the world!
Zh: 呃，那就真是见鬼了！我们对这个世界的了解又多了一件！

En: Could it be that Ruby was cleaning up after herself? That the lynching was a cover-up for *her* -- and she organized the boys to do it?
Zh: 也许是那个露比在掩饰自己？那个私刑其实是为*她*打的掩护——而且她还组织兄弟去干的？

En: It's ominous -- you buying into her theory and running with it. Be careful with this Logic guy.
Zh: 这是个不祥的预兆——你相信了她的理论而且还被牵着鼻子走。要小心这个叫逻辑的家伙。

En: No, wait! Look at her! Teeth like pearls, face like a pebble -- she looks like a Twenties movie star. You can almost see her in Automatik Polychrome. Those posters in Ruby's lorry...
Zh: 不，等等！看看她！牙齿像珍珠，脸庞像鹅卵石——她看起来就像一个20多岁的电影明星。你几乎能看见她在那些自动多色印刷品。露比货車里的那些海报……

En: Look at her: teeth like pearls, face like a pebble -- she looks like a Twenties movie star. You can almost see her in Automatik Polychrome. Those posters in Ruby's lorry...
Zh: 看看她：牙齿像珍珠，脸庞像鹅卵石——她看起来就像一个20多岁的电影明星。你几乎能看见她在那些自动多色印刷品。露比货車里的那些海报……

En: She was her type.
Zh: 她是她喜欢的类型。

En: You've been trying to kick that door down, right? She points to the barred door, next to the window. "Maybe it leads downstairs?"
Zh: “你想试着把那道门踹开，对吗？”她指向封起来的大门，就在窗户旁边。“也许它是通往楼下的？”

En: You've been through there, right? I saw you come out. She points to the door that leads to the pinball workshop. "It leads downstairs."
Zh: “你是从那里过来的，对吗？我看见你走出来的。”她指着通往弹球工作室的那扇大门。“它是通往楼下的。”

En: She could have had access to the roof. She points to the barred door next to the window. "You've noticed that door there, right? Maybe it leads downstairs?"
Zh: “她可能来过屋顶。”她指着窗户旁边那扇封闭的门。“你已经注意到那扇门了，对吗？也许它是通往楼下的？”

En: She could have come up through there, then taken the shot -- right here, where I stand. It was too dark outside, I wouldn't have seen her.
Zh: “她可能是从那里上来的，然后开枪——就在这里，我现在站的地方。外面太黑了，我不可能看见她的。”

En: Yes. She's been up here many times. She used to come to drink on the roof when... The young woman falls silent -- her mouth moves without a sound.
Zh: “是的。她来过这里很多次。那时她经常会来屋顶喝酒……”年轻女子陷入沉默——嘴唇在移动，但是没有发出一点声音。

En: Yes, of course. She's been up here many times, jacking private stations off the ring antenna. She used to come here, to drink on the roof with me. Before it got... weird.
Zh: “是的，当然了。她来过这里很多次，用环形天线架设私人电台。她以前会上来这里，跟我一起在屋顶上喝酒。在一切变的……奇怪之前。”

En: She could have come to the roof through that, then taken the shot -- right here, where I stand. It was dark outside, I wouldn't have seen her.
Zh: “她可能是从那里上来的，然后开枪——就在这里，我现在站的地方。外面太黑了，我不可能看见她的。”

En: She could have come to the roof through that, then made the shot right here, where I stand. It was dark outside, I wouldn't have seen her.
Zh: “她可能是从那里上来的，然后从这里开枪，就在我现在的位置。外面太黑了，我不可能看见她的。”

En: Why? She told us some pretty interesting things there.
Zh: 为什么？她跟我们说了很多有意思的事情啊。

En: Don't arrest her. Push her a bit more but then let her off. That Ruby theory was solid. (And she's beautiful.)
Zh: 不要逮捕她。再把她逼的更紧一点，然后放过她。关于露比的那套理论已经很可靠了。（而且她还那么漂亮。）

En: She's stuck here. She's already in prison -- look around! She's only trying to help you...
Zh: 她被困在这里了。她已经身陷囹圄——看看这周围！她只是想帮你……

En: She knows something!
Zh: 她知道些什么！

En: I've made my choice. (Decide what to do with her.)
Zh: “我做好选择了。”（决定怎么处置她。）

En: She looks at you in silence, her face filled with fear, lips parted...
Zh: 她沉默不语地看着你，脸上写满恐惧，双唇微启……

En: She's gearing up for a last stand. This is it.
Zh: 她已经准备好背水一战。要来了。

En: Chill. I'm the cool cop, remember?
Zh: “冷静一点。我是很酷的警探，还记得吗？”

En: She cracks a weary smile. "I'm sorry for wasting your time. I should have known you'd... that you'd find out."
Zh: 她露出一个疲惫的笑容。“很抱歉，让你浪费时间了。我应该知道你会……发现的。”

En: If you knew we would find out eventually -- why *did* you waste our time?
Zh: “如果你知道我们最后肯定会发现——为什么*还要*浪费我们的时间？”

En: Why *did* you waste my time then?
Zh: “那你为什么*要*浪费我的时间？”

En: You're right... there's more.
Zh: “你说得对……还有更多原因。”

En: More?
Zh: “更多？”

En: You answer to the Coalition Government -- and, by extension, the *Moralintern*... She reaches for a new cigarette.
Zh: “你要对联盟国政府负责——引申开来，就是*国际道德伦理委员会*……”她伸手去拿一支新的香烟。

En: The RCM isn't the lapdog of the Moralintern. You don't have to worry.
Zh: “RCM不是国际道德伦理委员会的宠物狗。你不用担心。”

En: She nods. "I have people after me. And they have friends in the Moralintern -- some *are* Moralintern. If I show up in your records, officer, they will find me. They will..."
Zh: 她点点头。“有人在追踪我。而且他们有朋友在国际道德伦理委员会——有些本身*就是*国际道德伦理委员会的人。如果我出现在你的记录里，警官，他们就会找到我。他们会……”

En: What happens if they find you?
Zh: “如果他们找到你了会怎么样？”

En: They will kill me, officer. If you file my name, take me in for questioning, enter me into the Moralintern mill...
Zh: “他们会杀了我，先生。如果你记录下我的名字——把我逮捕进去审讯——就等于把我送到国际道德伦理委员会的手上……”

En: There's a wince and a pained little smile. "Well, then I'm fucked. For nothing. This murder didn't have anything to do with me."
Zh: 她的脸庞在抽搐，露出了一个痛苦的微笑。“然后我就完蛋了。白白送死。这起凶杀案跟我一点关系都没有。”

En: Fucked? People after her? *Moralintern* people? This isn't Oranjese lit!
Zh: 送死？有人在追她？*国际道德伦理委员会*的人？这跟什么奥兰治文学根本没关系！

En: Here we go. For once you seem to have her off-guard.
Zh: 很好。这一次你好像打了她一个措手不及。

En: Nothing to do with her? You'll be the judge of that.
Zh: 跟她没有关系？这点应该交给你来判断。

En: A lot of people got hurt, she concludes. "But that's just more of my shit you shouldn't have to deal with. You're solving a murder."
Zh: “很多人受到了伤害，”她总结到。“但是这些破事不应该交给你去处理。你还要破获凶杀案呢。”

En: I... She looks down, then into your eyes. "One of them killed themselves. Because of me."
Zh: “我……”她低下头，然后抬头望着你的眼睛。“有一个人自杀了。因为我。”

En: That can wait. Look into her eyes -- there's more.
Zh: 那个可以等一下。看着她的眼睛——还有更多东西。

En: What did you *do*?
Zh: “你*做了*什么？”

En: Let's not pry. It's not part of this case.
Zh: 还是别刺探了。这跟案子没有关系。

En: Of course. This case takes precedence.
Zh: 当然。案子的优先级更高。

En: That's... (Shake your head.) "Not easy to deal with."
Zh: “那还真是……”（摇摇头。）“不容易处理啊。”

En: The young woman looks at her cigarette -- with melancholy. It's almost gone.
Zh: 年轻女子看着香烟——满脸哀愁。它几乎快要熄灭了。

En: (Nod.) "Let's get back to what happened here, okay?"
Zh: （点头。）“那我们还是说回这里发生的事吧，好吗？”

En: How do you deal with anything? It's all just... She looks at you with pleading eyes. "How do *you* do it?"
Zh: “你是怎么处理问题的？这一切都……”她用恳求的眼神看着你。“*你*是怎么做到的？”

En: I *don't* either.
Zh: “我也*做不到*。”

En: There you have it. She's already almost devoured the cigarette she just lit and looks at it sadly. "The way of the warrior."
Zh: “你懂了。”刚点燃的香烟差不多已经被她吞下去了，她悲伤地看着它。“战士的道路。”

En: She's already in prison here. For what happened. And she is prepared to never leave. Even after his death.
Zh: 她已经身陷囹圄了。因为发生的事情。而且她永远也不打算离开。就算在他死后也是一样。

En: Around her -- a drop in atmospheric pressure. Not long from now it will get dark. The rain will come. And the air will start moving faster, circling the box she stands on...
Zh: 在她周围——气压下降了。不久之后，天就会变黑。雨会降落。空气会流动地更快，在她脚下的箱子周围盘旋……

En: Her chest rises and falls with each word. She keeps herself together, and says it...
Zh: 她的胸口随着说出的每一个字起起伏伏。她控制住了自己，而且说出来了……

En: You don't have to continue if you don't want to.
Zh: “如果你不想的话，可以不用继续。”

En: No. I want to. Let's get this done.
Zh: “不。我想。让我们把这件事做完。”

En: It's okay. Tell us as much as you're comfortable with. He waits for her to continue.
Zh: “没事的。你自己能说多少就说多少。”他在等她继续。

En: Alright. Tell me as much as you're comfortable with.
Zh: “好吧。你自己能说多少就说多少。”

En: Did you hear or see the shooter -- at any point?
Zh: “你有听见或者看见枪手吗——在任何时间点？”

En: Wait -- Titus said she was gurning her jaw off. Much more than usual.
Zh: 等等——提图斯说她的下巴都快扭曲了。比平常更严重一些。

En: This doesn't fit with the testimony of Titus Hardie. He said she was higher than usual.
Zh: 这跟提图斯·哈迪的证词并不吻合。他说她比平常要更嗨一些。

En: Titus said you looked pretty high.
Zh: “提图斯说你看来挺嗨的。”

En: It doesn't matter. Better not to mention it.
Zh: 没关系的。最好还是别提了。

En: Of course. Keep your eyes open for the killer instead.
Zh: 当然。还是睁大眼睛留心凶手吧。

En: Of course. Keep your eyes open for the killer instead.
Zh: 当然。还是睁大眼睛留心凶手吧。

En: Oh yeah... She tilts her head. "I did one of his lines. Just to clear my head."
Zh: “是啊……”她歪着头。“我抽了点他的可卡因。只是为了让自己清醒一些。”

En: Good thinking. Clear your head. You should clear *your* head... get into his mindset.
Zh: 好主意。让自己清醒。你也应该让*你的*头脑清醒一点……进入他的思维模式。

En: No -- not just yet. He turns to the woman. "Who is *Ruby*?"
Zh: “不——还不行。”他转向女子。“*露比*是谁？”

En: *Let's go on*, what are you talking about? Did you forget how to cop? There's a new actor in the investigation -- change the question immediately!
Zh: *我们继续吧*，你在说什么。你忘记怎么当警察了吗？这次调查里出现了一个新角色——马上改变问题啊！

En: I don't understand what you're doing detective -- *go on*? A new name just emerged -- Ruby! This is important. Change the question immediately.
Zh: 我不明白你在干什么，警探——*继续*？刚刚出现一个新名字——露比！这很重要。马上改变问题。

En: I can't restrain this one. The need to *cop* is too strong. You just need to ask it.
Zh: 我控制不了这个。*侦查*的需求太强烈。你需要问出来。

En: Ruby?!
Zh: 露比？！

En: Would you say she is the... He looks at you. "Eighth Hardie boy?"
Zh: “你说她是……”他看着你。“第八个哈迪兄弟？”

En: Why not.
Zh: “为什么不是呢。”

En: Would you say she is... the eighth Hardie boy?
Zh: “你说她是……第八个哈迪兄弟？”

En: They tampered with the body to disguise the cause of death.
Zh: 他们破坏了尸体，伪造死因。

En: To mislead you! They were tampering with the body.
Zh: 为了误导你！他们破坏了尸体。

En: How could we have *missed* that... He shakes his head. "And when was the tampering -- twenty minutes after death?"
Zh: “我们怎么会*漏掉*那个呢……”他摇摇头。“破坏行为是什么时候发生的——死后20分钟吗？”

En: What did you do while they were hanging him?
Zh: “他们把他吊起来的时候，你在做什么？”

En: Ruby said to wait here. She also said I wouldn't see her for a while; that we should lay low or something... so I did.
Zh: “露比让我在这里等着。她还说我有一段时间不会看见她了；说我们应该保持低调什么的……所以我照做了。”

En: There is something suspicious about that theory, but you can't put your finger on it.
Zh: 那个理论有些可疑的地方，但是你还说不清楚。

En: It could have been a *desire* murder. Maybe an act of jealousy?
Zh: 这可能是一次*欲望*谋杀。也许是出于妒忌？

En: It's okay if you did it in self defence.
Zh: “如果你这么做是为了自卫的话，其实没问题的。”

En: I did not kill him to defend myself from rape, she says. "I told you before. That wasn't what happened."
Zh: “我没有因为反抗强暴而杀了他，”她说到。“我已经跟你说过了。事情不是这样的。”

En: We'll try to make it less stressful, miss. But these are questions we *need* to ask.
Zh: “我们会尽量减少你的压力，小姐。但是有些问题我们*必须*要问。”

En: Relax. One more thing.
Zh: “放松。还有一件事。”

En: Sure. She nods and relaxes her hands.
Zh: “当然。”她点点头，放松了自己的双手。

En: No. She shakes her head. "People don't take drugs to kill each other -- they take them to feel okay with each other."
Zh: “不。”她摇摇头。“大家吸毒不是为了杀掉彼此——而是为了彼此感觉更好。”

En: I don't.
Zh: “我没有。”

En: ...or maybe you should take her to the station? For safekeeping. She *lied* to you. And she's a flight risk.
Zh: ……或者你应该把她带到警局？为了保护她的安全。她对你*撒谎*了。而且她有潜逃的风险。

En: No. That won't be necessary. Just leave her alone, she's been through enough. She doesn't need this... police brutality.
Zh: 不。没这个必要。不用管她，她已经遭了很多罪。她不需要……警察的暴行。

En: Multi-Face! I'm beginning to doubt your judgement -- are you sure you're not sleeping on the post? You have to wake this one up -- with force -- if you want to continue pushing her.
Zh: 你这个多面人！我开始怀疑你的判断了——你确定自己没在岗位上睡着？你要叫醒这个人——用强迫的——如果你想继续推着她往前走的话。

En: You're not done. She's a flight risk and she lied to you -- wake up, man.
Zh: 你还没完事呢。她有潜逃的风险，而且还跟你撒了谎——快醒醒，老兄。

En: You're not done. She *lied* to you. And she's a flight risk -- wake up, man.
Zh: 你还没完事呢。她对你*撒了谎*，而且还有潜逃风险——快醒醒，老兄。

En: Nice view you've got here.
Zh: “你这里的景色很好。”

En: You mentioned a cleaning lady -- I think I need one. (Proceed.)
Zh: “你提到一个清洁女工——我觉得我需要一个。”（继续。）

En: Is there a cleaning lady? I think I *need* one. (Proceed politely.)
Zh: “有清洁女工的吗？我觉得我*需要*一个。”（礼貌地继续。）

En: No time for pleasantries. We have questions for you. (Proceed in a rush.)
Zh: “没时间开玩笑了。我们有问题要问你。”（匆忙继续。）

En: Have I ever.... This is the one and only Superstar Cop. He's kind of a big deal around here.
Zh: “我有没有……这就是独一无二的超级警探。他可是这里的大人物。”

En: Have I ever! This is the biggest fan of Ostentatious Orchestrations I have seen in my *life*. And I have seen a few...
Zh: “当然见过！这是我*这辈子*遇见的招摇乐团最大的粉丝。而且我之前还见过不少……”

En: 'Oh yeah,' she declares. "'Life gets hard -- but we go on.'"
Zh: “‘当然，’”她说到。“‘生活很艰难——但是我们也要继续。’”

En: What happened, man? The end was supposed to be mere seconds away. Why are we still here?
Zh: “出什么事了，老兄？末日不是已经近在咫尺了吗。我们为什么还留在这里？”

En: His arrival is a sign of the end times. All this, she looks around, "will come crashing down in mere days."
Zh: “他的到来是世界末日的标志。这一切，”她环顾四周，“将会在短短几天内崩溃。”

En: Although -- if I remember correctly -- there are still some centuries to go. Very anticlimactic.
Zh: “不过——如果我记的没错——还有好几个世纪的时间呢。非常虎头蛇尾。”

En: Now, I'm no expert on cops, but that sounds like one special police officer to me.
Zh: “我不是警察方面的专家，不过在我听起来，好像是一个非常特殊的警官。”

En: Yes, my copotype is unbelievably rare.
Zh: “是的，我这种类型罕见地难以置信。”

En: The words *washed away* sound distant and strange suddenly. Somewhere far away, a dog barks.
Zh: *冲走*这个词听起来很遥远，突然间还有些奇怪。远处的某个地方传来一阵狗吠。

En: There is something in her eyes -- the same sadness? Yes, from back in the hallway when she first told you about the hanging, in the hallway.
Zh: 她的眼睛里有些什么……相同的悲伤？是的，还是她第一次在走廊里告诉你上吊男人的时候，没错，是在走廊里。

En: There is something in her light brown eyes... a sadness. When she thinks about the death of that man.
Zh: 她的浅棕色眼睛里有些什么……一种悲伤。在她想到那个男人死去的时候。

En: It's like she's... disappointed this wasn't about more entertaining matters.
Zh: 就好像她有点……失望，因为这不是什么更加令人愉悦的事。

En: What could she be thinking of? It's hard to say -- her shoulders are relaxed, her eyes on the cigarette.
Zh: 她可能在想些什么呢？很难说——她的肩膀很放松，眼睛一直盯着香烟。

En: Let's talk about Sunday night again -- the sounds from my room.
Zh: “我们再说说周日晚上吧——就是我房间里的声音。”

En: Now that Kim isn't here -- let's talk about Sunday night.
Zh: “现在金不在——我们说说周日晚上的事吧。”

En: Let's return to Sunday night -- the sounds from my room
Zh: “我们说回周日晚上吧——就是我房间里的声音。”

En: I noticed your room is close to mine. I have a personal question.
Zh: “我注意到你的房间离我的很近。我有个私人问题。”

En: Now she's trapped here, in the cage -- just like you.
Zh: 现在她被困在这里了，在笼子里——就跟你一样。

En: If that's true then *Revacholian lit* would fit you like a glove.
Zh: 如果说这是真的，那*瑞瓦肖文学*就像手套一样适合你。

En: Of course she won a beauty pageant. She's very symmetrical.
Zh: 她当然会赢得选美比赛了。她很匀称。

En: The lieutenant gives you a little look.
Zh: 警督看了你一眼。

En: We should have discussed your history before we came here, he thinks. So many unpleasant surprises with this guy...
Zh: 我们来之前应该就讨论过你的历史了，他想到。这家伙真是有太多让人不不愉快的惊喜……

En: It's good we discussed your history with her, he thinks. I can play along.
Zh: 能跟她讨论你的历史其实挺好的，他想到。我可以配合。

En: Sheer terror. Panic and screaming.
Zh: 纯粹的恐惧。让人惊慌，尖叫。

En: She's not angry, but it's true: she's gonna have to find some more Preptide now.
Zh: 她没有生气，不过是真的：现在她得去找更多帕立太特了。

En: I need to talk to you about your room again.
Zh: “我需要再跟你说说你房间的事。”

En: She's straight as a stick, suddenly.
Zh: 突然之间，她站地像棍子一样笔直。

En: Miss, I'm investigating, uh... (Gesture down to the yard.) "The people who put him there said to talk to you."
Zh: “小姐，我在调查，呃……”（朝院子下面比个手势。）“把他放在那里的人说，我们应该跟你聊聊。”

En: Miss, I'm investigating, uh... (Gesture down to the body in the yard.) "The people who put him there said to talk to you."
Zh: “小姐，我在调查，呃……”（朝院子下面的尸体比个手势。）“把他放在那里的人说，我们应该跟你聊聊。”

En: Across the city, the lieutenant stops to consider a smoke break. He shrugs off the urge: maybe tonight. His absence is palpable. Too late now.
Zh: 在城市的另一边，警督停下来，打算抽根烟休息一下。他甩掉了这种冲动：还是今晚再说吧。你明显能感觉到他的缺席。现在已经太晚了。

En: Know that I am no longer the Cop of the Apocalypse. The prophecy was wrong. The signs meant nothing. The hangover has passed.
Zh: “我已经不再是末日警探了。预言是错误的。那些兆头毫无意义。宿醉已经过去了。”

En: Oh yes! Legends of Room #1 have made their way around the building. They say a portal to Hell has opened in there.
Zh: “是的！1号房间的传说已经在这里传开了。他们说那里已经开启了通往地狱的大门。”

En: Welcome to the roof. The young woman has a cigarette in one hand and a cup of coffee in the other.
Zh: “欢迎来到屋顶。”年轻女子一手夹着一支烟，另一只手端着咖啡。

En: I'm sorry for the mess down there. The cleaning lady hasn't come by in *days*. I'm beginning to wonder if she ever will...
Zh: “抱歉，下面搞得那么乱。清洁女工已经好*几天*没有来过了。我在想她还会不会出现……”

En: ...then toned over several years of self-defence training. You can sense it in the sharp pointing of her elbows.
Zh: ……经过多年的防身术训练后有所改善。你能从她的手肘尖角上感觉出来。

En: Where a ripe corpse still hangs from the tree, spotted with green and purple.
Zh: 树上还挂着一具成熟的尸体，上面布满绿色和紫色的斑点。

En: Where the ripe corpse of a man still lies on the ground, spotted with green and purple.
Zh: 地面上还躺着一个男人成熟的尸体，上面布满绿色和紫色的斑点。

En: Below her silvery jumpsuit -- an athletic young body. Built long and lean...
Zh: 在她那银色的连体裤下面——是一副年轻又健美的身体。修长而纤瘦……

En: I'm Kim Kitsuragi. The lieutenant steps in. "I'm a detective from Precinct 57. I believe you have already met my colleague from Precinct 41."
Zh: “我是金·曷城。”警督上前一步。“我是57分局的一名警探。相信你一定已经见过我这位41分局的同事了吧。”

En: I'm Kim Kitsuragi. The lieutenant steps in. "I'm a detective from Precinct 57. I see you've already met my colleague."
Zh: “我是金·曷城。”警督上前一步。“我是57分局的一名警探。看来你已经见过我的同事了。”

En: Have I ever... This is the fabled Cop of the Apocalypse.
Zh: “我有没有……这就是传说中的末日警察。”

En: There are vortices of dark energy present, but not to Hell -- to another place. A *third* place, much different from our world.
Zh: “这里确实存在黑暗能量漩涡，不过不是通往地狱——而是另一个地方。*第三个*地方，跟我们的世界很不一样。”

En: It has.
Zh: “没错。”

En: They *also* say it's why the cleaning lady quit.
Zh: “他们*还说*这就是清洁女工辞职的原因。”

En: Disco Infernum.
Zh: “迪斯科地狱。”

En: Hell? You know not of which you speak!
Zh: “地狱？你根本不知道自己在说什么！”

En: No, I just trashed the place.
Zh: “不，我只是把这个地方搞得乱七八糟。”

En: A third place? Interesting. She takes a sip of coffee. "That's probably why the cleaning lady quit."
Zh: “第三个地方？有意思。”她抿了一口咖啡。“这也许就是清洁女工辞职的原因吧。”

En: I know. She smiles with her eyebrows. "That's *probably* also why the cleaning lady quit."
Zh: “我知道。”她的眉毛笑弯了腰。“这*可能*也是清洁女工辞职的原因。”

En: Oh, I *do* -- they also say it's why the cleaning lady quit.
Zh: “哦，我*当然*知道了——他们还说这就是清洁女工辞职的原因。”

En: She just admitted to being on familiar terms with Hell -- a rough life, certainly.
Zh: 她刚刚承认跟地狱之间的交情很好——生活不易啊，这是肯定的。

En: Nice? I don't know about that, officer... She looks north, toward the yard.
Zh: “很好？那我就不知道了，警官……”她看向北边，院子的方向。

En: She tries to look indifferent, but still has to take a drag of her cigarette -- so as not to flinch.
Zh: 她想表现出漠不关心的样子，不过还是要抽上一口——才能让自己不畏惧。

En: That must be her tell: the cigarette. It hurts her to look directly at the corpse so she reverts her gaze to you.
Zh: 这一定就是她说的：香烟。直接看着尸体对她而言是个伤害，所以她又把目光转回到你身上。

En: It's much nicer now. Her eyes wander north, toward the yard...
Zh: “现在好多了。”她看向北边，院子的方向。

En: Where the dead body used to hang, clearly visible from the roof. But no longer.
Zh: 那个曾经挂着尸体的地方，从屋顶可以一览无余。但是现在已经不在。

En: Thank you for that, officers. Truly.
Zh: “谢谢你，警官。真心的。”

En: This does not come as good news to him.
Zh: 这对他来说也不是什么好消息。

En: Not met *per se*, but I've seen him around, detective. Swooshing by. It has been quite a sight.
Zh: “我*本身*没有见过，不过在附近见过他，警探。嗖的一下就过去了。场面挺壮观的。”

En: Have I ever... This is the world's most lamentable cop.
Zh: “我曾经……这是世界上最不幸的警察。”

En: Have I ever... This man has an *unforgettable* way with words.
Zh: “我曾经……这个男人的言语表达方式特别*难忘*。”

En: Have I ever... She turns to you. "Have you grown accustomed to your role as a 'police officer'?"
Zh: “我曾经……”她转向你。“你已经习惯‘警察’的角色了吗？”

En: Once a cop -- always a cop.
Zh: “曾经当过警察——永远都是警察。”

En: I have *very little* idea of what I'm doing.
Zh: “我也*不知道*自己在做什么。”

En: That is unimportant to the case at hand.
Zh: “这对眼前这起案件不重要。”

En: OH YEAH, FEEL THE POWER.
Zh: 哦耶，感受力量吧。

En: I have grown accustomed to the *power*, miss.
Zh: “我已经习惯于这种*力量*了，小姐。”

En: I've found something more *downbeat* for my next appearance. (Whip out the tape of "Smallest Church")
Zh: “我为自己的下一次出场找到了更*悲惨*的东西。”（拿出“小教堂”的磁带）

En: How long, do you think, until the hard wears us down?
Zh: “你觉得，我们距离被困苦击垮还有多久呢？”

En: It's gotten pretty hard in the meantime -- but I go on.
Zh: “与此同时，情况变得更加艰难了——不过我还会继续。”

En: I can see that. She takes a sip. "It suits you."
Zh: “我能看出来。”她抿了一口咖啡。“很适合你。”

En: There is the tiniest bit of flattery in there -- the way her eyes move from your shoulder to shoulder.
Zh: 她的目光在你的肩膀上游移的方式——蕴含着一点点奉承的意味。

En: Like riding a 'copocycle.' She takes a sip of her coffee.
Zh: “就像骑在一辆‘警車’上。”她抿了一口咖啡。

En: Ah, I can see that you have, officer. She nods.
Zh: “啊，我能看出来你有的，警官。”她点点头。

En: You're doing just fine -- after all, your path has led you back to me.
Zh: “你做得挺好的——毕竟，你的道路指引你又回到了我这里。”

En: Her hair is still slick from the shower.
Zh: 她那头刚刚沐浴过的头发还有些湿滑。

En: Ah, I see. She takes a pensive drag of her menthol cigarette.
Zh: “啊，我明白了。”她沉思着抽了一口薄荷香烟。

En: Be careful. Ask something else first. When you go *there*, use words like 'I hear you have been through something difficult.'
Zh: 要小心。先问点别的。当你问到*那件事*的时候，用一些类似‘我听说你经历了一些困难’的话语。

En: No need to dilly-dally. She's tough.
Zh: 没必要磨磨蹭蹭的。她很坚强。

En: I'll go straight to it. I don't wanna sound like a namby-pamby.
Zh: 我就单刀直入了。我可不想表现地像个多愁善感的人。

En: Thanks Coach, but I think I'll *dilly dally* this time.
Zh: 谢了教练，不过我觉得这次我还是*磨蹭*一点的好。

En: YOU'LL SOUND LIKE A NAMBY-PAMBY!
Zh: 你会显得很多愁善感的！

En: Good on you, boy. She can take it.
Zh: 干得好，小子。她能承受的。

En: Coach Physical Instrument is overdoing it -- even by my standards. Say something else first.
Zh: 强身健体教练表现的有点过火——就算按我的标准来看也是如此。先说点别的吧。

En: You ain't seen nothing yet. There's a *karaoke* stage downstairs.
Zh: “你还什么都没看见呢。楼下有个*卡拉OK*舞台。”

En: Wow... She shakes her head in silent disbelief, breathing in the coffee smell.
Zh: “哇哦……”她怀疑地默默摇摇头，吸了一口咖啡的香气。

En: Johnny Law is about to tear it up. Sad-style.
Zh: “警察要开始花天酒地了。用悲伤的风格。”

En: That's the one! The Small Sad Church Song! I've missed it, I really have.
Zh: “就是这个！小教堂悲歌！我很想念它，真的。”

En: You have? Why?
Zh: “是吗？为什么？”

En: The Small Sad Church Song tells it like it is, detective -- *sad*.
Zh: “小教堂悲歌里就是这么唱的，警探——*悲伤*。”

En: I assure you, he is not about to tear it up in *any* style. In fact...
Zh: “我向你保证，他不会以*任何*方式花天酒地的。事实上……”

En: We'll show him. You'll tear it up ALL the styles!
Zh: 我们要让他看看。你要用所有方式花天酒地！

En: It's no mere turn of phrase. This young woman has a deeply rooted understanding of what *sad* means.
Zh: 这不仅仅是一种措辞。这个年轻女子对*悲伤*的含义有着根深蒂固的理解。

En: Things are only getting easier for me. I'm a police man: Too-doo-roo-doo! (Imitate a siren.)
Zh: “事情对我来说变得简单多了。我是一个警察：嘀嘀嘀-嘟嘟嘟！”（模仿警笛的声音。）

En: No comment.
Zh: “无可奉告。”

En: Actually, Johnny Law isn't going to tear it up, that would be unprofessional.
Zh: “其实，警察不会花天酒地，这样太不专业了。”

En: It would, wouldn't it? It would be *strange*. You see...
Zh: “的确，不是吗？这样会很*奇怪*。你明白的……”

En: No, it wouldn't. It would be thematic.
Zh: 不，不会的。它会成为主旋律。

En: That is not actually what a police siren sounds like. The lieutenant turns to the young woman.
Zh: “警笛的声音其实不是这样的。”警督转向年轻女子。

En: Oh, I've got a couple of good years left on my warranty... She looks down at her body shimmering in the silver jumpsuit.
Zh: “哦，我的保证书还有好几年呢……”她看着银色连体裤下闪闪发光的身体。

En: I can see that. She nods. "I'm glad all this *onward going* has brought you to me."
Zh: “我能看出来。”她点点头。“很高兴这些*进行中的调查*把你带到了我的身边。”

En: No. She reached the end of her elasticity about two months ago -- from here on out, every chip and knick and blow stays in there somewhere.
Zh: 不。她在两个月之前就达到了忍耐的极限——从现在起，她的一举一动都会停留在某个地方。

En: You must have me confused with someone else. I'm just a regular, down-to-earth law official.
Zh: “你一定是把我跟其他人弄混了。我只是个普通又现实的执法人员。”

En: I would never do anything interesting like that. I'm trying to become the world's most regular cop.
Zh: “我永远也不会做出什么有趣的事情。我要努力变成世界上最普通的警察。”

En: I would like to withdraw the comment I made earlier. Given the situation we find ourselves in now, it seems out of place.
Zh: “我想收回我之前说过的话。考虑到我们现在的处境，那些好像有些不合时宜。”

En: I stand by my words.
Zh: “我坚持自己说过的话。”

En: She tilts her head to the side and smiles. Vapour rises from her coffee cup.
Zh: 她把头歪向一旁，笑了。水蒸气从她手中的咖啡杯上升腾而起。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: “什么也不说。”

En: See? She turns to the lieutenant. "'Say nothing'. So strange. Who says that? What is going on here?"
Zh: “看见了吗？”她转向警督。“‘什么也不说’。真奇怪。谁说的？这里出什么事了？”

En: And what is that situation? She looks at the lieutenant.
Zh: “这又是怎么个情况？”她看着警督。

En: It's almost imperceptible in the cold wind, but the blood vessels on her cheeks and neck dilate.
Zh: 在寒风中几乎难以察觉，但是她脸颊和脖子上的血管扩张了。

En: I doubt it. She shakes her head. "You can't bury that kind of fire. What brought you back to me, Fire Man?"
Zh: “我表示怀疑。”她摇摇头。“那种火焰是隐藏不住的。是什么把你又带回了我身边，消防员？”

En: It's okay, it's absolutely okay to be that. She nods compassionately. "What is this about, officers?"
Zh: “没事的，这样完全没问题。”她同情地点点头。“有什么事吗，警官？”

En: I'm sorry. I'm the Sorry Cop.
Zh: “很抱歉。我是抱歉警探。”

En: No. That's not me anymore. I am a Superstar Cop now.
Zh: “不。那已经不再是我了。现在我是明星警探。”

En: No. That's not me anymore. I am the Cop of the Apocalypse now.
Zh: “不。那已经不再是我了。现在我是末日警探。”

En: That's not me anymore. I am just a regular cop now. Regular law official.
Zh: “那已经不再是我了。现在我是一个普通的警察。普通的执法人员。”

En: Those days are behind me. I am here to ask questions.
Zh: “那些日子已经离我远去。我来是为了问问题的。”

En: Do *not* go along with this.
Zh: *不要*赞同这一点。

En: An unnecessary development, if you ask me. She shakes her head. "What brings this supposedly *regular* law official back to me?"
Zh: “如果问我的话，这是个不必要的发展。”她摇摇头。“那是什么把这个所谓的*普通*执法人员带回我身边的呢？”

En: I stand corrected. What brings you back to me -- superstardom?
Zh: “我错了。是什么把你带回到我身边的——超级巨星吗？”

En: I stand corrected. What brings you back to me -- the coming apocalypse?
Zh: “我错了。是什么把你带回到我身边的——即将降临的末日吗？”

En: Questions? What about?
Zh: “问题？什么问题？”

En: Hear that, Kim? I am a *huge* deal around here.
Zh: “听见了吗，金？我是这附近的*大*人物。”

En: I am sad to say I have shifted *copotype* since we last saw each other. I am a different cop now.
Zh: “很遗憾，自从我们上次见面以来，我已经改变了*警察类型*。现在我是个不同的警察了。”

En: Martinaise is very lucky. Only one in a *thousand* cops wakes up as the Superstar Cop.
Zh: “马丁内斯很幸运。*一千个*警察中间只会出现一个明星警探。”

En: Of course there are. Look at him... She looks at you, then turns back to the lieutenant. "He is The Phoenix. What brings The Phoenix back to me?"
Zh: “当然是了。你看看他……”她看着你，然后看着警督。“他是凤凰。是什么把凤凰带回我身边的？”

En: Wow... Vapour rises from the coffee cup in her hand.
Zh: “哇哦……”水蒸气从她手中的咖啡杯上升腾而起。

En: Wow. She turns to the lieutenant. "What brings *The Phoenix* back to me?"
Zh: “哇哦……”她转向警督。“是什么把*凤凰*带回我身边的？”

En: Miss, I don't know what he's told you, but there are no superstars among police officers.
Zh: “小姐，我不知道他跟你说过什么，不过警察里面没有什么超级明星。”

En: Yes. *Wow*. (Lean in.) "I am now a herald of the Planetary Slaughterhouse. The Abattoir To Come."
Zh: “是的。*哇哦*。”（靠近。）“现在我是行星屠宰场的传令官。即将降临的屠宰场。”

En: No. Not wow. (Lean in.) "I am the most boring cop in the world now. Nothing like *that* will ever happen again."
Zh: “不是。不是哇。”（靠近。）“现在我是这个世界上最无聊的警察。*那种*事情永远也不会发生了。”

En: Things are very far from *wow*, little miss. (Lean in.) "I am a changed man, a cop reborn to *repent* my shortcomings -- I am the Sorry Cop."
Zh: “事情离*哇哦*还离得很远呢，小姐。”（靠近。）“我已经变了，一个重生的警察，为自己的缺点而*忏悔*——我是抱歉警探。”

En: This is part of the... khm... *thing* I told you about.
Zh: “这是……呃……我告诉过你的*那些事*的一部分。”

En: Yes, I understood that, officer. He turns to the young woman.
Zh: “没错，我能理解，警官。”他转向那个年轻女子。

En: Kim, I swear I have no idea what she's talking about.
Zh: “金，我发誓不知道她在说什么。”

En: The Phoenix is one of the many nicknames of Guillaume le Million, Revachol's second greatest (male) disco artist. This is an honour.
Zh: 凤凰是纪尧姆·列米利翁众多昵称中的一个，他是瑞瓦肖第二伟大的（男性）迪斯科舞者。这是一种荣耀。

En: A penitent cop-monk... she says to herself. "What brings this *changed man* to me?"
Zh: “一个忏悔的僧侣警探……”她自言自语。“是什么把这个*改变的男人*带到我身边的呢？”

En: I very much doubt that. What brings this *so-called* boring cop back to me?
Zh: “我很怀疑。是什么把这个*所谓的*无聊警探带到我身边的呢？”

En: Wow indeed... And what brings this herald back to me?
Zh: “确实让人惊叹……那是什么把这个传令官带回到我身边的呢？”

En: I'm disappointed it's dragging out like this. What a farce... (Look around.)
Zh: “拖了这么久，让我真的很失望。真是一场闹剧……”（看着周围。）

En: (With contempt written all over your face.) "Fucking *end* already."
Zh: （满脸写满了轻视。）“他妈的已经*结束*了。”

En: New information has come to light -- it is a matter of *weeks* now. A great reckoning of the classes. Revachol explodes in vigilante violence. It's foretold.
Zh: “新的信息已经浮出水面——现在只是几个*星期*的问题了。一次伟大的阶级审判。瑞瓦肖会在义务警员的暴力行径中爆发。这是我的预言。”

En: Know that I am no longer the Cop of the Apocalypse. The prophecy was wrong. The signs meant nothing. The hangover has passed.
Zh: “要知道，我不再是末日警探了。预言是错误的。那些符号没有任何意义。宿醉已经过去了。”

En: Oh. She pauses. "Well, you know... I'm sad to hear that."
Zh: “哦。”她停顿了一下。“呃，你知道吗……听到这个我很难过。”

En: Wow, there are *copotypes*... Vapour rises from the coffee cup in her hand.
Zh: “哇哦，还有*警察类型*……”水蒸气从她手中的咖啡杯上升腾而起。

En: I can assure you -- there really, really are *not*.
Zh: “我可以向你保证——真的，绝对*没有*。”

En: Yeah, man. Her face wrinkles into a rock 'n' roll smile.
Zh: “是啊，老兄。”她的脸上露出摇滚式的笑容。

En: I know, she says with great melancholy. "What now?"
Zh: “我知道，”她带着极大的忧虑说到。“现在怎么样？”

En: What is there to do now -- stranded here? The question is personal to her. More than humour.
Zh: 现在要做什么——困在这里？这个问题对她来说很私密。远不止幽默这么简单。

En: Wow... Vapour rises from her coffee cup. "Can it be stopped? And if not, what are we to do with the time left?"
Zh: “哇哦……”水蒸气从她手中的咖啡杯上升腾而起。“那能停止吗？如果不能，剩下来的时间我们能做些什么？”

En: I am sure it has. In the time we've spent together, I must have covered 30 kilometres. He has a *track and field* approach to police work.
Zh: “我肯定是这样的。在我们一起度过的时间里，我肯定已经走了30公里。他对警察工作有一套*田径*的方法。”

En: Isn't that odd? How *can* you run so much?
Zh: 难道不觉得奇怪吗？你怎么*能*跑这么久？

En: Why not? You're a powerhouse.
Zh: 为什么不可以呢？你就是一个精力充沛的人。

En: And what brings this track-and-field god all the way up here?
Zh: “那是什么把田径之神一路带到这里的呢？”

En: Actually, yeah... How? You have mediocre lungs.
Zh: 事实上，是啊……怎么做到的？你的肺平平无奇。

En: Indeed. You don't strike yourself like a powerhouse at all.
Zh: 确实。你完全不觉得自己像个精力充沛的人。

En: It's about DEDICATION, son. That and you're *probably* a gym teacher. Only a former gym teacher can think these thoughts and drink so much.
Zh: 这跟奉献有关，孩子。你*可能*是一个体育老师。只有当过体育老师的人会产生这些想法，还能喝这么多酒。

En: Can I ask more about you? For the record.
Zh: “我能再问点你的事吗？为了备案。”

En: What is your name, miss? For the record.
Zh: “你叫什么名字，小姐？我们要备案。”

En: What is this wild flower? (Show her the flower.)
Zh: “这是什么野花？”（把花给她看。）

En: Wait what? I'm a what?
Zh: 等什么？我是什么？

En: Gym teacher?
Zh: 体育老师？

En: Doesn't matter! The flow of events is already passing you by! The woman said something, now the man's saying something... KEEP YOUR HEAD IN THE GAME, SON!
Zh: 没关系！这件事情已经过去了！女人说了些什么，现在男人在说什么……要专心一点，孩子！

En: Then how am I able to do it...
Zh: 我是怎么做到的……

En: Doesn't matter.
Zh: 没关系。

En: Doesn't matter! The flow of events is passing you by! The woman said something, now the man's saying something... KEEP YOUR HEAD IN THE GAME, SON!
Zh: 没关系！这件事情已经过去了！女人说了些什么，现在男人在说什么……要专心一点，孩子！

En: Officer. She nods to you in greeting. "It's a fine day for questions."
Zh: “警官。”她点头向你示意。“今天是个问问题的好日子。”

En: The woman doesn't flinch, but there is something in her light brown eyes... a sadness.
Zh: 女人没有退缩，但是她的浅棕色眼睛里有些什么……是一种悲伤。

En: The woman doesn't flinch, but there is something in her eyes -- the same sadness? Yes, from when she first told you about the hanging, in the hallway.
Zh: 女人没有退缩，但是她的眼睛里有些什么……相同的悲伤？是的，还是她告诉你上吊男人的时候，没错，是在走廊里。

En: She nods approvingly. "They also say that's why the cleaning lady quit. Because of the *Infernum*."
Zh: 她赞同地点点头。“他们还说这就是清洁女工辞职的原因。因为*地狱*。”

En: I doubt you will succeed. She shakes her head. "So, what is this about, officers?"
Zh: “我怀疑你能不能成功。”她摇摇头。“所以，找我有什么事呢，警官？”

En: They tell us you've been through something difficult.
Zh: “他们说你经历了一些很艰难的事情。”

En: x
Zh: x

En: They tell us you were raped.
Zh: “他们说你被强暴了。”

En: Right. She turns to face the wind, letting it dry her hair.
Zh: “没错。”她转身迎着风，让风吹干她的头发。

En: Let's return to this later. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们稍后再继续。”[离开。]

En: Let's return to this later, miss. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们稍后再继续，小姐。”[离开。]

En: Why not? I'll be here until 23.00, drinking coffee most likely...
Zh: “为什么不呢？我会在这里待到23点，很可能是在喝咖啡……”

En: Klaasje Amandou.
Zh: “卡拉洁·阿曼多。”

En: Are you from Oranje?
Zh: “你是奥兰治来的吗？”

En: And where are you from?
Zh: “你是从哪里来的？”

En: Right sir. Vredefort, Republic of Oranje. I guess you could say I am an Oranjese *expatriate*.
Zh: “是的，长官。奥兰治共和国，弗里德堡。我想你可以说我是一个奥兰治*侨民*。”

En: Vredefort, Republic of Oranje.
Zh: “奥兰治共和国，弗里德堡。”

En: Wasn't the tattoo on the hanged man an *Oranjese* Map of the Waterways? The thought begins to form in your head, then dissipates -- something for later.
Zh: 那个上吊男人身上不是有*奥兰治*水路地图的纹身吗？一个想法慢慢在你脑海中形成，然后又消散了——这件事之后再说。

En: What is Oranje?
Zh: “奥兰治是什么？”

En: What do you do, miss? What is your *specialization*?
Zh: “你是做什么的，小姐？你有什么*特长*？”

En: Could we take a look at your passport, please?
Zh: “能让我们看看你的护照吗？”

En: Could I see your passport, please?
Zh: “请问能看看你的护照吗？”

En: Same name that Titus gave you. Like her accent, it sounds Oranjese.
Zh: 跟提图斯说的名字相同。就像她的口音，听起来就像奥兰治人。

En: Same name that Titus gave you. It sounds Oranjese -- as does her accent.
Zh: 跟提图斯说的名字相同。听起来很像奥兰治人——她的口音也一样。

En: Her birthmarks also signal Oranje. You don't know why, but Oranjese girls tend to come speckled with them.
Zh: 她的胎记也是奥兰治的标志。你不知道为什么，但是奥兰治女孩的身上往往会有斑点。

En: The victim's ethnicity *could* have been Oranjese... -- something for later?
Zh: 被害人的种族也*可能*是奥兰治……——也许之后再深究？

En: A bad memory, officer.
Zh: “一段糟糕的记忆，警官。”

En: A bad memory of what?
Zh: “什么糟糕的记忆？”

En: Understood, miss.
Zh: “明白，小姐。”

En: Of lilacs and lightning. She squints her eyes as if to see them in the distance. "Parks, glass, duraluminium -- Vredefort is a conference city. It's always autumn there. And night. At least it was for me."
Zh: “丁香花和闪电。”她眯起眼睛，好像在远处看到了它们。“公园，玻璃，杜拉铝——弗里德堡是一个会议很多的城市。那里一直是秋天。还有夜晚。至少对于我来说是这样的。”

En: The Republic of Oranje is a democratic nation on the Mundi isola, north of here, over the sea and across the pale. It is one of the six major Occidental nations to hold stake in the continued occupation of Revachol (the Coalition). People consider it a *reasonable* superpower.
Zh: 奥兰治共和国是蒙迪洲上的一个民主国家，就在这里的北部，跨过海洋，穿越灰域。它是六大奥西登国家之一，保持着对瑞瓦肖的占有权（联盟国）。大家都觉得它是一个*通情达理*的超级大国。

En: Reasonable?
Zh: 通情达理？

En: They will make you into a fiscal colony, divert your natural resources, hold patents and shares, but they won't threaten to *wipe you out any time* like Revachol did in its prime.
Zh: 他们会让你变成一个财政殖民地，转移你的自然资源，持有专利和股份，但是不会像全盛时期的瑞瓦肖一样，威胁着*随时可以把你灭掉*。

En: And what's so bad about that?
Zh: “那有什么不好的？”

En: Nothing -- if you're no longer there.
Zh: “没什么——如果你不在那里的话。”

En: The conference centres are filled with people who get angry at you if you do something *wrong*.
Zh: 会议中心里面全是会因为你的*错误*而生气的人。

En: It's the past. She takes a sip of her coffee. "People can't go back to the past."
Zh: “那是过去。”她抿了一口咖啡。“谁都回不到过去。”

En: How old are you?
Zh: “你多大？”

En: I'm 28. She takes a drag.
Zh: “28岁。”她抽了一口烟。

En: Did you do something wrong?
Zh: “你做了什么错事吗？”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Sooner or later, everyone does.
Zh: “迟早，大家都会做错事。”

En: Thankfully -- it's implied.
Zh: 谢天谢地——这是在暗示。

En: Something stupid.
Zh: “一些蠢事。”

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: What's so bad about that?
Zh: “那有什么不好的？”

En: Wanna hear what's stupid? Somewhere in a one room apartment on Boogie Street a young man shows Patrol Officer Tillbrook his genital warts, asking if they're *cancer*.
Zh: 想听是什么蠢事？在布吉街某处的一间公寓里，一个年轻男人向巡警提尔布鲁克展示着自己的生殖器疣，询问它们是不是*癌症*。

En: You're a cop?
Zh: “你是个警察？”

En: Oranjese lit.
Zh: “奥兰治文学。”

En: Oranjese lit?
Zh: “奥兰治文学？”

En: Oranjese literature. It's what I studied *at the university*. She raises both eyebrows.
Zh: “奥兰治文学。我在*大学*研究的就是这个。”她扬起双眉。

En: What is Oranjese literature about?
Zh: “奥兰治文学是讲什么的？”

En: Fear of failure, fear of death. How it *sucks* to be Oranjese. All national literatures are -- only the name of the nation changes.
Zh: “害怕失败，害怕死亡。做奥兰治人有多么*糟糕*。所有国家的文学作品都是这样——只是换了个国家名称。”

En: Revacholian literature too?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖文学也是吗？”

En: No! She seems glad that you asked. "Revacholian lit is about how *magnificent and serious* Revachol is. It's about how you have to save the world."
Zh: “不是！”她好像很高兴你能这么问。“瑞瓦肖文学说的都是瑞瓦肖有多么的*壮丽和庄重*。讲的都是你该如何拯救这个世界。”

En: No-no. *More* stupid -- Oranjese lit.
Zh: “不是的。*更加*愚蠢一些——奥兰治文学。”

En: His partner Émile Mollins can't be there -- he's in another apartment with another man who's showing him a dead dog under the radiator. 'It's dead,' Mollins says. "No," the man replies. "I touched him. He's warm."
Zh: 他的搭档埃米尔·莫林有事来不了了……他在另一间公寓里，另一个男人在给他看暖气片下面的一条死狗。“那玩意死了。”莫林说道。“不，”男人回应，“我摸过他了。他还有体温。”“他之所以是温热的，”莫林答道，“是因为他就在暖气片下面。”

En: He's warm, Mollins replies, "because he's under the radiator."
Zh: ‘它是热的，’莫林回复到，‘是因为它在散热器下面。’

En: I have no intention of doing that.
Zh: “我没有那样做的打算。”

En: I do feel the urge to save the world, yes. I guess that's why I'm on this case.
Zh: “我确实感觉到拯救世界的冲动，没错。我猜这就是我调查这起案件的原因。”

En: The world is unimportant -- I want to hear about you.
Zh: “这个世界不重要——我想听听你的事。”

En: That's just a phase the Revacholian hero goes through... She breathes in the menthol-flavoured fumes, savouring it...
Zh: “这只是瑞瓦肖英雄经历的一个阶段……”她吸了一口薄荷味的香烟，细细品味……

En: That's the natural state of the Revacholian hero... She breathes in the menthol flavoured fumes, savouring it -- then breathes out.
Zh: “那是瑞瓦肖英雄的自然状态……”她吸了一口薄荷味的香烟，细细品味——然后吐了出来。

En: Strange, usually the Revacholian hero does not save women... She breathes in the menthol flavoured fumes, savouring it...
Zh: “奇怪，一般瑞瓦肖英雄不会拯救女人的……”她吸了一口薄荷味的香烟，细细品味……

En: Back to you.
Zh: “说回你吧。”

En: Back to me. She breathes in the menthol-flavoured fumes, savouring it in her lungs.
Zh: “说回我。”她吸了一口薄荷味的香烟，在肺里细细品味。

En: How do you make money then? Money is very important. (Show her some money.)
Zh: “那你怎么赚钱呢？钱很重要的。”（拿点钱给她看。）

En: Nothing. I do nothing with it.
Zh: “没什么。我什么也不做。”

En: ...then breathes out. "Before they plunge into full-core humanity- and world-saving."
Zh: ……然后吐了出来。“在他们全身心投入到拯救人类和拯救世界的使命之前。”

En: ...then breathes out: "It's a frivolous pursuit. He saves all of mankind instead."
Zh: ……然后吐了出来。“这是一种轻佻的追求。他转而拯救了全人类。”

En: Oranjese lit -- what do you *do* with it?
Zh: “奥兰治文学——你拿它*做什么*呢？”

En: Cool. She looks at the black note in your hand. "I've made more money by just *being* than I have with Oranjese lit."
Zh: “很酷。”她看着你手上的黑色纸币。“做*我本人*都比奥兰治文学要赚的多。”

En: Cool.
Zh: “挺酷的。”

En: Sure. She takes a drag.
Zh: “当然。”她抽了一口烟。

En: What about Revacholian literature? People sometimes reveal things about themselves when they discuss such matters...
Zh: 瑞瓦肖文学呢？人们在讨论一些事情的时候，有时候会暴露出自己的一些东西……

En: It's not very cool, but what can you do?
Zh: “没什么酷的，但是你又能做什么呢？”

En: How do you make money then? Money is very important -- everyone tells me that.
Zh: “那你怎么赚钱呢？钱很重要的——大家都这么跟我说。”

En: Cool. She looks at the white cents in your hand. "I've made more money by just *being* than I have with Oranjese lit."
Zh: “很酷。”她看着你手里的白色硬币。“做*我本人*都比奥兰治文学要赚的多。”

En: Oranjese lit certainly hasn't helped. I've made more money by just *being* than I have with Oranjese lit.
Zh: “奥兰治文学确实没帮多少忙。做*我本人*都比奥兰治文学要赚的多。”

En: Being what?
Zh: “那你本人是什么？”

En: Many things. A roller skating instructor, a secretary, Miss Oranje '37. She smiles.
Zh: “很多身份。轮滑教练，秘书，37年的奥兰治小姐。”她笑了。

En: It's the world's most tired smile.
Zh: 这是世界上最疲惫的笑容。

En: What is Miss Oranje '37?
Zh: “37年的奥兰治小姐是什么？”

En: What is a beauty pageant?
Zh: “选美比赛是什么？”

En: Let's move on. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我们继续吧。”（总结。）

En: Understood. (Conclude.)
Zh: “明白了。”（总结。）

En: You were 14?
Zh: “那时你14岁？”

En: It's a beauty pageant. Held in the year of our century '37 -- in Oranje.
Zh: “是一个选美比赛。本世纪37年举办的——在奥兰治。”

En: It's a competition. She takes a drag. "For stupid girls. Men look at them and choose the most beautiful."
Zh: “是一个比赛。”她抽了一口烟。“专门为蠢女孩准备的。男人看着她们，然后选出里面最漂亮的那一个。”

En: She was 14 at the time.
Zh: 那时候她14岁。

En: What is beauty?
Zh: “什么是漂亮？”

En: When you have the fewest deviations from the norm. I've been told it's a type of mediocrity that I possess.
Zh: “当你跟标准之间的偏差最小的时候就是漂亮。别人告诉我说，这是我拥有的一种平庸之才。”

En: So you were the most beautiful?
Zh: “所以你是最漂亮的那一个了？”

En: So you were stupid and mediocre?
Zh: “所以你很蠢，还很平庸？”

En: No. She cranes her neck. "I was the *most* stupid and mediocre. And I got a crown and a sash -- and a sceptre -- for it. And a scholarship to study Oranjese lit."
Zh: “不。”她伸长脖子。“我是*最*蠢，最平庸的那个。我得到了一顶王冠和一条饰带——还有一根权杖。还有学习奥兰治文学的奖学金。”

En: Yes. And for that I got a crown and a sash -- and a sceptre. And a scholarship to study Oranjese lit.
Zh: “是的。因为这样，我得到了一顶王冠和一条饰带——还有一根权杖。还有学习奥兰治文学的奖学金。”

En: Indeed.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Her voice is indifferent to the number.
Zh: 她的声音对于数字漠不关心。

En: I don't think I've ever been 14.
Zh: “我觉得14岁的我根本不存在。”

En: Cool. (Raise your thumb.)
Zh: “厉害。”（竖起大拇指。）

En: (Lower your thumb.) "I disapprove of this custom."
Zh: （放下拇指。）“我不赞成这种习俗。”

En: I don't think I've ever heard anything less relevant to any investigation.
Zh: “我觉得我没有听过任何跟调查无关的事。”

En: Supercool. She raises her coffee cup as though making a toast. "Full disclosure -- I was Miss *Teen* Oranje '37. The pageant was discontinued recently."
Zh: “超级酷的。”她举起咖啡杯，像是在祝酒。“大曝光——我是37年奥兰治的*妙龄*小姐。选美比赛最近中断了。”

En: It was okay. She shrugs. "And it was called Miss *Teen* Oranje '37, actually. You'll be glad to hear the pageant was discontinued."
Zh: “没事的。”她耸耸肩。“其实是叫37年奥兰治*妙龄*小姐。选美比赛已经中断了，听到这个消息你一定很高兴吧。”

En: Mhm. She gestures toward the table. "I would offer you coffee, but it's garbage -- literally tastes like poison." She takes a sip.
Zh: “嗯。”她朝着桌子比了个手势。“我想给你冲杯咖啡，不过它实在是太垃圾——基本上尝起来就是毒药的味道。”她抿了一口。

En: I'm afraid you can't, officer.
Zh: “恐怕不行，警官。”

En: C'mon, detective, cut him some slack. These may be the final hours of human existence.
Zh: “拜托，警探，放他一马吧。这可能是人类存在的最后时刻了。”

En: C'mon, detective, cut him some slack. This guy's been through some heavy-duty emotional stuff.
Zh: “拜托，警探，放他一马吧。这家伙经历了一些很沉重的感情负担。”

En: C'mon, detective, he's the most repentant law official in the land. I don't mind.
Zh: “拜托，警探，他是这片土地上最追悔莫及的执法人员了。我不介意的。”

En: It's okay, he's just getting his bearings. The world of beauty pageants *is* bewildering.
Zh: “没事的，他只是在找方向。选美比赛的世界*的确*很让人困惑的。”

En: It's okay, I don't mind. The world of beauty pageants is absurd and bewildering.
Zh: “没事的，我不介意。选美比赛的世界本身就很荒谬，很让人困惑。”

En: It's okay. This is what superstar law officials do. I'm getting used to it.
Zh: “没事的。这是明星执法人员会做的事。我已经习惯了。”

En: It's okay. This is just him being a scintillating wordsmith. I'm used to it.
Zh: “没事的。毕竟他是个才气横溢的作家。我已经习惯了。”

En: Neither do I, she says calmly. "I think it's *alcohol* that makes you feel like that. It must have something to do with partying."
Zh: “我也没有，”她平静地说到。“我觉得是*酒精*让你产生了这种感觉。肯定跟派对有所关联。”

En: Another innocent tradition bulldozed by the march of ethical *progress*!
Zh: 又一个无辜的传统被道德*发展*前进的步伐给消除了！

En: The march of ethical progress continues.
Zh: 道德发展前进的步伐还在继续。

En: Thank you. That's it -- for the record.
Zh: “谢谢你。就这些了——只是为了备案而已。”

En: Why is that?
Zh: “为什么不行？”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Thank you for your understanding. She looks a little surprised.
Zh: “感谢你的理解。”她看起来有些吃惊。

En: 'The record' -- so official!
Zh: “‘备案’——真的很官方！”

En: Because it's buried in a sealed plastic bag at an undisclosed location on the coast. Along with cash and airline tickets.
Zh: “因为它被埋在一个密封的塑料袋里，就藏在海岸边一个隐蔽的地方。里面还有现金和机票。”

En: Thank you for your candour -- why?
Zh: “感谢你能这么直率——为什么呢？”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: They say so in your tourist brochure -- keep travel documents away from your person when west of the river.
Zh: “你们的旅游手册上是这么说的——来到河流西边的时候，不要把旅游证件放在自己身边。”

En: I'm pretty sure I have the legal right to demand your passport, miss.
Zh: “我很肯定我有权要求你出示护照，小姐。”

En: How do I know you've told us your real name?
Zh: “我怎么知道你告诉我们的是不是真名？”

En: Okay then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “那好吧。”（总结。）

En: Look officers, she turns to face you both, "I like this place, but I don't want to be stuck *wandering* the city like a ghost after being robbed of my travel documents."
Zh: “是这样的，警官，”她转而面向你们，“我喜欢这个地方，但我不想自己的旅游证件被洗劫一空之后，像个幽灵一样困在这个城市里*游荡*。”

En: Officer, I like this place, but I don't want to be stuck *wandering* the city like a ghost after being robbed of my travel documents.
Zh: “警官，我喜欢这个地方，但我不想自己的旅游证件被洗劫一空之后，像个幽灵一样困在这个城市里*游荡*。”

En: I don't want to become an indentured servant in a brothel on Boogie Street -- and I don't want my relatives to pay the ransom.
Zh: “我不想成为布吉街妓院里的契约奴仆——而且我也不希望我的亲戚来支付赎金。”

En: There are plenty of other reasons for hiding your documents from the law.
Zh: 在执法人员面前隐藏自己的证件还有很多别的原因。

En: That's not why you hid it, though.
Zh: “不过这不是你把它藏起来的原因。”

En: It's *also* why I hid it.
Zh: “这*也是*我把它藏起来的原因。”

En: Why else? He's suddenly very attentive.
Zh: “还有什么别的理由？”他突然变得专心起来。

En: Why else?
Zh: “还有什么别的理由？”

En: I'm an unpopular girl. She takes a drag. "There are people back home who don't like me -- if they show up, I'm in a *hurry*."
Zh: “我是个不受欢迎的姑娘。”她抽了一口烟。“我的家乡有些不喜欢我的人——如果他们出现，我就变得*很匆忙*。”

En: The kind of hurry where you can't afford to *not* find your documents -- but don't worry. This has *nothing* to do with your investigation.
Zh: “那种你承受不起*找不到*证件的匆忙——但是别担心。这跟你的调查没有*任何关系*。”

En: The people who don't like you -- you mean the ones you're running from, right?
Zh: “那些不喜欢你的人——你的意思是说你正在逃避的人，对吗？”

En: What kind of *people* are these -- the ones that don't like you?
Zh: “那些是什么*人*——那些不喜欢你的人？”

En: I would *love* to change the subject. It's not important. Unless I'm some kind of... suspect? She arches an eyebrow.
Zh: “我很*乐意*换个话题。这不重要。除非我有些……嫌疑？”她弯起眉毛。

En: Earlier you said I would have to *interrogate* you. Suspects are interrogated.
Zh: “之前你说我可能会*审问*你。只有嫌疑人才会被审问。”

En: Not yet.
Zh: “还没有。”

En: No. We've just come to talk.
Zh: “不。我们只是来聊聊的。”

En: No. I've just come to talk.
Zh: “不。我只是来聊聊的。”

En: Didn't she say you would have to *interrogate* her? Before? And that she *didn't do it*? What was up with that?
Zh: 她不是说你可能需要*审问*她吗？之前说过？说她*没有做过*？这是怎么回事？

En: Well-well. She leans back. "It seems I either underestimated you -- or overestimated your hangover, officer."
Zh: “好吧。”她向后靠了靠。“看来我有些低估你了——或者说低估了你的宿醉，警官。”

En: You might be.
Zh: “也许是吧。”

En: If I were to lie to you, I would come up with a more *mainstream* name than *Klaasje Amandou*. It's... a weird name.
Zh: “如果我要对你撒谎，我会想出一个比*卡拉洁·阿曼多*更*主流*一些的名字。这可是个……奇怪的名字。”

En: She seems to be telling the truth, sire.
Zh: 她好像说的是实话，大人。

En: If I didn't know better, I'd say you feigned that 'fuck-having' line to get me to say something stupid. Was that so?
Zh: “如果我不知道的话，我会说你捏造了那些‘该死的’说辞来诱骗我说出一些蠢话。是不是这么回事呢？”

En: Okie-dokie. She pours herself more coffee.
Zh: “好的好的。”她给自己又倒了一些咖啡。

En: If any of this made her nervous, it certainly doesn't show in her expression or her movements.
Zh: 如果这些让她感到紧张，肯定没有在她的表情或者动作上表现出来。

En: Totally.
Zh: “完全就是。”

En: Turns out it did suck -- for *you*.
Zh: “结果它确实糟糕透顶——对于*你*来说。”

En: No, that was *sincere*.
Zh: “不，那是*真诚的*。”

En: I'm flattered. You told us about the investigation Friday night. Downstairs. At a table full of local kids and dockworkers. You told pretty much everyone. After you showed them your gun...
Zh: “我受宠若惊。周五晚上你给我们说了调查的事。就在楼下。在一张坐满本地小孩和码头工人的桌子前面。你基本上跟所有人都说过了。就在你把枪拿给他们看之后……”

En: Cool. You told us about the investigation Friday night. Downstairs. At a table full of local kids and dockworkers. You told pretty much everyone. After you showed them your gun, that is...
Zh: “好吧。周五晚上你给我们说了调查的事。就在楼下。在一张坐满本地小孩和码头工人的桌子前面。你基本上跟所有人都说过了。就在你把枪拿给他们看之后……”

En: Not too hard. You told us about the investigation Friday night. Downstairs. At a table full of local kids and dockworkers. You told pretty much everyone. After you showed them your gun...
Zh: “还不赖。周五晚上你给我们说了调查的事。就在楼下。在一张坐满本地小孩和码头工人的桌子前面。你基本上跟所有人都说过了。就在你把枪拿给他们看之后……”

En: Whooh... She wipes her brow.
Zh: “呼……”她揉了揉眉毛。

En: No, I'm not. She waves you off.
Zh: “不，我没有。”她挥手让你离开。

En: She doesn't seem to be overly distressed. Or distressed at all, really.
Zh: 她不像是过度悲伤的样子。或者说一点也不悲伤，真的。

En: Whooh... She wipes her brow.
Zh: “呼……”她揉了揉眉毛。

En: My colleague has an *exploratory* sense of humour. Don't hold it against him.
Zh: “我的同事有一种*探索性*的幽默感。别跟他过不去。”

En: He's thinking: "I like this kind of prodding, whatever the situation. It may lead somewhere yet..."
Zh: 他在想：“我喜欢这种刺激，不管眼下是什么情况。可能会探出什么东西……”

En: Playfully. She doesn't seem to be overly distressed. Or distressed at all, really.
Zh: 开玩笑。她不像是过度悲伤的样子。或者说一点也不悲伤，真的。

En: What are you doing here -- in the Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: “你在这里做什么——在褴褛飞旋？”

En: (Look around.) "Hate to say it, but you've got *Sad* all over the place."
Zh: （环顾四周。）“我不想这么说，但是你的*悲伤*几乎无处不在。”

En: Where does that door lead to? (Point to the door on the roof.)
Zh: “这扇门是通向什么地方的？”（指着屋顶上的门。）

En: That window is new.
Zh: “窗户是新的。”

En: I have other questions for you.
Zh: “我还有其他问题要问你。”

En: Yeaaah... She draws out the vowel. "I've contaminated it pretty bad."
Zh: “是啊……”她拖长了声音。“我把它污染地挺严重的。”

En: Is that why you're out here?
Zh: “这就是你来这里的原因？”

En: She nods. "The Contamination spreads from room to room. First I escaped upstairs. The Sad got that too. Then I found the handle for the summer door..."
Zh: 她点点头。“污染从一个房间扩散到另一个房间。一开始我往楼上逃。但是悲伤也追了上来。然后我发现盛夏之门的把手……”

En: What if you contaminate the *this* too? (Spread your hands.)
Zh: “如果你也把*这个*污染了呢？”（摊开你的双手。）

En: The World? She looks around.
Zh: “这个世界？”她看了看周围。

En: What exactly is the nature of this 'Contamination'?
Zh: “这个‘污染’的本质到底是什么？”

En: A squall of seagulls under the low-hanging sky. Distant machinery from the harbour. It's wide open and cold.
Zh: 低垂的天空下，一群海鸥在嚎叫。远处的港口堆满机械。这里开阔又冰冷。

En: It's hard to contaminate such a wide open space -- even for me. At least I hope so. If I do... She smiles a quivering smile. "Where will any of us go?"
Zh: “要污染这种广阔的空间是很难的——就算对于我来说也是这样。至少我希望如此。如果我真的污染了……”她露出颤抖的笑容。“我们还能去哪里？”

En: For me it's a mix of *me* with a lack of cleaning services. How about you?
Zh: “对于我来说，这就是*我*和缺乏清洁服务的混合。你呢？”

En: Talk around the establishment is you have an industrial Sad-spill in there. She taps the roof with her heel.
Zh: “关于公司有些传闻，说那里发生了工业污染。”她用高跟鞋拍打着屋顶。

En: Somewhere below, military-grade Sad is dripping off the walls... you should say the first thing. It's honest. It will lead to introspection.
Zh: 下面的某个地方，军事级别的悲伤从墙上滴落下来……你应该说首先要做的事。这样比较诚实。它能引导你反省自我。

En: Something is bad, point to your head, "in my head. In the past."
Zh: “有些很不好的东西，”指着你的脑袋，“在我的脑子里。跟过去有关。”

En: I hate objects. I am part of the Anti-Object Task Force.
Zh: “我恨物体。我是反物体特遣部队的一份子。”

En: It's a demon who smells of betrayal. And apricots.
Zh: “它是一个能嗅到背叛味道的恶魔。还有杏子。”

En: For me it's alcoholism, miss.
Zh: “对于我来说，它是酒精中毒，小姐。”

En: That's where it always comes from, isn't it? From the head -- and from the past.
Zh: “它总是这么来的，不是吗？来自你的脑海——还有过去。”

En: I love apricot chewing gum!
Zh: “我喜欢杏子味的口香糖！”

En: So do I.
Zh: “我也是。”

En: I *hate* apricot chewing gum. It betrayed me.
Zh: “我*痛恨*杏子味的口香糖。它背叛了我。”

En: That's pretty hard core, she nods. "I'm part of the Anti-Human Task Force myself."
Zh: “那还挺硬核的，”她点点头。“我自己是反人类前特遣小队的一份子。”

En: It's a feeling. Acidic and crawling with doom. Gaseous. Invisible. Deadly. It's everywhere.
Zh: “它是一种感觉。酸性的，充满厄运。它是气态的。无形的。致命的。它无处不在。”

En: (Nod.) "Anyway."
Zh: （点点头。）“无所谓了。”

En: It's a feeling. White and filled with doom. Gaseous. Invisible. Deadly. It's everywhere.
Zh: “它是一种感觉。白色的，充满厄运。它是气态的。无形的。致命的。它无处不在。”

En: Yeah.
Zh: “是啊。”

En: That *yeah* is the kind that consoles and sweeps under the rug at the same time.
Zh: 那句*是啊*能产生一些安慰，同时又能避而不谈的效果。

En: Sounds like an advanced form of what I've got. With a bit of old love sprinkled on top.
Zh: “听起来像是我拥有的一种先进的形式。上面还喷洒了一些旧爱的味道。”

En: Doesn't feel like love at all.
Zh: “根本感觉不到爱。”

En: Probably, yeah.
Zh: “可能吧，是啊。”

En: Are you sure? Love is *terror*... She breathes in a large billow of menthol-infused smoke, savours it, then lets it out slowly.
Zh: “你确定吗？爱情是一种*恐惧*……”她吸入一大团薄荷味的烟雾，细细品味，然后慢慢吐了出来。

En: Brother, blast a ziggy now! Press that pleasure button, let the pain recede!
Zh: 老兄，炸裂起来吧！按下快乐的按钮，让痛苦减退！

En: Love is terror. She breathes in a large billow of menthol-infused smoke, savours it, then lets it out slowly...
Zh: “爱情是一种恐惧。”她吸入一大团薄荷味的烟雾，细细品味，然后慢慢吐了出来……

En: Betrayed by the things we love -- what a rotten deal... She breathes in a large billow of menthol-infused smoke, then lets it out slowly...
Zh: “被我们所爱的东西背叛——真是不公平的境遇……”她吸入一大团薄荷味的烟雾，细细品味，然后慢慢吐了出来……

En: Oh my...
Zh: 天啊……

En: Don't be alarmed, but Mr. Garte implied there's an industrial Sad-spill in there. She taps the roof with her heel.
Zh: “别惊慌，不过加尔特先生暗示说那里有工业污染。”她用高跟鞋拍打着屋顶。

En: I don't know what it is.
Zh: “我不知道那是什么。”

En: Cool. I think I'm developing a pinch of that too. It hasn't done wonders to my taste in interior decoration.
Zh: “好吧。我想我也有那么一点点吧。以我对室内装饰的品味，它并没有带来多大的惊喜。”

En: Mhm, it's a *mystery*. She takes a drag. "An alcohol-related mystery."
Zh: “嗯，这是个*秘密*。”她抽了一口烟。“一个跟酒精有关的秘密。”

En: You tell me, officer. I've been trying to open it. In case the Contamination gets to the roof and I have to move on. No luck.
Zh: “不如你告诉我吧，警官。我试过打开它。以免污染爬到屋顶，我就要继续跑路了。不过运气不佳。”

En: You tell me, officer -- when you've kicked it down. I've been wondering myself but I'm... light of foot. Not a good door-kicker.
Zh: “不如你告诉我吧，警官——当你把它踢下去的时候。我自己也一直在想，但是我……脚步很轻。不是很擅长踹门。”

En: I have no idea, officer. She looks at it calmly.
Zh: “我不知道，警官。”她镇静地看着它。

En: You tell me, officer. I've been trying to open it. In case the Contamination gets to the roof and I have to move on. No luck.
Zh: “不如你告诉我吧，警官。我试过打开它。以免污染爬到屋顶，我就要继续跑路了。不过运气不佳。”

En: You tell me, officer -- you kicked it down. I've been wondering myself but I'm... light of foot. Not a good door-kicker.
Zh: “不如你告诉我吧，警官——是你把它踢下去的。我自己也一直在想，但是我……脚步很轻。不是很擅长踹门。”

En: You kicked it down -- you tell me.
Zh: “你把它踹下去的——你告诉我吧。”

En: Nice room you've got here.
Zh: “你的房间挺不错的。”

En: Yeah, it's pretty *De Luxe*.
Zh: “是啊，它是挺*豪华*的。”

En: I'm wintering.
Zh: “我在过冬。”

En: How long have you been staying here?
Zh: “你在这里待多久了？”

En: She calculates. "About four months. I came in November."
Zh: 她计算着。“大概4个月。我是11月来的。”

En: The bills downstairs concur.
Zh: 跟楼下的账单一致。

En: How long have you been wintering?
Zh: “你在这里过冬多久了？”

En: Why here?
Zh: “为什么要来这里？”

En: Here in the Whirling, here in Martinaise, or here in Revachol?
Zh: “是来飞旋旅社，马丁内斯，还是瑞瓦肖？”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Funkytown, she says and takes a sip of her coffee.
Zh: “很放克的地方，”她一边说着，一边抿了一口咖啡。

En: Here in the Whirling.
Zh: “来飞旋旅社。”

En: Here in Martinaise.
Zh: “来马丁内斯。”

En: Here in Revachol.
Zh: “来瑞瓦肖。”

En: Here on the Insulindian isola.
Zh: “来伊苏林迪洲。”

En: Because it's the funkiest building in Martinaise. And because all the other buildings are bombed to hell.
Zh: “因为这是马丁内斯最放克风的建筑。而且其他建筑都被炸成了地狱的模样。”

En: I heard this is where the washed up disco has-beens go.
Zh: “我听说这里是过气迪斯科明星会来的地方。”

En: I always wanted to see 'The Only City in the World' in the worst time of the year. It's a tourist thing.
Zh: “我一直想在一年中最糟糕的时节来看看这座‘世界上唯一的城市’。跟旅游有点关系。”

En: Came to see the New New World, she shrugs. "It's a tourist thing."
Zh: “来看看新新世界，”她耸耸肩。“跟旅游有点关系。”

En: What happened to the old New World?
Zh: “那旧新世界怎么样了？”

En: What happened to the Old World?
Zh: “那旧世界怎么样了？”

En: This is by far not the only reason she's here -- and she isn't really hiding it, either.
Zh: 照目前来看，这不是她来到这里唯一的理由——而且她也没有真正隐藏这一点。

En: This is by far not the only reason she's here -- she isn't overtly hiding it, either.
Zh: 迄今为止，这不是她来到这里唯一的理由——而且她也没有公开隐瞒这一点。

En: You came to the right place.
Zh: “你来对地方了。”

En: You're too young to call yourself a disco has-been.
Zh: “你还太年轻，不能说自己是过气的迪斯科明星。”

En: No. This is Sassytown. It's where the future of dance lies.
Zh: “不是的。这是个时髦的地方。这里就是未来的舞蹈所在。”

En: If you say so, officer -- if you say so.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，警官——如果你想这么说的话。”

En: She smirks. "I'm really not."
Zh: 她笑了，“我真不是。”

En: She looks at you and nods.
Zh: 她看着你，点了点头。

En: It turned out to be a tzaraath-infested third-world slum.
Zh: “最后它成了个霉斑遍布的第三世界贫民窟。”

En: Conference centres. A lot of them.
Zh: “变成了会议中心。很多都是。”

En: All right. She takes a long, pensive drag of her Astra Menthol.
Zh: “好吧。”她沉思着，长长地抽了一口阿斯特拉薄荷烟。

En: Watching herself reflected in the bedroom window -- tall and sparkling and draped in smoke.
Zh: 看着她自己在卧室窗户上的倒影——高挑，闪耀，被烟雾萦绕。

En: Okay. She takes a pensive drag of her cigarette.
Zh: “好吧。”她深思着抽了一口烟。

En: Called Iilmaraa. And it didn't. Even after the Mesque colonized it seven ways from Sunday it's an industrial power house.
Zh: 名字叫做伊尔玛。而且它不是的。甚至在梅斯克从周日开始进行七次殖民之后，它依然是一个工业发电厂。

En: Yeah?
Zh: “是吗？”

En: It is. She moves slightly to your left to check her reflection in it.
Zh: “是的。”她微微移到你的左边，查看里面自己的倒影。

En: An evasive manoeuvre. Ask a follow up!
Zh: 这是逃避策略。要求跟进！

En: The lieutenant makes a note in his notebook.
Zh: 警督在笔记本里做了些笔记。

En: He finds the answer unsatisfying.
Zh: 他对答案不是很满意。

En: When was it changed? During your stay?
Zh: “什么时候换的？在你停留的期间内。”

En: Let her be.
Zh: 随她去吧。

En: Harry, you're becoming soft.
Zh: 哈里，你变得软弱了。

En: Yeah... I'm trying not to lie to you here. Stop making it hard.
Zh: “是啊……我不想骗你。别再给我增加难度了。”

En: What was that? Funkytown?
Zh: 那是什么？放克城市？

En: What's funky?
Zh: “什么是放克？”

En: Believe me, you don't wanna get into that shit. She flicks the ash from her cigarette.
Zh: “相信我，你不会想卷入这种破事的。”她弹了弹烟灰。

En: I do.
Zh: “我想。”

En: You're right.
Zh: “你说得对。”

En: She nods, inspecting your sideburns. "You've got it bad as it is."
Zh: 她点点头，查看你的鬓角。“你的情况已经很糟糕了。”

En: No-no. She leans in to inspect your sideburns. "You've got it bad as it is."
Zh: “不不不。”她靠近了一些，查看你的鬓角。“你的情况已经很糟糕了。”

En: Funk is an aggressive resurgence of *The New* aesthetic of the Revacholian Thirties, but this time originating in Graad. It's brought there by the Iilmaraan diaspora. Funk is more masculine and guitar-oriented than disco. Even more *dangerous*.
Zh: 放克是一种音乐形式，它是对瑞瓦肖三十年代产生的*新时代*审美的一种激进复兴，不过这一次是起源于格拉德的。它是随着伊尔玛大移民一起带来的。放克音乐比迪斯科更阳刚，更加以吉他为导向。甚至可以说更*危险*一些。

En: Then tell the truth.
Zh: “那就把真相告诉我。”

En: Then lie to me.
Zh: “那就对我撒谎吧。”

En: Okay then -- I'll cut you some slack.
Zh: “那好吧——我会放你一马。”

En: Thanks. She smiles. It's a sad little smile, like she's some miniature bird or tiny field mouse.
Zh: “谢谢。”她笑了。这个笑容有些悲伤，感觉她就像是一只小小鸟或者是一只小田鼠。

En: Behind it is deep, boundless gratitude.
Zh: 在那背后是深深的，无尽的感激。

En: I don't want to lie to the law. It's not my style. She smiles. It's a sad little smile, like she's some miniature bird or tiny field mouse.
Zh: “我不想对执法人员说谎。这不是我的作风。”她笑了。这个笑容有些悲伤，感觉她就像是一只小小鸟或者是一只小田鼠。

En: Then she whispers: "I'm just a girl whom windows break around."
Zh: 然后她低语到：“我只是一个周围窗户会碎裂的女孩。”

En: The truth is I'm a horrible girl. Windows break around me. It's not their fault.
Zh: “事实是我是一个可怕的女孩。我周围的窗户都会碎裂。这不是他们的错。”

En: You hear the scribbling of the lieutenant's pen.
Zh: 你听见警督的笔涂画的声音。

En: Feels like success.
Zh: 感觉很成功。

En: She looks at the dried petals in your palm, then lightly touches one with her fingernail.
Zh: 她看着你手中枯萎的花瓣，然后用手指甲轻轻碰触了其中一瓣。

En: Chipped white polish covers the nail. It's long and sharp, like a mini dagger. The petal crumbles on contact.
Zh: 斑驳的白色指甲油覆盖在她的指甲上。它很长很尖利，就像一把迷你匕首。花瓣一碰到就碎了。

En: Pretty. Looks like a dried *may bell* -- is that the one you caught? Sam Bo-style?
Zh: “很漂亮。看起来像是一朵枯萎的*铃兰*——这就是你抓到的那朵吗？萨拳风？”

En: Why was it there?
Zh: “它为什么会在那里？”

En: Sudden change of topic!
Zh: “突然转变话题！”

En: Why was there a flower on the roof? I don't know, officer. Because of the wind?
Zh: “为什么屋顶上有一朵花？我不知道，警探。因为风吹来的吗？”

En: Any admirers, miss?
Zh: “你有什么爱慕者吗，小姐？”

En: Admirers? I'm too old to be a débutante. She looks over the railing at the plaza below. "And this place is no fashionable society."
Zh: “爱慕者？我年纪太大，做不了初涉社交界的名媛。”她越过栏杆，看向下面的广场。“而且这里也不是上流社会。”

En: Or an admirer?
Zh: 或者有一个爱慕者？

En: What's Sam Bo?
Zh: “萨拳是什么？”

En: A martial art, sir. She raises an eyebrow. "Is that it?"
Zh: “一种武术，长官。”她扬起眉毛。“就这些吗？”

En: Question avoided. Let's conclude that she has admirers. Plenty of them.
Zh: 回避问题。可以推断她有仰慕者。而且还不少。

En: All right. She nods and takes a drag. "I like it sudden."
Zh: “好吧。”她点点头，抽了一口烟。“我喜欢突如其来的感觉。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: She watches you pocket the petals, then closes her eyes as she breathes in the cigarette fumes...
Zh: 她看着你把花瓣装进口袋，然后闭上眼镜，吸入了一口香烟……

En: Samaran boxing, or Sam Bo. Graceful martial arts stuff. 'Sam Bo- style' implies stealth and cool.
Zh: 萨马拉拳击，或者又称为萨拳。优雅的武术类的东西。“萨拳风”意味着潜行和镇静。

En: Look her in the eye.
Zh: 看着她的眼睛。

En: Slight confusion about bullet.
Zh: 对子弹稍微有些疑惑。

En: Strange answer to window.
Zh: 窗户的奇怪回应。

En: Why did she say she's a suspect?
Zh: 她为什么说自己是嫌疑人？

En: Standard Anti-Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准反威勒尔骰子。

En: I was just thinking -- what a nice day for questions. She lights a cigarette. "Pertaining to a murder investigation."
Zh: “我只是在想——今天可真是个问问题的好日子。”她点燃一只香烟，“跟一起凶杀案调查有关的问题。”

En: Officer. What brings you up here in the rain? She leans back against the railing.
Zh: “警官。你为什么冒雨来到这里呢？”她向后倚靠在栏杆上。

En: I have a couple of more questions about the deceased.
Zh: “关于那个死者，我还有一些问题。”

En: Now that you've had some time -- can you tell us more about the victim?
Zh: “既然你有时间了——能多给我们讲讲被害人吗？”

En: Now that you've had some time -- can you tell me more about him?
Zh: “既然你有时间了——能多给我们讲讲他的事吗？”

En: Can you tell us more about the deceased? General information...
Zh: “能多跟我们说说死者的事吗？基本信息什么的……”

En: Can you tell me more about the victim? General information, I mean.
Zh: “能多跟我说说被害人吗？我的意思是说，基本信息。”

En: Something in her demeanour has changed. She's tired, consigned to her fate -- to being here with you and what's to come.
Zh: 她的行为好像有些变化。她很疲惫，向自己的命运屈服了——跟你一起待在这里，还有接下来要发生的事情。

En: It's always good to see you. She cracks a weary smile, leaning back against the railing.
Zh: “能看见你总是很高兴的。”她挤出一个疲惫的笑容，向后靠在了栏杆上。

En: The chorus of the '35 single "Megafoning The Entire Human Race," instills you with the fuck-it-all swagger that prompts one to plough into grannies on your morning stroll.
Zh: 35年的合唱单曲“人类最强音”，向你灌输了一种*大爷我什么都不在乎*的招摇风范，让你在早起散步的时候可以毫无顾忌地在大妈群里横冲直撞。

En: Hello again, officer. She sounds tired now. All the pep is gone.
Zh: “又见面了，警官。”现在的她听上去有些疲惫。所有活力都已经消失不见。

En: I had a few more comments about what's behind that door...
Zh: “关于那扇门背后的东西，我还有些话要说……”

En: That door there... (Point to it.) "Did you know it leads to a downstairs *elevator*?"
Zh: “那边那扇门……”（指向它。）“你知道它是通向楼下的*电梯*吗？”

En: There's a peep hole on the other side, looking into your bedroom.
Zh: “另一边有一个偷窥孔，可以看到你的卧室。”

En: She looks at the door. Suddenly, her eyes widen. "Oh... Do you think *Ruby* could have gotten in that way? To the *roof*? She could have come up through there!"
Zh: 她看着那扇门。突然，她睁大了双眼。“哦……你觉得*露比*可能就是这样进来的吗？上到*屋顶*？她可以从那里上来的！”

En: I did not. She takes a drag of her cigarette and smiles. "Mystery solved then. I kept wondering where it led."
Zh: “我不知道。”她抽了一口烟，笑了。“那谜题就解开了。我一直也很好奇它会通向哪里。”

En: Okay... There's a pause as she processes this information.
Zh: “好吧……”为了消化这条信息，她稍微停顿了一下。

En: A peep hole? You mean like a hole in the wall?
Zh: “一个偷窥孔？你是说类似墙上那种孔吗？”

En: That's all for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “暂时先这样吧。”（总结。）

En: Mhm... She flicks the ash from her cigarette absent-mindedly.
Zh: “嗯……”她心不在焉地弹了弹烟灰。

En: I did not. She takes a drag of her cigarette. "Do you think this has something to do with what happened?"
Zh: “我没有。”她抽了一口烟。“你觉得这跟发生的事有什么关联吗？”

En: Huh. This isn't good.
Zh: “嗯。这可不太妙啊。”

En: She's lost in thought -- eyes narrowed, forehead furrowed...
Zh: 她陷入了沉思——眼睛眯起，前额挤出了沟痕……

En: It's possible.
Zh: “有这个可能。”

En: It's possible.
Zh: “有这个可能。”

En: Not really.
Zh: “不见得。”

En: She keeps looking at the door with a worried expression. The wind ruffles her hair.
Zh: 她忧虑地一直盯着那扇门看。风吹乱了她的头发。

En: I don't know... She frowns, uncertain. "Was there anything else? Back there?"
Zh: “我不知道……”她皱起眉头，不是很确定。“还有别的吗？那后面？”

En: There were tracks on the floor. They're recent.
Zh: “地板上留下了一些痕迹，是最近的。”

En: It's an old pinball workshop. The room back there. This place used to be a pinball arcade.
Zh: “那是一间破旧的弹球工作室，就是后面那个房间。这个地方曾经是一家弹球游戏室。”

En: Yes. Looking into your bedroom, miss. The lieutenant points to her window. The unmade bed is visible through the glass.
Zh: “是的。能看到你的卧室，小姐。”警督指着她的窗户。透过玻璃可以看到还没整理好的床铺。

En: Looking into your bedroom, miss.
Zh: “能看到你的卧室，小姐。”

En: She feels like quarry, encircled... Her eyes dart to the door.
Zh: 她感觉自己就像个猎物，被重重包围……她的眼睛瞥向那扇门。

En: Okay. She smiles. "I'm glad *someone's* had fun."
Zh: “好吧。”她笑了。“很高兴至少*有人*玩的很开心。”

En: A jitter of fear and disgust moves through her body, beginning from her shoulders and ending in her hips. The cigarette tastes foul to her now.
Zh: 她的身体因为恐惧和厌恶而颤抖着，从肩膀开始，一直延伸到臀部。现在连香烟的味道也变得很难闻。

En: It could be connected.
Zh: “可能有联系。”

En: It could just be a coincidence.
Zh: “可能只是个巧合。”

En: We don't know at this point.
Zh: “我们现在还不知道。”

En: Okay... do you have any way of knowing how *long* it has been there?
Zh: “好吧……你能不能搞清楚它在那里已经*多久*了？”

En: Do you think this is somehow *connected* to me?
Zh: “你觉得这跟我*有关系*吗？”

En: Do you think... this is somehow related to... Ruby passing through there?
Zh: “你会不会觉得……露比经过那里……会不会跟这个有关？”

En: Unfortunately, no, but if I were to guess -- long enough. The perforation is under the book shelf on your wall. It should not be hard to cover with some tape.
Zh: “很遗憾，不能，不过按照我的猜测——很久了。偷窥孔在你房间墙壁的书架下面。用胶带封起来应该不难。”

En: There's no way of telling.
Zh: “这个说不清楚。”

En: God... where is it? I think I need to cover it up.
Zh: “天啦……它在哪？我想我要把它封起来。”

En: The perforation should be under your bookcase, in your bedroom.
Zh: “偷窥孔应该在你卧室的书架下面。”

En: Thank you. She looks toward the window. A cold wind blows in from the sea.
Zh: “谢谢你。”她看着窗户。海面吹来一阵冷风。

En: If I'd make a guess I'd say it's recent.
Zh: “要我猜的话，应该是最近的事。”

En: Let's not discuss it any longer.
Zh: “我们还是不要再讨论它了。”

En: No, it's okay. Was there anything more?
Zh: “不，没事的。还有别的吗？”

En: It's very hard to believe -- that she'd do something like that. But... no. I don't know. She squints. "I just don't know about this, officer."
Zh: “很难相信——她居然会做出这种事。但是……不。我不知道。”她眯起眼睛。“我真的不知道这件事，警官。”

En: Shit... I don't know. Maybe it's been there for a long time. Maybe the local kids use it or something. I don't know...
Zh: “该死……我不知道。也许已经很久了吧。也许本地的小孩会用，或者什么的。我不知道……”

En: She *could* have. Maybe she did. Or maybe it's been there for a long time, the local kids use it or something. Was there anything else back there?
Zh: “她*可以*的。也许就是她做的。或者它已经在那里很久了，也许本地的小孩会用，或者什么的。那后面还有什么别的吗？”

En: She does. She must have *some* idea.
Zh: 她知道。她肯定*有些*想法。

En: I'll be fucking covering it up with *a lot* of tape -- that's for sure. Was there anything else back there?
Zh: “我要用*很多*胶带把它封起来——这是肯定的。后面还有什么别的吗？”

En: You must have *some* idea -- who might have been interested in making that hole?
Zh: “你肯定*有些*想法——谁可能会有兴趣挖出那个孔呢？”

En: Honestly, I have no clue.
Zh: “说实话，我真的不知道。”

En: Yet? Maybe it's something she's keeping for a later move, when she's more sure of herself...
Zh: 暂时不行？也许她是打算留作下一步动作，等到她自己更确定一些的时候……

En: Why do I feel like you've *won* here?
Zh: “为什么我觉得这里是你*赢了*？”

En: I really don't know, sir. She takes a sip of her cold coffee. "I certainly don't *feel* like I've won. I feel like shit, sir. All the time."
Zh: “我真的不知道，先生。”她抿了一口冷掉的咖啡。“我完全不*觉得*自己赢了。我感觉很糟糕，先生。一直都是这样。”

En: She smiles. A bitter little smile. She means it.
Zh: 她笑了。一丝苦涩的微笑。她是认真的。

En: But this turned out well for you. You've *slipped* past all suspicions.
Zh: “但是这对你来说是件好事。你*躲过了*所有怀疑。”

En: You're right.
Zh: “你说得对。”

En: Clearly, I haven't. We're having this conversation, aren't we? How *well* could it have gone. I mean...  She looks around.
Zh: “显然并没有。我们现在就在谈话，不是吗？它能有*多好*呢。我的意思是说……”她看了看周围。

En: How *well* could it have turned out for me. I mean...  She looks around.
Zh: “我的意思是说……它对我来说能变得*多好*呢。”她看了看周围。

En: I'm stuck in Martinaise, just like all of us. I've been up here for... I don't know how long now? I like to call this my *rooftop containment facility*.
Zh: “我被困在了马丁内斯。就像所有人一样。我在这里已经待了……我也不知道多久？我喜欢把这个叫做是我的*屋顶封隔设施*。”

En: The snow falls on her shoulders, then melts in her light blond hair... Cold and soft.
Zh: 雪花飘落在她的肩头，然后融化在她浅金色的头发上……冰冷而柔软。

En: The rain falls on her shoulders, tinting her blond hair light brown. It smells of industrial pollution and brine.
Zh: 雨滴拍打在她的剪头，把她的金发染上了一层浅棕色。闻起来有工业污染和海水的味道。

En: The wet wind, heavy with brine and industrial pollution from the stacks nearby, ruffles her hair.
Zh: 潮湿的海风，夹杂着大量海水的咸味，还有附近堆栈散发的工业污染的味道，拨乱了她的头发。

En: What are you contained for then?
Zh: “那你为什么要留下来？”

En: This all turned out well enough for you -- you somehow managed to not become a *suspect*.
Zh: “这一切对你来说已经够好了——你不知怎么的已经成功脱离了*嫌疑人*的范围。”

En: That's it then, I guess. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我猜，那就这样吧。”（总结。）

En: For my *sins* of course. The long standing sins of a bad, frivolous person.
Zh: “当然是因为我的*罪过*。一个邪恶又轻浮的人长久以来犯下的罪过。”

En: She nods, slowly -- carefully, even.
Zh: 她慢慢点点头——甚至可以说有些小心。

En: There is suddenly a strange glint in her eyes. Not malicious... but dangerous.
Zh: 她的眼睛里突然闪过一道奇怪的光芒。并不邪恶……但却很危险。

En: For destroying my first love. For working for bad people. The list goes on and on.
Zh: “因为毁掉了我的初恋。因为帮坏人做事。这张清单还有很长很长。”

En: Did I? She arches an eyebrow and sighs. "Why do I still feel *suspicion* hanging over me then? What I managed was to get him killed. I'm the love that does people in, remember?"
Zh: “我有吗？”她弯起眉毛，叹了口气。“为什么我还是觉得自己没有脱离*嫌疑*呢？我所做的就是把他害死了。我可是要人命的爱情，记得吗？”

En: Did I? She arches an eyebrow and sighs. "Why do I still feel *suspicion* hanging over me then? What I managed was to get him killed. I understand that."
Zh: “我有吗？”她弯起眉毛，叹了口气。“为什么我还是觉得自己没有脱离*嫌疑*呢？我所做的就是把他害死了。我能理解这一点。”

En: The lieutenant looks away, over the railing.
Zh: 警督的目光越过栏杆，看向了其他地方。

En: He feels uncomfortable with this conversation. He doesn't know what to add.
Zh: 他对这次谈话感觉不太舒服。他不知道该补充些什么。

En: The dried flowers on the roof -- could Ruby have left them? These don't fit with anything.
Zh: “屋顶上那朵枯萎的花——可能是露比留下的吗？这些跟任何事都不符合。”

En: The what? She narrows her eyes. "Oh, those ones! I don't know, sir. I said I have no idea what to make of them. Honestly, I think they're just trash."
Zh: “什么？”她眯起眼睛。“哦，那些啊！我不知道，长官。我说过了，我不知道它们是怎么一回事。老实说，我觉得它们只是垃圾。”

En: She narrows her eyes. "You mean that little thing you caught -- when you first came here? On the roof there?"
Zh: 她眯起眼睛。“你是说你抓到的那些小玩意吗——就是你第一次来这里的时候？在那边的屋顶上？”

En: I mean, there's no reason for me to think they're somehow connected to her.
Zh: “我的意思是说，我没有任何理由认为这些跟她有着某种关联。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I don't know, sir. I said I have no idea what to make of them. Honestly, I think they're just trash
Zh: “我不知道，长官。我说过了，我不知道它们是怎么一回事。老实说，我觉得它们只是垃圾。”

En: Something seems... *off* with this theory I've developed. About Ruby.
Zh: “我的理论好像……有些不太*对劲*的地方——关于露比的。”

En: I don't know what to say.
Zh: “我不知道该说什么。”

En: It all seems fortuitous -- for you.
Zh: “这一切好像都是偶然的——对于你来说。”

En: *None* of this is fortuitous for me.
Zh: “对我来说*没有什么*是偶然的。”

En: Look. She greets you, taking a drag. "It's dark outside, the dogs are barking and I'm still here, drinking my coffee -- just like I promised." She smiles faintly.
Zh: “你看。”她抽了一口烟，向你打了个招呼。“外面很黑，狗在叫，而我还在这里，喝我的咖啡——就像我保证过的。”她微微一笑。

En: She did promise she'd be here at night if you don't arrest her... Good girl.
Zh: 她确实保证过，如果你不逮捕她的话，她晚上就会出现在这里……好姑娘。
