En: Pff... It's surely an act. Ask questions. Tough ones, preferably.
Zh: 啐……这肯定是在演戏。问问题吧。最好是很难回答的那种。

En: Yes. Why was it there?
Zh: “是的。它为什么会在那里呢？”

En: Mhm. She takes a drag. "Go ahead."
Zh: “嗯。”她抽了一口烟。“继续吧。”

En: Look up to the grey sky.
Zh: 抬头望向灰蒙蒙的天空。

En: Independent Revachol.
Zh: 独立的瑞瓦肖。

En: Snowing. Try again when it's stopped.
Zh: 正在下雪。等停了之后再试一次。

En: Raining. Try again when it's stopped.
Zh: 正在下雨。等停了之后再试一次。

En: She's trapped here
Zh: 她被困在这里了。

En: In god's name, *wake up*!
Zh: 苍天在上，快*醒醒*！

En: Manipulating your skillset!
Zh: 操纵你的技能！

En: *Clearly* lied about the bullet.
Zh: *明显*对子弹的事撒了谎。

En: The love that did him in.
Zh: 害他丧命的爱情。

En: Lied about Lely enjoying death.
Zh: 谎称莱利享受死亡。

En: Semen sample avoidance.
Zh: 回避精液样本。

En: Everything went fine for her.
Zh: 对她来说一切都很顺利。

En: Lied about making the call?
Zh: 在打电话的事上撒谎？

En: Discussed secret route.
Zh: 讨论秘密路线。

En: Last minute suspicion...
Zh: 最后一分钟的怀疑……

En: Let's get back to those lies you told.
Zh: “我们说回你的那些谎言吧。”

En: Lies, I... She repeats -- then trails off. It's unclear what she intended to say.
Zh: “谎言，我……”她重复着——然后声音逐渐减弱。不清楚她到底打算说什么。

En: Yes! We demand she be punished for deceiving us. We demand her anxiety. We demand her fear.
Zh: 是的！因为欺骗吾辈，她必须受到惩罚。吾辈要求她焦虑。吾辈要求她恐惧。

En: Tell me what really happened, one more time.
Zh: “再跟我说一次事情的真相。”

En: The Hardie boys told us what really happened.
Zh: “哈迪兄弟把事情的真相告诉我们了。”

En: I have a couple of more questions about the deceased.
Zh: “关于那个死者，我还有一些问题。”

En: Can you tell me more about Lely, the hanged man? My partner needs to hear this.
Zh: “能跟我们多说说莱利，那个上吊的男人吗？我的搭档需要听听这个。”

En: We have a couple more questions about the deceased.
Zh: “关于那个死者，我们还有一些问题。”

En: We have concluded our autopsy and would like to ask questions about Lely.
Zh: “我们已经完成了尸检，想问一些有关莱利的问题。”

En: Let's see. I wrote down the nickname 'Lelystad'. The lieutenant has taken out his notebook. He moves his finger across the page…
Zh: 警督点点头，拿出了他的笔记本。“我来看看。我记下了一个叫‘莱利斯塔德’的昵称。”他的手指在页面上滑动……

En: ...then tears one out and hands it to you. "We have questions that might help solve some gaps in our field autopsy. I'm sure my colleague has a few of his own while I take notes."
Zh: ……然后撕下一张，递给了你。“我们有些问题，也许能帮忙解决现场尸检的时候发现的一些空白。我做笔记的同时，相信我的同事会有一些自己的问题。”

En: Yes, miss. I hope you don't mind. The lieutenant hands you his notes.
Zh: “是的，小姐。希望你不要介意。”警督把他的笔记递给了你。

En: I found your buoy. It was empty. Just seawater.
Zh: “我找到了你说的浮标。里面是空的。只有海水。”

En: Her eyes widen. "Oh..."
Zh: 她睁大眼睛。“哦……”

En: She doesn't seem to be lying -- she's genuinely spooked by their disappearance.
Zh: 她不像是在撒谎——她是真心被它们的失踪吓坏了。

En: Did you take the documents?
Zh: “证件是你拿走的吗？”

En: Did you take the documents?
Zh: “证件是你拿走的吗？”

En: No, of course not. As I said, it would have been too risky for me to use those documents anyway -- my employer gave them to me. In truth, I should have destroyed them.
Zh: “不，当然不是。我说过了，用那些证件对我来说太危险——是我的雇主把它们给我的。说真的，我应该把它们销毁掉。”

En: No, of course not. As I said, it would have been too risky for me to use those documents anyway -- my employer gave them to me. In truth, I should have destroyed them.
Zh: “不，当然不是。我说过了，用那些证件对我来说太危险——是我的雇主把它们给我的。说真的，我应该把它们销毁掉。”

En: My working hypothesis is that there is a cosmic conspiracy of some kind -- and it's out to get us all.
Zh: “我的工作假说是，这里面有着某种阴谋——而且它打算让我们所有人都陷入大麻烦。”

En: I have this strange feeling that it was an undiscovered *cryptid* that took your documents.
Zh: “我有种奇怪的感觉——是一种未被发现的*神秘动物*拿走了你的证件。”

En: I'm afraid you may be in danger.
Zh: “恐怕你现在有危险了。”

En: Perhaps the people who are after you took it.
Zh: “也许是追踪你的人拿走的。”

En: She flashes you a sudden half-smile. "Yes, that must be it. Thank you. I feel better already."
Zh: 她突然对你微微一笑。“是的，肯定就是这样。谢谢你。我已经感觉好多了。”

En: Cryptid? Of course. That must be it. She takes a drag of her cigarette, warm amusement in her eyes. "Thank you, I feel better already."
Zh: “神秘动物？当然了。肯定就是这样。”她抽了一口烟，眼睛里流露出暖心的愉悦。“谢谢你。我已经感觉好多了。”

En: She thinks for a moment, then shrugs. "Yes, I am -- regardless of who took the documents."
Zh: 她想了一会儿，然后耸耸肩。“没错——不管到底是谁拿走了证件。”

En: She thinks for a moment, then shrugs. "They couldn't know where I put it. I'm absolutely sure I was not followed. And I've told no one but you..."
Zh: 她想了一会儿，然后耸耸肩。“他们不可能知道我放在哪里了。我很肯定自己没有被跟踪。而且除了你，我没有告诉任何人……”

En: Now that I think about it -- you mentioned sea water. I was worried I'd been too careless with the latch. The documents were probably, she waves her cigarette, "just washed away..."
Zh: “我仔细想了一下——你提到海水。我担心我对锁闩装置不太小心。那些证件可能，”她晃了晃手中的香烟，“被冲走了……”

En: He's trying to throw her off with the bluntness of the question.
Zh: 他想用直言不讳的提问来迷惑她。

En: I'll file this under a *theft* of some kind.
Zh: “我要把这件事归档到*盗窃*的门类下。”

En: Thank you, but actually -- now that I think about it -- you mentioned sea water? I was worried I'd been too careless with the latch mechanism... The documents were probably, she waves her cigarette, "just washed away..."
Zh: “谢谢你，不过老实说——我仔细想了一下——你提到了海水？我担心我对锁闩装置不太小心……那些证件可能，”她晃了晃手中的香烟，“被冲走了……”

En: It was probably just an accident. I was worried I'd been too careless with the latch. The documents were probably just, she waves her cigarette, "washed away..."
Zh: “这很可能只是个意外。我担心我对锁闩装置不太小心。那些证件可能，”她晃了晃手中的香烟，“被冲走了……”

En: You mentioned sea water? I was worried I'd been too careless with the latch. The documents were probably, she waves her cigarette, "just washed away..."
Zh: “你提到了海水？我担心我对锁闩装置不太小心。那些证件可能，”她晃了晃手中的香烟，“被冲走了……”

En: Miss, we are investigating the murder of the man who was hanged. He looks down at the yard. "The people responsible have asked us to talk to you."
Zh: “小姐，我们在调查一个男人被绞死的凶杀案。”他低头看着院子。“相关责任人让我们跟你聊聊。”

En: Miss, we are investigating the murder of the man down there. He looks down at the yard. "The people who put him there have asked us to talk to you."
Zh: “小姐，我们正在调查下面那个死者的凶杀案。”他低头看着院子。“把他放在那里的人让我们跟你聊聊。”

En: I was just thinking what a nice evening it is. She looks around. "For taking part in a murder investigation."
Zh: “我刚刚还在想这是一个多么美丽的夜晚。”她看了看周围。“正好适合参加凶杀案调查。”

En: Hello, officer. Legs crossed, she leans back against the railing. "What brings you up here again?"
Zh: “你好，警官。”她双腿交叉，向后倚靠在栏杆上。“是什么风又把你给吹来了？”

En: Let's talk more about this so-called assault.
Zh: “我们再说说这个所谓的袭击吧。”

En: She seems quite relaxed for a victim of assault. But, of course, what *seems* should not be your priority.
Zh: 作为一个袭击的被害人，她似乎挺放松的。不过当然了，*似乎*并不是你的优先级。

En: Miss Oranje? Now there is something you should *linger* on.
Zh: 奥兰治小姐？这才是你应该*流连*的问题。

En: Wouldn't it be, um... inappropriate to linger?
Zh: 难道不觉得，呃……流连有些不合适吗？

En: All things considered -- very. Now linger.
Zh: 综合各方面考虑——非常不合适。现在开始流连吧。

En: Anyway -- I thought it would be *funny* to say I didn't do it. Because I knew about your investigation... She cringes. "Does that make me a suspect? I really must stop saying stupid things..."
Zh: “不管怎么说——我觉得如果说不是我做的，好像有点*好笑*。因为我知道你的调查……”她揶揄到。“那我是不是就成了嫌疑人？我真的不能再想这种愚蠢的事情了……”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: It's mostly about the lack of cleaning services. And an abundance of *me*.
Zh: “主要还是缺少清洁服务。还有大量的*我*。”

En: No one likes to talk about their dirty laundry and their empty bottles. Maybe if you phrase it emotionally...
Zh: 没人喜欢谈论他们的脏衣服还有空瓶子。也许如果你的措辞再情绪化一些……

En: About that drug collection you have down there...
Zh: “关于你收藏的那些处方药……”

En: You have quite the collection of prescription drugs down there.
Zh: “你那里还真是收藏了不少处方药啊。”

En: I was undergoing a massive speed hangover. I needed to prop myself up.
Zh: “当时我正在经历思必得带来的严重副作用。我需要支撑自己。”

En: Thank you -- I've put a lot of time and effort into it, she says without any discernible irony.
Zh: “谢谢你——我在里面投入了大量的时间还有精力，”她的话里没有任何明显的讽刺意味。

En: Technically, possession of narcotics is legal in Revachol. But you should still reprimand her.
Zh: 从严格的意义上讲，持有麻醉剂在瑞瓦肖是合法的。不过你还是应该训斥她。

En: Just looking out for your health, miss.
Zh: “只是要注意自己的健康，小姐。”

En: Hmh?
It was confiscation.
Zh: “这叫没收。”

En: Better safe than sorry. She takes a drag and smiles.
Zh: “小心驶得万年船。”她抽了一口烟，笑了。

En: Oh yes. One of my favourites. It cures many ailments.
Zh: “哦，是的。我的最爱之一。它能治愈很多疾病。”

En: No, no, don't listen to *the stiff*. Be cool.
Zh: 不不，别听这些*死板的话*。要酷一点。

En: Tsk... tsk... she says to herself. "Like a common highwayman. This *will* come up again during what will be an opportune moment for me, Officer Disco."
Zh: “啧……啧啧……”她自言自语。“就像个普通的拦路强盗。对于我来说，这个问题在一个恰当的时间点*还会*出现的，迪斯科警官。”

En: You heard what I said: Narco-mania is a serious issue and nothing to scoff at, miss.
Zh: “你听见我说的了：麻醉剂上瘾是一个很严重的问题，没什么好嘲笑的，小姐。”

En: Tsk... tsk... she says to herself. "Took it like a common highwayman. Don't think this won't come up again during what will be an opportune moment for me, Officer Disco." She smiles.
Zh: “啧……啧啧……”她自言自语。“像个普通的拦路强盗把它拿走了。别以为这个问题不会再出现了——对于我来说——只要遇到一个恰当的时间点，迪斯科警官。”她笑了。

En: *Narcomania* is nothing to be proud of, miss.
Zh: “*麻醉剂上瘾*不是什么值得骄傲的事，小姐。”

En: She almost spits out her coffee: "What did you say?"
Zh: 她差点就把咖啡喷出来了：“你说什么？”

En: Wow... a hardened, callous highwayman. Don't think this won't come up again during what will be an opportune moment for me, Officer Disco. She smiles.
Zh: “哇哦……一个老练又无情的拦路强盗。别以为这个问题不会再出现了——对于我来说——只要遇到一个恰当的时间点，迪斯科警官。”她笑了。

En: Oh my god, now it's turning into Narco Mania. It's got to be serious if it's *two words*.
Zh: “天啦，现在又变成麻醉剂上瘾了。如果是*两个词*的话，那就是来真的了。”

En: Like not being able to stay up for 36 hours. She thinks. "Not being happy. It cures *those* ailments. It's just a mirrored speed molecule, basically."
Zh: “比如没办法36小时不睡觉。”她想到。“不开心。它能治愈*那些*疾病。基本上它只是镜像版的思必得微粒。”

En: Is that something that happens to you often, miss? His tone isn't aggressive, just inquisitive.
Zh: “你经常会出现这种情况吗，小姐？”他的口气并不咄咄逼人，只是有些好奇。

En: Does that happen to you often?
Zh: “你经常会出现这种情况吗？”

En: This does not amount to actual corruption. I can easily log it under a confiscation.
Zh: “这并不相当于真正的腐败。我很容易就能地把它记录在没收的栏目下面。”

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: No-no! You said *narcomania*. What kind of word is that?
Zh: “不！你说*麻醉剂上瘾*。这是什么词？”

En: Mhm, she nods, smiling: "I.e. *narcomaniacs*."
Zh: “嗯，”她点点头，笑了。“比如说，*麻醉剂上瘾*。”

En: It's hard to tell if she means it in earnest or as a joke.
Zh: 很难说她是认真的，还是只当个玩笑。

En: In my head it designates people who abuse substances.
Zh: “在我的脑子里，它指定的是那些滥用药物的人。”

En: Comes in handy when you've done too many opioids.
Zh: “当你吃了太多阿片类药物的时候，它就能派上用场了。”

En: Her face lights up with laughter. "I'm sorry," she gathers herself.
Zh: 她的脸被笑容点亮了。“很抱歉，”她恢复了镇定。

En: Very funky. She takes a drag, looking you straight in the eye.
Zh: “非常放克风。”她抽了一口烟，直直地望向你的眼睛。

En: It was quite impressive. How did you amass such a hoard?
Zh: “挺让人印象深刻的。你是怎么积累这么一大堆的？”

En: She laughs, turning to the lieutenant. "There it is again. Man of *words*. I can appreciate that, having half a bachelor's degree in words and all..."
Zh: 她笑着转向警督。“又来了。那个*只会说*的男人。我能领会，毕竟我拥有半个语言学士学位……”

En: She laughs, turning to the lieutenant. "There it is again -- man of *words*. I can appreciate that."
Zh: 她笑着转向警督。“又来了——那个*只会说*的男人。我能领会。”

En: With money, sir. She takes a drag. "It's not exactly the Antistar-sized caboodle I intend for it to be one day, but it's getting there."
Zh: “用钱，长官。”她抽了一口烟。“并不完全是我希望它某天能达到反明星的高度，但是正在朝那个方向发展。”

En: The Antistar is (or was) a Vespertine rock and roll star, who liked to do drugs. He did so many drugs he eventually mutated -- into a corpse.
Zh: 反明星是（或者曾经是）一个很喜欢嗑药的维斯珀摇滚明星。由于实在是嗑的太狠，最后他突变了——变成了一具尸体。

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如什么？”

En: She laughs. "There it is again -- man of *words*. I can appreciate that, having half a bachelor's degree in words and all..."
Zh: 她笑了：“又来了。那个*只会说*的男人。我能领会，毕竟我拥有半个语言学士学位……”

En: She laughs: "There it is again -- man of *words*. I can appreciate that."
Zh: 她笑了：“又来了——那个*只会说*的男人。我能领会。”

En: It's a word. Let's get on with it.
Zh: “这只是一个词语。我们还是继续吧。”

En: Sure, she smiles. "Onto where?"
Zh: “当然，”她笑了。“继续什么？”

En: Huh? She looks at the drugs in your hand. "Motherfucker took my Preptide! Looks like you owe me one, officer. I'm talking serious corruption here."
Zh: “嗯？”她看着手中的毒品。“混帐东西把我的帕立太特拿走了！看来你欠了我一个人情，警官。我说的可是严重的腐败行为。”

En: Cool. I took some. For personal use. (Show her the *Preptide*.)
Zh: “好吧。我拿了一些。个人用。”（把*帕立太特*拿给她看。）

En: That's all as far as that goes, then.
Zh: “那么，就到此为止吧。”

En: We're done with this subject.
Zh: “这个话题可以结束了。”

En: Sure, she smiles.
Zh: “当然，”她笑了。

En: The collection includes NACRA -- an opioid antagonist.
Zh: “藏品里还包含纳克拉——一种阿片类拮抗剂。”

En: You seem to have, among other things, Preptide.
Zh: “除此之外，你好像还有，帕立太特。”

En: No further questions.
Zh: “没有什么问题了。”

En: I always heard you RCM guys were cool. She gestures toward you with her cigarette. "You are."
Zh: “我一直听说你们RCM的人挺酷的。”她用香烟朝你比划着。“你的确很酷。”

En: Tsk... tsk... she says to herself with a smile. "Robbed by common highwaymen."
Zh: “啧……啧啧……”她笑着自言自语到。“被一个普通的拦路强盗打劫了。”

En: There may be more to this *mystery* -- at some later time. She's holding back. Let's make a mental note for now.
Zh: 这个*秘密*的内容可能远不止这样——之后再说吧。她有所隐瞒。暂时先记在心里吧。

En: If it was recent, who do you think made it -- Ruby?
Zh: “如果是最近的，你觉得是谁做的——露比吗？”

En: If it *is* recent -- who do you think made it?
Zh: “如果它是*最近*的——你觉得是谁做的？”

En: Ruby? She couldn't have left it. She's not old-fashioned like that. I don't know what that flower was doing there, honestly.
Zh: “露比？她不可能把它留下的。她可不是那种老派的人。老实说，我不知道那朵花为什么会在那里。”

En: I can't seem to find Ruby. Do you have any idea where she might be?
Zh: “我好像找不到露比。你知道她可能去哪了吗？”

En: I wouldn't know, officer. She had a crash pad somewhere on the coast... but she was very secretive about it, even with me.
Zh: “我不知道，警官。她在海岸的某个地方有个临时住所……但她对此守口如瓶，甚至都不肯告诉我。”

En: Yes, I know about the *crash pad*. Even spoke to the land lady. But Ruby's not there anymore.
Zh: “没错，我知道这个*临时住所*。甚至还跟女房东聊过了。但是露比已经不在那里了。”

En: We found that *crash pad* on the coast, miss, and even spoke to the land lady. She seemed... rather devoted to the suspect...
Zh: “我们在海岸上找到了那个*临时住所*，小姐，甚至还跟女房东聊过了。她好像……对嫌疑人相当忠诚……”

En: Yes, Ruby has her gruff charm.
Zh: “是的，露比的粗鲁也有她自己的魅力。”

En: ...At any rate, the suspect isn't there anymore.
Zh: “……无论如何，嫌疑人已经不在那里了。”

En: I really don't know what to tell you...
Zh: “我真的不知道还能跟你说什么……”

En: She's in dire straits. Could she have hidden in a place to which she has some kind of emotional attachment?
Zh: “她正处于极大的困境之中。她有没有可能藏在某个对她来说有情感依赖的地方呢？”

En: A cornered animal might seek a hiding place that has some sentimental value.
Zh: 走投无路的动物可能会寻找某处有情感价值的藏身之所。

En: Her situation is pretty bad, wherever she is. Maybe she'd hide some place that's meaningful to her?
Zh: “不管她在哪，现在的情况一定很糟糕。也许她会藏在某个对她来说很有意义的地方？”

En: Ruby's certainly passionate, but sentimental -- no, not really... Except maybe about radios -- all kinds of radio technology. So sorry I can't be of any more help to you...
Zh: “露比确实充满热情，不过也很多愁善感——不，也算不上……不过有可能是收音机——各种各样的无线电技术。很抱歉，我也帮不上你多少忙……”

En: I think I found it, but I couldn't get the land lady to talk.
Zh: “我想我找到了，但是我没办法让女房东开口。”

En: Ruby has a gruff charm that inspires loyalty... I do wish you luck in your search, officer.
Zh: “露比有一种粗鲁的魅力，能够激起别人的忠诚……我衷心祝愿你的搜查工作顺顺利利，警官。”

En: Thank you, miss. He turns to you. "We should do a more thorough search of the fishing village."
Zh: “谢谢你，小姐。”他转向你。“我们应该对渔村进行更彻底地搜查。”

En: Thank you, miss. He turns to you. "We should do a thorough search of the fishing village."
Zh: “谢谢你，小姐。”他转向你。“我们应该对渔村进行彻底地搜查。”

En: I guess I should go ask around more in the fishing village.
Zh: “我想我应该再去渔村里四处问问。”

En: I guess I should go ask around the fishing village.
Zh: “我想我应该去渔村里四处问问。”

En: Good luck, officer.
Zh: “祝你好运，警官。”

En: Best of luck to you, officers.
Zh: “祝你们好运，警官。”

En: Ruby is definitely not at her old crash pad anymore.
Zh: “露比肯定已经不在她的老临时住所了。”

En: That doesn't surprise me... She's careful. But -- before you ask -- I really have no clue where she could've gone.
Zh: “我不觉得吃惊……她是个很小心的人。但是——在你问我之前——我真的不知道她可能去了哪里。”

En: I have more questions about the deceased, but I need my partner here before I ask...
Zh: “关于死者，我还有一些问题，不过在我询问之前，我得先把搭档找来……”

En: I have more questions about the deceased, but I need my partner here before I ask...
Zh: “关于死者，我还有一些问题，不过在我询问之前，我得先把搭档找来……”

En: Sure. She smiles, smoke trailing from the cigarette between her fingers. "Do whatever you need to do."
Zh: “当然可以。”她笑了笑，指尖的香烟上升腾起袅袅轻烟。“做你需要做的事吧。”

En: Warming them.
Zh: 为了暖手。

En: She must be very cold. And exhausted of this life.
Zh: 她一定很冷。而且厌倦了这种生活。

En: Warming up your hands relieves stress and anxiety, makes you feel and appear calmer, more comfortable. Seems she's doing that here -- consciously or not.
Zh: 温暖你的双手能够释放压力和焦虑，让你的感觉和样子都变得更加镇定，也更加舒适。似乎她也是这么做的——不管有意还是无意。

En: About a month? Something like that.
Zh: “大概一个月？差不多吧。”

En: A little? Like you partied with the deceased?
Zh: “有一点？就像你跟死者一起派对那样？”

En: I'm sorry to have to ask this, but have you had a physical relationship with any of the Hardie boys?
Zh: “很抱歉，但我必须要问，你跟某位哈迪兄弟有没有过身体上的关系？”

En: Um, how should I put this...
Zh: “嗯，我该怎么说呢……”

En: But have you had sexual relations with any of the Hardie boys?
Zh: “你跟某位哈迪兄弟有没有过性关系？

En: Have I slept with any of them? Yes.
Zh: “我有没有跟他们谁睡过？有。”

En: Get a grip, he thinks. At least do your *personal* stuff when I'm not here.
Zh: 振作起来，他想到。我不在这里的时候，至少要把你*自己*的事做好。

En: Quick! Say something professional.
Zh: 快！说一些专业点的东西。

En: Do you?
Zh: 你有吗？

En: Jackpot!
Zh: 中大奖了！

En: Tearing off his armour, demeaning him, throwing rocks at him...
Zh: 拨下他的盔甲，羞辱他，朝他扔石头……

En: We're not *entirely* convinced about that answer, but okay -- let it slide. Pushing won't help here.
Zh: 我们没有*完全*被那个答案说服，不过也好——听其自然吧。步步紧逼也帮不上什么忙。

En: In my dream, he thinks, he said I've failed his children... you glance at the lieutenant. Then back to her.
Zh: 在我的梦里，他想到，他说我辜负了他的孩子……你瞥了一眼警督。然后又看向她。

En: It's hard to know what to think about that answer.
Zh: 很难知道该如何看待这个答案。

En: She's just tired. Don't push her too much.
Zh: 她只是累了。别把她逼得太紧。

En: I hope I've been... you know... helpful. All things considered.
Zh: “希望我能……你知道的……帮上忙，就各方面而言。”

En: As she says so you feel the young woman looking at you... and get a *feeling* you can't quite put your finger on. A suspicion?
Zh: 正如她所说的，这个年轻女子看着你……有一种*感觉*，你也说不太清楚。一种怀疑？

En: Wait a moment...
Zh: 等一下……

En: She's a glib girl, but she liked the wording.
Zh: 她是个伶牙俐齿的姑娘，不过她喜欢这种措辞。

En: By *they* she means the Hardie boys.
Zh: 她说的*他们*指的是哈迪兄弟。

En: 'Rapeable'? What an unfortunate choice of words. Does she really mean that some people are more...?
Zh: ‘容易被强暴’？这个选词太不成功了。她真的想说有些人会更……？

En: What do you mean by -- 'rapeable'?
Zh: “你说的这个——‘容易被强暴’是什么意思？”

En: Apologies, that didn't come out very well. I meant someone who would go along with the lie.
Zh: “抱歉，这样说不是太好。我的意思是那些附和谎言的人。”

En: Say nothing -- wait for Kim to lead.
Zh: 什么也不说——等待金的引导。

En: It's all very organic -- her mannerisms, her movements. If she's acting, she's quite gifted.
Zh: 这一切全都是有组织的——她的怪癖，她的行动。如果她在演戏，那她真的很有天赋。

En: By *they* she means the Hardie boys.
Zh: 她说的*他们*指的是哈迪兄弟。

En: Are you saying that you were *asked* to tell us you were assaulted?
Zh: “你是说有人*要求*你告诉我们你被侵犯了？”

En: Apologies, that didn't come out very well. I meant someone who would go along with the lie.
Zh: “抱歉，这样说不是太好。我的意思是那些附和谎言的人。”

En: Hold on, what do you mean by -- 'rapeable'?
Zh: “等等，你说的这个——‘容易被强暴’是什么意思？”

En: So you were asked to say you were assaulted? By the Hardie boys?
Zh: “所以有人要求你说自己被侵犯了？是哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: You were asked to say you were assaulted? By the Hardie boys?
Zh: “有人要求你说自己被侵犯了？是哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: Not explicitly, but I understood what they meant. It wouldn't hurt to spice things up a bit -- some assault and battery, *sexual* assault maybe. It was clear the latter would be *spicier*.
Zh: “没有说的很明确，但是我理解他们的意思。把事情搞得有趣一点也无伤大雅——比如人身攻击，也许是*性*侵犯。很明显，后者要更*刺激*一些。”

En: 'Spicier'. Of course. Rape's nothing but a plot device for these guys -- to throw around as they wish.
Zh: ‘更刺激’。当然了。对于这些人来说，强暴不过是一种情节设计——按照他们希望的

En: There are numerous cigarette burns on those silvery scales -- easy to see now that you're closer.
Zh: 那些银色鳞片上有很多香烟烫伤的痕迹——由于你靠的更近了,很容易看出来。

En: Something *difficult*? She raises her eyebrows. "I've been through at least half a dozen difficult things -- which one do you mean?"
Zh: “一些*很艰难*的事情？”她扬起眉毛。“我至少经历过半打艰难的事情——你说的是哪一个？”

En: It's a bit early in the morning for *raped*, isn't it?
Zh: “大清早就聊*强暴*的话题，时间是不是有点太早了？”

En: Were you sexually assaulted, miss?
Zh: “小姐，你受到性侵犯了吗？”

En: Miss, were you sexually assaulted recently?
Zh: “小姐，你最近受到性侵犯了吗？”

En: Have you been raped?
Zh: “你被强暴了吗？”

En: Forget about it. I don't wanna talk about this shit.
Zh: “忘了吧。我不想说这些破事。”

En: Her face lights up with laughter. "I don't wanna talk about *shit* either! What shall we talk about instead?"
Zh: 她的脸被笑容点亮了。“我也不想说这些*破事*！那我们说什么好呢？”

En: By 'sexually assaulted' you mean 'raped'? She takes a quick drag, unperturbed.
Zh: “你说‘性侵犯’是指‘强暴’吗？”她快速地抽了一口烟，样子很镇定。

En: It's not *that* early.
Zh: “也没有*那么*早。”

En: Actually it's already afternoon.
Zh: “其实已经是下午了。”

En: Actually, it's evening, miss.
Zh: “其实已经很晚了，小姐。”

En: And what does that mean? Were you...
Zh: “那是什么意思？你是不是……”

En: She sounds positively *buoyant*. Vivacious. Totally unbothered.
Zh: 她的声音真的很*轻快*。很活跃。完全一副不相干的样子。

En: Is it? Squinting, she takes a look around. The sun sets in the sea; long shadows pass over the plaza below.
Zh: “是吗？”她眯起眼睛，看了看周围。太阳落入海面；长长的阴影笼罩在下面的广场上。

En: Is it? Squinting, she takes a look around. The spring sun is high in the sky; people pass below.
Zh: “是吗？”她眯起眼睛，看了看周围。春日的太阳高高挂在天空上；下面的人群熙熙攘攘。

En: She looks around. The sun has risen over the sea; people are rushing to work below...
Zh: 她看了看周围。太阳已经从海平面上升起；下面的人们忙着赶去工作……

En: It *is* afternoon. She looks into her coffee cup. "Time flies, man."
Zh: “*是*在下午。”她看着咖啡杯。“时光飞逝啊，老兄。”

En: It *is* evening. She looks into her coffee cup. "Time flies, man."
Zh: “*是*在晚上。”她看着咖啡杯。“时光飞逝啊，老兄。”

En: It *is* -- it's murderously early. I'm amazed I'm awake. Why *am* I even awake?
Zh: “*是*的——早地很凶残。我很惊讶自己居然醒了。我为什么*会*醒呢？”

En: Yeah... She draws out the word. "I'm gonna go with *not raped*. I don't wanna say that shit about him."
Zh: “是啊……”她拉长了音节。“我会说那不叫*强暴*。我不想说他的坏话。”

En: It's a bit early in the morning for *raped*.
Zh: “要说*强暴*的话，时间是不是有点太早了？”

En: So were you?
Zh: “那你有吗？”

En: So you weren't?
Zh: “那你没有吗？”

En: Tell them it's not my style. They'll have to, you know -- if they want to jazz up the charges -- they'll have to get someone more... She searches for the word, then shrugs. "Rapeable."
Zh: “告诉他们不是我的风格。你知道的，他们——如果他们想让指控变得更吸引人——他们应该找个更……”她在脑海中搜寻着词汇，然后耸耸肩。“容易被强暴的人。”

En: Yeah... She draws the word out. "I'm gonna go with *not raped*. I can't say that stuff about him."
Zh: “是啊……”她拉长了音节。“我会说那不叫*强暴*。我不能这么说他。”

En: It's all very organic -- her mannerisms, her movements. If she's acting, she's quite gifted.
Zh: 一切都很自然——她的言行举止，她的动作。如果她在演戏，那她真的很有天赋。

En: Titus asked you to spice things up -- for us?
Zh: “提图斯让你把事情弄得再有趣一点——是因为我们吗？”

En: Are you sure you weren't raped?
Zh: “你确定自己没被强暴吗？”

En: What *did* happen between you and the victim?
Zh: “你和被害人之间*到底*发生了什么？”

En: Why was there a bullet in his head?
Zh: “为什么他的脑袋里有一枚子弹？”

En: Are you saying that you were *asked* to tell us you were assaulted?
Zh: “你是说有人*要求*你告诉我们你被侵犯了？”

En: Not explicitly, but I understood what they meant. It wouldn't hurt to spice things up a bit -- some assault and battery, *sexual* assault maybe. It was clear the latter would be *spicier*.
Zh: “没有说的很明确，但是我理解他们的意思。把事情搞得有趣一点也无伤大雅——比如人身攻击，也许是*性*侵犯。很明显，后者要更*刺激*一些。”

En: You were asked to say you were assaulted? By the Hardie boys?
Zh: “有人要求你说自己被侵犯了？是哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: Pretty much. She cradles her coffee cup in both hands.
Zh: “差不多吧。”她用双手捧起咖啡杯。

En: I'm 89% sure.
Zh: “我有89%的肯定。”

En: You know how it is.
Zh: “你知道是怎么回事。”

En: Does that mean you're 11% not sure?
Zh: “那是不是说你有11%的不肯定？”

En: So you're not entirely sure?
Zh: “所以你也不是完全肯定？”

En: Got it. 89% is good enough. Moving on.
Zh: “懂了。89%已经够了。继续吧。”

En: You *do* know how it is.
Zh: 你*的确*知道是怎么回事。

En: Actually I don't.
Zh: “其实，我不知道。”

En: I may.
Zh: “我可能知道。”

En: How is it then?
Zh: “那是怎么回事？”

En: What did they hang him for, if not rape?
Zh: “如果不是强暴的话，他们为什么要把他吊起来？”

En: By *him* she must mean the victim.
Zh: 她说的那个*他*一定是被害人。

En: What can you tell me about him. Name, eyes, age...
Zh: “关于他的事，你能告诉我些什么。名字，眼睛颜色，年龄……”

En: Thank you for telling us all of this, miss. (Conclude.)
Zh: “感谢你告诉我们的一切，小姐。”（总结。）

En: Hmh... She flicks a bit of ash away. "Maybe you don't..." The ash lands on her jumpsuit. She brushes it off.
Zh: “嗯……”她弹了弹烟灰。“也许你不知道……”烟灰落在了她的连体裤上。她伸手把它拍掉了。

En: Do I?
Zh: “我知道吗？”

En: It sounds like something happened, and you don't want to acknowledge it.
Zh: “听起来好像发生了什么，而且你不想承认。”

En: Let me make this 100% clear then, officer. I was not sexually assaulted. She tilts her head. "Would I be as flippant if I had been?"
Zh: “那就让我100%声明一下。我没有被性侵犯。”她歪着头。“如果有过的话，我还会这么轻率吗？”

En: In conclusion, officer -- I'm gonna go with a mild to medium *not raped* here.
Zh: “总之，警官——我有轻度到中度的肯定自己没有*被强暴*。”

En: Sure you do, you disco animal... She cuts you down to size with her light brown eyes.
Zh: “你当然明白了，你这个迪斯科动物……”她用浅棕色的眼睛挫败了你的锐气。

En: Sure you do, apocalypse cop... She cuts you down to size with her light brown eyes.
Zh: “你当然明白了，末日警探……”她用浅棕色的眼睛挫败了你的锐气。

En: Sure you do, detective... She cuts you down to size with her light brown eyes.
Zh: “你当然明白了，警探……”她用浅棕色的眼睛挫败了你的锐气。

En: You *may* know...
Zh: 你*也许*知道……

En: I do.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: Hmh... She flicks a bit of ash away. "Maybe you *don't* know." The ash lands on her jumpsuit. She brushes it off.
Zh: “嗯……”她弹了弹烟灰。“也许你*不知道*。”烟灰落在了她的连体裤上。她伸手把它拍掉了。

En: You get the feeling she might be.
Zh: 你感觉到她可能是的。

En: No one deserves to be raped.
Zh: “没人应该被强暴。”

En: Sexual assault is a serious matter. I need a serious statement from you.
Zh: “性侵犯是一个很严重的事情。我需要你的严肃声明。”

En: Lieutenant, I don't know what to say about this.
Zh: “警督，我不知道该说什么好了。”

En: I'm sure there's *one* person in the world who does. A crooked smile. "I mean there are *so many* people. But let me make this clear: The man who was hanged from that tree did not rape me."
Zh: “我肯定世界上还是*有一个人*应该的吧。”她露出一个狡黠的微笑。“我的意思是说，这个世界上人*这么多*。不过我要声明一点：树上挂着的那个男人没有强暴我。”

En: I'm sure there's *one* person in the world who does. A crooked smile. "I mean, there are *so many* people. But let me make this clear: The man in that tree did not rape me."
Zh: “我肯定世界上还是*有一个人*应该的吧。”她露出一个狡黠的微笑。“我的意思是说，这个世界上人*这么多*。不过我要声明一点：树上挂着的那个男人没有强暴我。”

En: The wording -- a verbal eye roll. She's almost disgusted with the platitude you served up.
Zh: 她的措辞——还有一个白眼。她几乎对你口中的陈词滥调感到有些恶心。

En: Serious... She takes a long, hard drag of her cigarette, then stretches her neck.
Zh: “说真的……”她猛地抽了一大口烟，然后伸长了脖子。

En: You hear cracking in her shoulders.
Zh: 你听见她的肩膀发出了响声。

En: Let me make this 100% clear then, officer. I was not sexually assaulted. Would I be this flippant if I had been?
Zh: “那就让我100%声明一下。我没有被性侵犯。”她歪着头。“如果有过的话，我还会这么轻率吗？”

En: I'm sorry, miss, but we need a definite statement.
Zh: “很抱歉，小姐，但是我们需要一个明确的声明。”

En: Let me make this 100% clear then, officers. I was not sexually assaulted. Would I be this flippant if I had been?
Zh: “那就让我100%声明一下。我没有被性侵犯。”她歪着头。“如果有过的话，我还会这么轻率吗？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: She takes a long, hard drag of her cigarette, then stretches her neck.
Zh: 她猛地抽了一大口烟，然后伸长了脖子。

En: You get the feeling she's done enough opiates -- and needed enough NACRA -- to still be flippant if her arm were cut off and fed to her.
Zh: 你有种感觉，她吃了太多的阿片制剂——还有太多的纳克拉——哪怕胳膊被砍下来塞到她嘴里也动摇不了这种轻浮的态度。

En: You hear cracking in her shoulders.
Zh: 你听见她的肩膀发出了响声。

En: We partied.
Zh: “我们开了个派对。”

En: Cool.
Zh: “真酷。”

En: She sincerely thinks it's very cool of you.
Zh: 她是真心觉得你这样很酷。

En: What kind of partying? (Point to your bloated face.) "The kind I do?"
Zh: “什么类型的派对？”（指着自己浮肿的脸。）“我这种吗？”

En: You mean like a birthday party?
Zh: “你是说像生日派对那种吗？”

En: I don't get it. What do you mean *partied*?
Zh: “我不明白。你说*派对*是什么意思？”

En: Suspect partied with victim. Write it down, Kim.
Zh: “嫌疑人跟被害人开派对。快记下来，金。”

En: You're being sarcastic.
Zh: “你在说风凉话。”

En: I am. Care to elaborate, miss?
Zh: “是的。小姐，能详细说说吗？”

En: No, I'm literally satisfied with that answer.
Zh: “不，我真的对这个答案很满意。”

En: I'm not. He turns to the woman. "What was your relationship with the man they hanged?"
Zh: “我没有。”他转向女子。“你跟那个被吊起来的男人是什么关系？”

En: Yes. She makes a twisting motion with her hands. "We made balloon animals, drank lemonade, inflated a bouncy castle."
Zh: “是的。”她用手做了一个扭转的动作。“我们做了气球动物，喝了柠檬水，还弄一个充气城堡。”

En: Did you rim the lemonade glasses with sugar? I like to do that.
Zh: “你在柠檬水杯的边缘洒了一圈糖吗？我喜欢这样做。”

En: You're being sarcastic. That's not actually how you partied.
Zh: “你在说风凉话。其实你不是这么派对的。”

En: Yeah, I love that too! Her voice is girlishly high-pitched now. "We frosted the glasses, I unwrapped my bike, there was a petting zoo with little white rabbits..."
Zh: “是啊，我也喜欢！”现在她的声音变得像少女一样尖锐起来。“我们冰冻了玻璃杯，我拆开自行車的包装，还有个有小白兔的宠物动物园……”

En: With all due respect, sir, I think we partied a little harder than that.
Zh: “恕我直言，先生。我觉得我们的派对要更加疯狂一些。”

En: *No one* parties harder than me.
Zh: “*没人*能比我玩得更疯狂。”

En: Harder than this? (Keep pointing to your face.) "I didn't know it was physically possible."
Zh: “比这个更疯狂？”（一直指着你的脸。）“我不知道这在肉体上是可能的。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: I would like it to have been that way. But... it wasn't.
Zh: “我也希望是这样的。不过……并不是。”

En: What did you do? When you *partied*?
Zh: “你做了什么？什么时候*派对*的？”

En: It's not, sir. We drank alcohol. A lot of it. We had that effect on each other -- made each other drink harder.
Zh: “不是的，长官。我们喝了酒。很多酒。我们互相影响——让对方喝的更凶。”

En: How was it?
Zh: “那是什么样？”

En: How was it?
Zh: “那是什么样？”

En: We drank, sir... She takes a sip of her coffee. "A lot. For weeks basically. We had that effect on each other -- we made each other drink harder. That's why I liked him."
Zh: “我们喝酒啊，长官……”她抿了一口咖啡。“很多酒。基本上连喝了好几周。我们互相影响——让对方喝的更凶。这就是我喜欢他的理由。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有什么？”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有什么？”

En: Stimulants. Speed also has that effect -- making you drink harder. And then drinking harder makes you do more speed. It's quite the combination. We also had sex.
Zh: “思必得。思必得也有那种效果——让你喝的更凶。喝多了之后又会让你嗑更多思必得。这两是很好的组合。我们还上床了。”

En: You were lovers?
Zh: “你们是情人？”

En: Were feelings involved?
Zh: “带感情的那种吗？”

En: Hold on. After so much drinking and drugs... *how?*
Zh: “等等。喝了那么多酒，嗑了那么多药……*怎么还能做到*？”

En: Please, you're alive and *fifty*! I've known people who party so hard they're dead at 14... She stops to take a short drag.
Zh: “拜托，你还活着，而且*50*多岁了！我知道一些玩得很疯的人，14岁就把自己玩死了……”她停了下来，小抽了一口。

En: What no one does *on your level* is hitting the repeat button, I'll give you that. And *maybe* room-trashing.
Zh: “我可以告诉你，类似你*这种水平*的人，还没有谁能按下重复按钮。把房间搞废*也许*有可能。”

En: What did you do? During these parties.
Zh: “你做了什么？在开派对的时候。”

En: Oh, it is. She takes a long drag. "You're still *alive*."
Zh: “哦，没错。”她抽了一大口烟。“你还*活着*。”

En: I guess you can say that, yes. A bit. Lovers is such an emotional word. But there was something there. We did enough drugs for there to be.
Zh: “我猜可以这么说吧，没错。是有一点。情人这个词太感性。不过确实有点什么。毕竟我们嗑的药已经到位了。”

En: Whoah. After so much drinking *and* drugs -- how did they manage it?
Zh: 哇哦。在喝了那么多酒，*而且*还嗑药之后——他们是怎么做到的？

En: *Untraditionally*, officer. She puts out her Astra, but lights another one with its end first.
Zh: “*非传统*的做法，警官。”她掏出阿斯特拉香烟，不过先用末端又点燃了一根。

En: A little. The drugs were good enough -- and we did enough of them.
Zh: “有一点。毒品已经够好了——而且我们嗑的也很到位。”

En: She's cold and strange. I'm not picking anything up here.
Zh: 她冰冷又奇怪。我什么也没有发现。

En: Your imagination starts whirring, whether you want it to or not.
Zh: 不管你愿不愿意，你开始胡思乱想了。

En: Understood.
Zh: “理解。”

En: Mhm. She looks down at the yard.
Zh: “嗯。”她低头看向院子。

En: How did you two meet? The lieutenant's voice is quiet, calm.
Zh: “你们是怎么遇见的？”警督的声音平静又镇定。

En: Downstairs. She taps on the roof with her 10 cm heel. "At the bar. He was on some sort of assignment -- a military man, as you probably know. Had a cool, scary scar."
Zh: “楼下。”她用10厘米的高跟鞋轻轻拍打着屋顶。“在酒吧。他好像在执行什么任务——你可能也知道了，他是个军人。有一个很酷，很吓人的伤疤。”

En: How did you meet?
Zh: “你们是怎么遇见的？”

En: How long ago was that?
Zh: “那是多久以前的事？”

En: When was this?
Zh: “这是什么时候的事？”

En: A month ago? Something like that.
Zh: “一个月前？差不多吧。”

En: She appears aloof, but that scar part... the *scary* is stressed and drawn out. What's that about? Apprehension?
Zh: 她好像很冷漠，不过伤疤那部分……她强调而且拉长了那句*吓人*。这是怎么回事？恐惧？

En: With longing. She misses him.
Zh: 带着一些渴望。她很想他。

En: It must have been hard for you... (Point to the yard.) "Seeing him there."
Zh: “对你来说一定很难吧……”（指着院子。）“看见他挂在那里。”

En: It must have been hard for you... (Point to the yard.) "Seeing him there."
Zh: “对你来说一定很难吧……”（指着院子。）“看见他挂在那里。”

En: It must be hard for you... (Point to the yard.) "Seeing him there all the time."
Zh: “对你来说一定很难吧……”（指着院子。）“看见他一直挂在那里。”

En: It must be hard... (Point to the yard.) "Seeing him there."
Zh: “一定很难吧……”（指着院子。）“看见他挂在那里。”

En: I'm sorry, I can't do it. She puffs on her cigarette. "Not right now. Later maybe. I keep seeing him. Like he is now. I can't talk about his -- I don't know -- *hair...*" Another puff, more nervous.
Zh: “很抱歉，我做不到。”她抽了一口烟。“现在不行。之后再说吧。我一直看见他。就像他现在还在一样。我不能说他的——我不知道——*头发……*”又抽了一口，更紧张了。

En: A bitter cringe. It *hurts*. You look to the lieutenant...
Zh: 因为痛苦而畏缩。感觉很*疼*。你看向警督……

En: You called us. The RCM...
Zh: “你打给了我们。RCM……”

En: He takes a small step closer.
Zh: 他稍微靠近了一小步。

En: A bitter cringe. It hurts her.
Zh: 因为痛苦而畏缩。让她心痛。

En: Thank you for making the call, miss. It was the right thing to do.
Zh: “感谢你能给我们打电话，小姐。你做了一件正确的事。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: The call -- reporting the hanging? That was you?
Zh: “那通电话——报告吊人的事？是你打的？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: I made it. She nods. "And I would appreciate it if you didn't tell everyone. In Martinaise they call it *snitching*."
Zh: “我打的。”她点点头。“如果你不告诉别人的话，我会很感激的。在马丁内斯，他们把这个叫做*告密*。”

En: I made the call. And I would appreciate it if you didn't tell everyone. In Martinaise they call it *snitching*.
Zh: “电话是我打的。如果你不告诉别人的话，我会很感激的。在马丁内斯，他们把这个叫做*告密*。”

En: Because I couldn't *handle* it anymore. She takes a drag -- her voice thick with disgust. "None of these people called. He just kept hanging there. Then they started stripping him..."
Zh: “因为我实在是*受不了*了。”她抽了一口烟——嗓音透露出浓浓的厌恶。“这里没人打电话。他就一直挂在那里。然后他们开始扒他的衣服……”

En: If it's *snitching* then why do it?
Zh: “如果是*告密*的话，你为什么要这么做呢？”

En: Before we go on -- if it's *snitching* then why do it?
Zh: “在我们继续之前——如果是*告密*的话，你为什么要这么做呢？”

En: The caller's voice was disguised.
Zh: “呼叫者的声音是伪装的。”

En: I didn't exactly *disguise* it. I just muffled the mic and nicked the landline a little.
Zh: “我并没有完全*伪装*。我只是蒙住了话筒，然后稍微割了下地线。”

En: And, in the process, you broke the landline downstairs.
Zh: “所以，在那个过程中，你把楼下的地线弄坏了。”

En: Did I? She looks into her coffee. "Fuck. I didn't mean to. I had no idea what I was doing."
Zh: “我有吗？”她看着手中的咖啡。“该死。我不是有意的。我不知道自己在做什么。”

En: Still, that's pretty clever tampering. Simple and clever, crossing the lines like that.
Zh: 不过，这种篡改手法其实挺聪明的。像那样交叉线路，简单又聪明。

En: Nicked it -- how?
Zh: “割线——怎么弄的？”

En: With nail clippers. And I diverted some radio fuzz into it. Into the cold wire.
Zh: “用指甲钳。而且我把一些无线电杂音转到里面去了。接入冷线。”

En: Seems like you had *some* idea. That was nifty.
Zh: “看来你还*挺有*主意的。干得挺漂亮。”

En: Thanks. She manages a smile.
Zh: “谢谢。”她挤出一个笑容。

En: I don't know, sir. It was stupid. I was drunk too. I was *probably* afraid the Union was listening in -- locals say they have ears in the wires.
Zh: “我不知道，长官。其实挺蠢的。我也喝多了。我*可能*是害怕工会会监听——本地人说他们会监听线路。”

En: Why go through all the trouble?
Zh: “为什么要搞得这么麻烦？”

En: She looks a bit like a little girl who's just been complimented on her bike repair skills.
Zh: 她看起来有点像一个因为自行車修理技术而被表扬的小女孩。

En: I didn't want to, sir. But if I hadn't, he'd *still* be hanging there... She looks away.
Zh: “我也不想的，长官。不过如果我不这么做，他可能*还会*挂在那里……”她移开了目光。

En: I didn't want to, sir. But if I hadn't, he'd *still* be hanging there...
Zh: “我也不想的，长官。不过如果我不这么做，他可能*还会*挂在那里。”

En: He won't be down there long, miss. We will move the body to the morgue soon.
Zh: “他不会在那里停留很久的，小姐的。我们很快会把他的尸体送到陈尸所。”

En: We will move the body to the morgue today, miss. Don't worry.
Zh: “我们今天就会把他的尸体送到陈尸所，小姐。别担心。”

En: I didn't want to, sir. And he's *still* hanging there... She looks away.
Zh: “我也不想的，长官。而且他*还*挂在那里……”她移开了目光。

En: You wonder... is it enough to...
Zh: 你在想……现在足够……

En: It hurts her. But does it hurt enough to...
Zh: 这让她很心痛。不过是不是足够心痛……

En: Did you make the call?
Zh: “是你打的电话吗？”

En: Don't say it.
Zh: 别说出来。

En: She hunches her shoulders a bit, making herself smaller.
Zh: 她微微耸起肩膀，让她显得更加娇小了。

En: Reporting crimes is confidential in Revachol, miss.
Zh: “小姐，报告犯罪行为在瑞瓦肖是保密的。”

En: The voice was disguised, he asks, clearly wishing to distract her from the images. "Did you?..."
Zh: “声音是伪装过的，”他问到，明显是想把她的注意力从那些画面里转移出来。“你是不是……”

En: So that's why the phone is out of order. She tampered with the Whirling landline!
Zh: 所以这就是电话发生故障的原因。她篡改了飞旋旅社的地线！

En: Jigsaw falling into place. This is satisfying. Good catch, I appreciate the symmetry.
Zh: 拼图逐渐回归原位。让人感觉挺满意的。做得好，我很欣赏这种对称性。

En: Which landline?
Zh: “哪条地线？”

En: The one downstairs, officer. I used the Whirling landline.
Zh: “楼下的那个，警官。我用的是飞旋旅社的地线。”

En: That's some clever tampering. Simple and clever, crossing the lines like that.
Zh: 这种篡改手法其实挺聪明的。像那样交叉线路，简单又聪明。

En: Don't mention it.
Zh: 别提了。

En: She must have had training of some kind...
Zh: 她肯定接受过某种训练……

En: Again -- how do the Hardie boys know you?
Zh: “再说一遍——哈迪兄弟是怎么认识你的？”

En: How do the Hardie boys know you?
Zh: “哈迪兄弟是怎么认识你的？”

En: I know it's difficult, miss. We can return to it later.
Zh: “我知道这很难，小姐。我们之后再回来。”

En: She meant she sees him in her dreams.
Zh: 她的意思是说在梦里看见他了。

En: I've also seen him in a dream.
Zh: “我也在梦里见到他了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: You *pissed* it all away... All of it. Elysium.
Zh: 你把它全都*挥霍*掉了……所有的一切。极乐世界。

En: You have? Not like I do, I imagine. She's run out of cigarette -- time to light a new one.
Zh: “你有吗？我想，应该不像我这样吧。”她的烟快抽完了——是时候点一支新的了。

En: She sees them in each other's arms.
Zh: 她看见他两拥抱在一起。

En: How do you see him?
Zh: “你是怎么看见他的？”

En: You're in each other's arms.
Zh: “你们抱在一起。”

En: I see him as *me*.
Zh: “我把他看作是*我自己*。”

En: You don't wanna know. She looks into the pack -- it's light green -- then pulls out a ciggy.
Zh: “你不会想知道的。”她看着包装盒——它是浅绿色的——然后抽出一根香烟。

En: Yes. And then he's dead. His mouth agape... She looks into the light green pack of cigarettes and pulls out another one, preparing to light it.
Zh: “是的。然后他死了。嘴巴张得很大……”她看着浅绿色包装盒里的香烟，从里面又抽出来一根，准备点燃。

En: Agape? Ready to catch a bullet? A coincidence? Make a note and return to it later.
Zh: 张大嘴巴？准备接子弹吗？一个巧合？先记下来，之后再研究。

En: She dips the cigarette in the lighter's flame and inhales, then looks at you -- with her lungs full of smoke.
Zh: 她把香烟伸进打火机的火焰里，吸了一口，然后看着你——肺里充满香烟。

En: I can see the similarity, yes. She breathes out, through her nostrils. The air smells of menthol.
Zh: “是的，我能看出相似的地方。”她呼出一口气，香烟从鼻孔里喷了出来。空气里满是薄荷的味道。

En: Funny, the lieutenant says softly.
Zh: “有意思，”警督轻声说到。

En: Funny how?
Zh: “怎么有意思？”

En: Nothing. He nods toward the yard. "I also saw him. We had a long inspection and that sort of thing... sticks with you. Let's move on."
Zh: “没什么。”他朝着院子点了点头。“我也能看见他。我们的调查持续了很长时间，还有诸如此类的事情……跟你有关系的事情。我们还是继续吧。”

En: He had something to do with the strike. One has been roiling since I got here. Rotten timing... She thinks. "But you probably know all about it."
Zh: “他跟罢工有些关系。我来这里之后，一直动荡不安。真是挑了个坏时间……”她想到。“不过你可能已经全都知道了。”

En: And his role in this strike was... what?
Zh: “那他在罢工里扮演的角色……是什么？”

En: And his role in this strike was... what?
Zh: “那他在罢工里扮演的角色……是什么？”

En: I think he was in a security detail. He was ex-military. Worked for Wild Pines -- and against the Union. We didn't discuss work much, if you know what I mean. But I understood it was dangerous.
Zh: “我觉得他是安保人员。他是个退伍军人。为野松公司工作——跟工会是对抗关系。我们聊的不多，如果你明白我是什么意思。但是我能理解这份工作很危险。”

En: And they lynched him for it?
Zh: “他们对他处以私刑就是这个原因？”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: I think he was in a security detail. He was ex-military. Worked for Wild Pines; against the Union. We didn't discuss work much, if you know what I mean. But I understood it was dangerous.
Zh: “我觉得他是安保人员。他是个退伍军人。为野松公司工作——跟工会是对抗关系。我们聊的不多，如果你明白我是什么意思。但是我能理解这份工作很危险。”

En: And you think they lynched him for it?
Zh: “他们对他处以私刑就是这个原因？”

En: Bullet? There's a silence. Her brows meet in the middle, for a pained frown. "They *shot* him too?"
Zh: “子弹？”一阵沉默。她的两条眉毛皱了起来，很痛苦的样子。“他们还朝他*开枪*了？”

En: I'm not picking up any theatre-craft here, sir. The pause is sincere.
Zh: 吾辈没有发现有戏剧表演的成分，大人。这个停顿是真诚的。

En: They stripped his clothes *and* they shot him...
Zh: “他们扒了他的衣服，*而且*还开枪打他……”

En: You mean *after* they hanged him?
Zh: “你的意思是说在他们把他吊起来*之后*？”

En: I'm confused. Sorry.
Zh: “我很困惑。抱歉。”

En: So am I. Were you aware that he had also been shot, miss?
Zh: “我也是。你知道他还中了一枪吗，小姐？”

En: Things are starting to go a little over my head here... I thought he was hanged? I was not *present* when they did it. I don't know what happened. I just know what they told me -- and Sylvie, the bartender.
Zh: “我开始有点无法理解整件事了……他不是被吊死的吗？他们动手的时候我*不在场*。我不知道发生了什么。我只知道他们告诉我——还有酒保塞尔维的事。”

En: They're frequent guests. Downstairs. She looks at the floor. It's tarred. "They have a booth for Union members. They're probably down there now."
Zh: “他们是楼下的常客。”她看着地板。上面铺了一层焦油。“他们这里有一个为工会成员准备的包厢。人现在就有可能在那里。”

En: And how did you meet?
Zh: “你们是怎么认识的？”

En: Over drinks. It's been a long, boring winter.
Zh: “喝酒。这个冬天很漫长，而且还很无聊。”

En: Did you *party*?
Zh: “跟他们*派对*过吗？”

En: A little, yeah.
Zh: “有一点，没错。”

En: Like you partied with the deceased?
Zh: “就像你跟死者一起派对那样？”

En: No. Not as hard.
Zh: “不。没那么激烈。”

En: I have.
Zh: “有过。”

En: Which one?
Zh: “哪一个？”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白。”

En: Which *ones*, sir -- I don't remember precisely. Titus, obviously. But as I said, it's been a long winter.
Zh: “是哪*几个*，先生——我记不太清了。很明显，有提图斯。不过我也说过了，这是个漫长的冬季。”

En: She sips her coffee. Birds circle overhead.
Zh: 她抿了一口咖啡。小鸟在头顶盘旋。

En: Could that have been part of the reason they hanged him?
Zh: 他们把他吊起来会不会也有这方面的原因？

En: There *must* be more to it. You should return to this topic after you've talked about her relationship with the deceased, maybe?
Zh: *肯定*远不止这些。也许在你询问她跟死者的关系之后，应该再回到这个话题？

En: Like I said, we met over drinks.
Zh: “我说过了，我们是喝酒认识的。”

En: Could that be why they lynched him? Jealousy?
Zh: “他们就是因为这个对他处以私刑的吗？因为妒忌？”

En: Don't mention it.
Zh: 别提了。

En: I hope not. A pause. "Actually, I *know* that's not the reason. I'm careful about that kind of thing. Not crossing the wires, you know. But that's probably where they got the rape idea."
Zh: “希望不是。”她停顿了一下。“其实，我*知道*不是这个原因。我对这种事情一向很小心。不会越线，你明白的。这可能也是他们想到强暴的理由吧。”

En: Men like that? I don't know... Another pause. "It's the way their imaginations work. I suspect it's what they'd like to do to me."
Zh: “那种男人？我不知道……”又停顿了一下。“是他们的想象力在作祟。我怀疑这是他们想对我做的事。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Don't be tactful. She doesn't care.
Zh: 不用这么圆滑。她不在乎的。

En: She changes balance from one foot to another, crossing her legs.
Zh: 她换了一只脚保持平衡，双腿交叉。

En: You mean *after* they hanged him?
Zh: “你是说在他们把他吊起来*之后*吗？”

En: I'm confused. Sorry.
Zh: “我有点困惑。抱歉。”

En: Were you aware that he was also shot, miss? In the mouth?
Zh: “你知道他还中枪了吗，小姐？在嘴里？”

En: She breathes a silvery sigh of relief. And weariness. The air on the roof feels humid.
Zh: 她如释重负地呼出一口气。还有疲惫。屋顶上的空气感觉有些潮湿。

En: Should we head back downstairs, officer? The lieutenant looks at you. "We may have things to discuss there."
Zh: “我们应该到楼下去，警官？”警督看着你。“我们可能有些事要讨论一下。”

En: How about we, you know, change the subject to something more light-hearted now?
Zh: “不如我们现在，你明白的，换个更轻松一点的话题？”

En: Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: I had something else. Before we go. A little thing.
Zh: “在我们离开之前。我还有点别的事。一点小事。”

En: She nods. Silvery cigarette fumes disappear into her mouth...
Zh: 她点点头。银色的烟雾消失在她口中……

En: Not my favourite topic... She flicks the ash away. "But okay."
Zh: “不是我最喜欢的话题……”她弹了弹烟灰。“不过也可以。”

En: Yes, you're just one room away. She pours herself more coffee. "Very personal."
Zh: “是的，你就在我隔壁的房间。”她给自己又倒了一点咖啡。“确实很私人。”

En: Were you in Sunday night? I need to know what I did before I lost my memory.
Zh: “你周日晚上在吗？我需要知道在我失忆之前，我都做了些什么。”

En: It prides me to hear this. Tell me there was more.
Zh: “听你这么说让我很自豪。告诉我，是不是还有更多。”

En: That's all. Just wanted to establish that.
Zh: “就这些。只想证实一下。”

En: There was the usual ruckus, she nods. "Loud disco music."
Zh: “只是寻常的喧闹，”她点点头。“很大声的迪斯科音乐。”

En: Did I have any visitors?
Zh: “有人来找过我吗？”

En: Yes. It was very cool.
Zh: “是的。的确挺酷的。”

En: I went out afterwards, everything was quiet by then. Around four or five. She nods. "And that was it."
Zh: “之后我就出去了，那时候一切都很安静。大概是4点或者5点钟的样子吧。”她点点头。“就这些了。”

En: I can't say -- probably not. Sounded like you were flying solo.
Zh: “我说不好——也许不是。听起来你是一个人。”

En: Then you started screaming and trashed the place.
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，然后把地方搞得乱七八糟。”

En: Yeah, that's all I need to hear of this.
Zh: “是啊，我只要听到这些就够了。”

En: You mentioned loud disco music?
Zh: “你提到大声的迪斯科音乐？”

En: Oh yes, various artists. Ostentatious Orchestrations prime among them. She arches her eyebrow, waiting for it to connect with you.
Zh: “是啊，很多歌手的。里面还有招摇乐团全盛时期的作品。”她弯起眉毛，等待你想起些什么。

En: Please tell me there wasn't anything *else*.
Zh: “请告诉我没有*别的*什么了。”

En: There was. I think you screamed that you... didn't want to be this type of animal anymore. I may have misheard, but it was sort of memorable.
Zh: “有的。我想你在尖叫……说你不想再做这样的动物了。我可能听错了，不过确实有点让人难忘。”

En: Oh, *that*. Yeah, whooh... the less said about OO, the better.
Zh: 哦，*那个*。是啊，哈……关于招摇乐团的话题，越少越好。

En: I don't know about that... at around two o'clock the disco stopped and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “那我就不知道了……大概2点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停了，换了一种口味。”

En: OO were *huge* where I come from. I was very young then, of course. Like seven. 'Life gets haaard!' she says, half-singing. "'But we go ooooooon!'"
Zh: “招摇乐团在我的家乡可是*超级巨星*。当然了，那时候我还很年轻。大概7岁吧。生活不易！”她一半在说，一半在唱。“但我们仍要继续。”

En: Then what happened?
Zh: “然后发生了什么？”

En: When you say *wounded* do you mean that in a *cool* way? Like a *wild beast*?
Zh: “你说的*受伤*，是不是挺*酷*的那种？比如说一头*野兽*？”

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “很抱歉。”

En: Then, doesn't life get hard *because* we go on?
Zh: 不过，难道不是*因为*我们一直在继续，生活才会变得艰难吗？

En: Yeah, we go on alright.
Zh: “是啊，我们的确要继续。”

En: It mostly just gets hard, doesn't it?
Zh: “基本上只是变得更艰难了，不是吗？”

En: Maybe we should stop going on then? Doesn't the 'going on' cause the 'getting harder' part?
Zh: “那也许我们不应该继续？难道不是‘继续’才导致‘变得艰难’的吗？”

En: Was there anything else?
Zh: “还有别的事吗？”

En: What happened then?
Zh: “然后发生了什么？”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I think you might have come to the same conclusion last night. At around two o'clock the disco stopped and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “我觉得昨晚你可能也得出了相同的结论。大概两点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停了，然后换了一种口味。”

En: A slow, sad song started playing. Like organ music, on repeat. That went on for quite a while. Some of that time you were yelling along to it.
Zh: “是一首舒缓又悲伤的歌。像是风琴音乐，一遍遍重复播放。持续了很长一段时间。有时候你还会跟着一起大喊大叫。”

En: It would appear so. At around two o'clock the disco stopped and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “看起来像是这样。大概两点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停了，然后换了一种口味。”

En: Yes! There was a church in there. A really small church. Like the smallest, saddest church in the whole world. It was about that. And also...
Zh: “是的！歌词里有个教堂。一个真正的小教堂。好像是整个世界上最小、最悲伤的教堂。好像说的就是那个。而且……”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有什么？”

En: Don't be, I was going out later anyway. It didn't bother me.
Zh: “不用，反正我晚些时候也要出去。没有打扰到我。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: It's impossible to say whether she's being sarcastic or really means it.
Zh: 说不上来她到底是在挖苦你，还是真心这么想。

En: Are you sure I wasn't being assaulted?
Zh: “你确定我没有被袭击吗？”

En: You're making this up. I would never behave like that.
Zh: “你瞎编的。我绝对不会那样做的。”

En: No, it didn't sound like there was a fight. It sounded like someone was trashing their room. She takes a sip of her coffee and smiles.
Zh: “不，听起来没有打斗的声音。好像是有人在房间里发疯。”她抿了一口咖啡，笑了。

En: That's so *me*. What did I do?
Zh: “那真的挺像*我*的风格。我做了什么？”

En: What was I singing?
Zh: “我唱的是什么？”

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我已经听够了。”

En: Was I singing this? (Show her the tape of "The Smallest Church In Saint-Saëns".)
Zh: “我是在唱这个吗？”（把“圣桑小教堂”的空盒子拿给她看。）

En: Was I singing this? (Show her the empty case of "The Smallest Church In Saint-Saëns".)
Zh: “我是在唱这个吗？”（把“圣桑小教堂”的空盒子拿给她看。）

En: That it doesn't *matter* anymore. And that we are *alone* now. It was difficult to tell, the song itself was very quiet and soft, but you sounded like a wounded boar, sir. It was hard to understand what you were singing on top of it.
Zh: “这已经*不重要*了。现在*只有我们自己*。很难说，那首歌本身安静又舒缓，但是你的声音就像一头受伤的野猪，先生。很难理解你在唱些什么。”

En: Yes. She looks at you. "Never."
Zh: “是的。”她看着你。“永远不要了。”

En: A window was smashed. The tape player probably -- the song stopped -- and furniture too. A real *destructothon*. There was screaming. Then, I think, you passed out.
Zh: “一扇窗户被砸碎了。录音机可能——歌曲停了——还有家具。一场真正的*破坏*。有尖叫声。然后，我觉得，你晕过去了。”

En: Funky.
Zh: “满特别的。”

En: I was. She takes a sip. "It was the night before I saw you in the hallway -- and reminded you you're a police officer."
Zh: “有啊。”她抿了一口咖啡。“就在我在走廊里看见你的前一晚——而且还提醒你，你是个警察。”

En: Yes. Before I emerged like paleolithic megafauna.
Zh: “是的。在我像旧石器时代的巨兽一样出现之前。”

En: It's one of the first things I remember doing in Elysium.
Zh: “这是我能记起的在这个世界上做的第一件事。”

En: Did you... hear something? Sunday night -- from my room? (Move on.)
Zh: “你有没有……听到什么？周日晚上——我的房间？”（继续。）

En: The date of your re-entry into the fossil record.
Zh: 你重新进入化石记录的日期。

En: Wow, she nods. "That's some creation myth stuff right there."
Zh: “哇哦，”她点点头。“这还真像是创世神话什么的呢。”

En: Wow, she nods. "Elysium. You don't hear that term often."
Zh: “哇哦，”她点点头。“极乐世界。这个词可不常听见。”

En: Wow, she nods. "Mythopoetically adequate stuff."
Zh: “哇哦，”她点点头。“挺神话的东西。”

En: Before you was only the room -- the sound of the motor vehicle. Steam in the bathroom. And darkness.
Zh: “在你之前只有那个房间——汽車的声音。浴室里的水蒸气。还有黑暗。”

En: Oh... The woman makes a sad face.
Zh: “哦……”女人露出一个悲伤的表情。

En: Ha! Yes. My pleasure. She smiles. "Where did we leave off... hell, let's just take it from the top. You start."
Zh: “哈！没错。我很荣幸。”她笑了。“我们讲到哪了……见鬼，还是从头开始吧。你先开始。”

En: A gleeful smile. She's pretty happy that your partner's not here.
Zh: 一个愉快的笑容。她很高兴你的搭档不在这里。

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: No problem, sir. She feeds herself another cigarette.
Zh: “没关系，先生。”她又抽了一口烟。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: She feeds herself another cigarette.
Zh: 她又抽了一口烟。

En: As he says so you feel the young woman looking at you... and get a *feeling* you can't quite put your finger on. A suspicion?
Zh: 正如他所说的，这个年轻女子看着你……有一种*感觉*，你也说不太清楚。一种怀疑？

En: Wait a moment...
Zh: 等一下……

En: Where were you when this happened?
Zh: “这一切发生的时候，你在哪里？”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: This is beginning to get *confusing* for you too and we don't like that. Where was she last Sunday night?
Zh: 你也开始感到*困惑*了，我们不喜欢这样。上周日晚上她在哪里？

En: If we're letting things *be* out of courtesy -- why did you even dig the bullet out in the first place? But fine...
Zh: 如果只是因为出于礼貌就这样*坐视不管*——那你为什么一开始还要把子弹挖出来呢？不过，好吧……

En: Cowering. I was cowering downstairs with Sylvie.
Zh: “在害怕。在楼下，跟塞尔维在一起。”

En: Soft, light brown eyes look back at you, directly into the space behind your eye sockets. You see the smoke rise from between her painted red lips. She's beautiful...
Zh: 她用浅棕色的眼睛温和地看着你，直直地看向你眼眶背后的空间。你看见烟雾从她的两瓣红唇上升起。她很漂亮……

En: She looks back, time moves slowly. The triangles of her face rearranging into a weary smile...
Zh: 她回头望去，时间过得很慢。她脸上的三角形重新组合成一个疲惫的微笑……

En: Don't worry. We will protect you from her beauty. We will *consult* you through the reefs and sounds of her persona.
Zh: 别担心。我们会保护你不被她的美貌影响。我们会作为你的*顾问*，引导你穿越她伪装中的暗礁与海峡。

En: We will see through deceits. You are shielded. You are wise.
Zh: 我们会看穿那些谎言。你被保护好了。你很聪明。

En: You are advised. There are muscles on long white bones that line her limbs, just below the silver jumpsuit...
Zh: 我们给了你建议。她四肢那修长的白色骨骼上附着着肌肉，就在那身银色连体裤下……

En: Miss Oranje Disco Dancer... You want to put your hand on her back and feel it arch.
Zh: 奥兰治小姐，迪斯科舞者——你想把手放在她的后背上，感觉上面的弧度。

En: What is... happening?
Zh: 发生……什么了？

En: Am I being... beguiled?
Zh: 我是……入迷了吗？

En: Avert your eyes.
Zh: 移开你的视线。

En: Nothing. Just time passing. Don't worry.
Zh: 没什么。只是消磨时光。别担心。

En: You are not a *fool*.
Zh: 你不是个*傻瓜*。

En: Anything out of the ordinary and you would be notified.
Zh: 要是有什么不寻常的事情，你会注意到的。

En: She presses her elbows against her waist and slowly turns her head.
Zh: 她把胳膊肘抵在腰间，慢慢转过头。

En: Her hair brushes her shoulders, making a small hissing sound, almost imperceptible...
Zh: 她的头发扫过她的肩膀，发出一阵轻微的咝咝声，几乎很难察觉到……

En: The strange moment ends. It was brief, no longer than 2.2 seconds.
Zh: 这个奇怪的时刻结束了。它很短，不超过2.2秒。

En: Just like the last time, light brown eyes look back at you. All is as it was, you feel cigarette fumes rise from her lips. She's just as beautiful...
Zh: 就像上次一样，浅棕色的眼睛回头望向你。一切如常，你感觉香烟从她的嘴唇间升起。她还是那么漂亮……

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: You know these guys?
Zh: 你了解这些家伙吗？

En: Yes, you. He's talking about you, you boring stiff.
Zh: 是的，你。他说的就是你，无聊又死板的家伙。

En: Who, me?
Zh: 谁，我吗？

En: I have bad news for you.
Zh: 我有坏消息要告诉你。

En: You too.
Zh: 你也是。

En: Me? What did I do? I'm merely a master thespian...
Zh: 我？我做了什么？吾辈只不过是戏剧大师而已……

En: Who, me?
Zh: 谁，我吗？

En: Me? Get outta here, I'm solid.
Zh: 我？少来了，我很可靠的。

En: You too.
Zh: 你也是。

En: Yes, you. He's talking about you, you grovelling sycophant.
Zh: 是的，你。他说的就是你，你这个卑躬屈膝的马屁精。

En: Who, me?
Zh: 谁，我吗？

En: Yes, he's talking about you, you sinewy idiot.
Zh: 是的，你。他说的就是你，你这个筋肉白痴。

En: Me? What have I done this time?
Zh: 我吗？我这次又做什么了？

En: You too.
Zh: 你也是。

En: Who, me?
Zh: 谁，我吗？

En: Me? No way, I'm straight, man.
Zh: 我？不可能，我是直的，兄弟。

En: You too.
Zh: 你也是。

En: Yes, he's talking about you, you shifty asshole.
Zh: 是的，他说的就是你，你这个狡猾的混账。

En: Who, me?
Zh: 谁，我吗？

En: Yes, you. He's talking about you, you boring stiff.
Zh: 是的，你。他说的就是你，无聊又死板的家伙。

En: Me? What did I do?
Zh: 我？我做什么了？

En: You too.
Zh: 你也是。

En: These guys are compromised. She's got them singing along to her tune. The little bleeps and bloops you trust for info -- you can't trust them anymore.
Zh: 这些家伙已经妥协了。她让他们都跟着她唱一个调子。你信任的那些小哔哔声和杂音——你不能再相信他们了。

En: Oh my god.
Zh: 我的天。

En: I knew something was off...
Zh: 我就知道出事了……

En: Believe it.
Zh: 相信它。

En: I'm sorry I didn't catch it sooner. It takes conscious effort on your part.
Zh: 很抱歉我没能早点发现。你那部分要付出意识上的努力。

En: Bullshit, man, I ain't *compromised*.
Zh: 都是屁话，老兄，我没有*妥协*。

En: *Especially* that guy. That guy's the most compromised one in here.
Zh: *尤其*是那个家伙。那家伙是所有人里面最妥协的一个。

En: Which ones exactly are affected?
Zh: 到底是哪些个被影响了？

En: There's no way of knowing. At the moment I'm afraid it's best to assume...
Zh: 没办法知道的。目前恐怕最好假设……

En: Really? Quick, tell me what's under her jumpsuit?
Zh: 真的吗？快，告诉我她的连体裤下面有什么？

En: How did this happen?
Zh: 怎么会这样？

En: How it always does -- through subtlety.
Zh: 总是这样——通过细微的地方。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: ...*all* of them.
Zh: ……他们*全部*。

En: No fucking way, man, I just want a drag of that sweet Menthol ziggie.
Zh: 他妈的不可能，老兄，我只想来上一口那种甜蜜的薄荷味。

En: GLORY. TRUTH. SOFTNESS. PROTECT HER. SHE WANTS YOU.
Zh: 荣誉。真实。温柔。保护她。她想要你。

En: I think it's safe to assume: yes. Mr. Thespian here hasn't been speaking up. If he were, I suspect there would be paeans to her truthfulness. Like this...
Zh: 我觉得这样假设比较可靠：没错。演员先生一直没有开口说话。如果他是的话，我怀疑会有人为她的坦率唱起赞美歌。就像这样……

En: Does this mean she's been lying to me?
Zh: 这是不是意味着她在对我撒谎？

En: Yes. Mr. Thespian here has been singing paeans to how truthful she is...
Zh: 是的。演员先生一直在为她的真实唱赞美歌……

En: She isth a laedy most fair and juste!
Zh: 她是最为美丽和公正的女人！

En: Can't you turn them normal again?
Zh: 你不能把他们变回正常的样子吗？

En: What is her *plan*?
Zh: 她有什么*计划*？

En: You can't draw a sound conclusion. The one who usually does says...
Zh: 你没办法得出一个合理的结论。通常这样做的人会说……

En: She may want to control the information roll-out -- not to become a suspect. She may have a past she's escaping, unrelated to this case. You doubt it's something truly insidious.
Zh: 她可能想要控制信息的发布——不让自己成为嫌疑人。她可能想要逃避自己的过去，不过跟案件没有什么联系。你怀疑那是一些真正阴狠的东西。

En: (Don't say anything.) Just close your eyes and let the moment pass.
Zh: （什么也别说。）闭上眼睛，让这一刻过去。

En: (Say it out loud.) "Miss, are you manipulating me?"
Zh: （大声说出来。）“小姐，你是想操纵我吗？”

En: For a second her face disappears from your cornea. Only a silvery negative remains.
Zh: 有那么一会儿，她的脸从你的角膜上消失了。只留下一张银色的底片。

En: Still smiling a tired smile.
Zh: 还是那个疲惫的笑容。

En: No.
Zh: 不能。

En: What use is this then?
Zh: 那搞这一出有什么用呢？

En: It's better to know you're being played than to be played without knowing it, is it not?
Zh: 知道自己被耍总比不知道的好，对吧？

En: In his defence -- to reduce him to such inadequacy, she probably had to employ half-truths more often than outright lies.
Zh: 如果要为他辩护的话——为了让他变成这幅没用的模样，她可能经常要说些半真半假的话，而不是彻头彻尾的谎言。

En: That is correct. And omissions too.
Zh: 没错。而且还有些遗漏。

En: Can I trust that guy?
Zh: 我能相信那家伙吗？

En: Can I trust any of them *ever again?*
Zh: 我*还能*相信他们吗？

En: A little. They're all still of limited use, interpreting things to the best of their ability. Maybe they add flair or something? I wouldn't know. I don't add flair.
Zh: 一点点。他们的用途还是有限的，只能尽其所能地把事情解释清楚。也许他们可以增加一些鉴别力或者什么的？我也不知道。我不用增加鉴别力。

En: But when it comes to assessments of character and factual accuracy they are not to be trusted. Not with her.
Zh: 但是提到评估性格和事实准确性的时候，就不能相信他们了。反正跟她在一起的时候不行。

En: Okay.
Zh: 好吧。

En: Don't be melodramatic. You can trust them. Just not with Miss Oranje Disco Dancer here.
Zh: 不用这么夸张。你可以相信他们。只是跟这位*奥兰治迪斯科舞者*在一起的时候不行。

En: Don't be melodramatic. You can trust them. Just not with *her*.
Zh: 不用这么夸张。你可以相信他们。只是跟*她*在一起的时候不行。

En: A light green speck, and imperfection, on the outer rim of her right iris. It sparkles...
Zh: 她右眼虹膜的外缘有一个浅绿色的斑点和瑕疵。它在闪闪发光……

En: Good. The less paranoid option. You can trust them. Just not with Klaasje Amandou.
Zh: 很好。不那么偏执的选择。你可以相信他们。只是跟卡拉洁·阿曼多在一起的时候不行。

En: Good. The less paranoid option. You can trust them. Just not with *her*.
Zh: 很好。不那么偏执的选择。你可以相信他们。只是跟*她*在一起的时候不行。

En: ...no? He's not going to show up? I'm sorry, your lie detection isn't working. It's not her doing, he's just totally inept. It looks like you're also an idiot. But that's not her fault.
Zh: ……没有吗？他不打算出现吗？很抱歉，你的测谎系统不能正常工作了。不是她干的，只是他太无能了。看来你也是一个白痴。不过这不是她的错。

En: No, not an idiot. You're just more of a *sensitive* type. Thinks with his *heart*. This woman's *pain* draws you in.
Zh: 不，不是个白痴。你只是一个比较*感性*的人。用他的*心*去思考。这个女人的*痛苦*吸引着你。

En: See? It's oddly moderate. Probably compromised.
Zh: 看见了吗？这种节制太奇怪了。他很可能已经妥协了。

En: Huh... no, Mr. Conclusion. You're always kind of limited in your analytical abilities. That's not *her* fault. But still...
Zh: 嗯……不，结论先生。你的分析能力总是有些受限的感觉。这不是*她*的错。不过……

En: ...nothing? Someone else was supposed to chime in, weren't they? And then they didn't -- just me swooshing around in here now. Swoosh!
Zh: ……什么都没有？应该有其他人插嘴的，不是吗？但是他们没有——现在只有我这里到处晃荡。嗖嗖！

En: The silence broken, she exhales a little cloud of smoke and says...
Zh: 沉默被打破了，她吐出一小团烟雾，然后说到……

En: God, no!
Zh: “天啦，我没有！”

En: I take it back. He's got it pretty bad, but this *next guy's* on another level entirely...
Zh: 我收回。他的情况很糟糕，但是*下一个家伙*完全是另一个水平……

En: She likes you. The Crownhead is a boring condom. He's jealous. This is human nature.
Zh: 她喜欢你。皇冠头是个无聊的怂货。他在妒忌。这是人的天性。

En: Air moves in your windpipe. Your heart beats. You're a detective -- get back to detecting.
Zh: 空气在你的气管里流动。你的心砰砰跳动着。你是一个警探——继续回去侦查吧。

En: What can I do?
Zh: 我能做什么？

En: There's nothing you can do about it. You are how you are -- and she is how she is. Things will go as they do.
Zh: 你没什么能做的。你就是你——她就是她。事情会照着原样发展的。

En: You are *not* afraid. This is a mere girl, she was someone's child but a decade ago.
Zh: 你*不*害怕。她只是一个小女孩，十年前她只是别人的孩子。

En: I'm sorry, but we're not quite done here yet. He turns to you... "We have more questions. Better to be thorough now than have to come back to this later."
Zh: “很抱歉，但是我们还没说完呢。”他转向你……“我们还有一些问题。最好现在就彻底调查清楚，总好过之后又要再回来。”

En: So *she's* Garte's mystery phone cutter!
Zh: 所以*她*就是加尔特口中那个神秘的电话杀手！

En: You were right to suspect there might be foul play involved with the broken phone line!
Zh: 你怀疑电话线故障可能跟违规行为有关是对的！

En: Okay. What should we talk about now? She takes a drag of her cigarette.
Zh: “好吧。那现在我们应该说些什么呢？”她抽了一口烟。

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我听够了。”

En: Well, thank you for making the call anyway.
Zh: “不管怎么说，感谢你的那通电话。”

En: At least you called.
Zh: “至少你打电话了。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Nod.
Zh: 点点头。

En: Wait... *partied*. Where have I heard that before?
Zh: “等等……*派对*。我之前在哪里听过来着？”

En: A lot of partying going on.
Zh: “有很多派对正在进行。”

En: From Titus. About her and Titus' relationship -- that's where you heard it.
Zh: 提图斯说的。关于她和提图斯的关系——你就是从那里听到的。

En: Oh yes, she says bitterly. "I've had a great view. From the roof, out of the bathroom window. In my dreams..."
Zh: “是的，”她的声音有些苦涩。“我有一片很好的视野。在屋顶上，浴室窗户外面。在我的梦里……”

En: Oh yes, she says bitterly. "I have *multiple* viewpoints. From the roof, out of the bathroom window. In my dreams..."
Zh: “是的，”她的声音有些苦涩。“我有*很多*观察点。屋顶上，浴室窗户外面。在我的梦里……”

En: You should be wary of what this guy likes. He's easily duped by his love of jigsaw puzzles.
Zh: 你应该留神这家伙喜欢什么。他很容易被自己对拼图游戏的热爱所欺骗。

En: A risky move, altogether.
Zh: “总而言之，这是个很冒险的举动。”

En: Better conclude this part of our talk.
Zh: “最好先结束这部分的谈话。”

En: Oh yes. The young woman dips her cigarette into the lighter's flame and inhales.
Zh: “是的。”年轻女子把香烟凑近打火机的火苗，吸了一口。

En: She fishes a cigarette out of her pack and lights it -- then looks at you with her lungs full of smoke.
Zh: 她从盒子里抽出一支香烟，把它点燃——然后看着你，肺里满是烟气。

En: Again -- how do the Hardie boys know you?
Zh: “再说一遍——哈迪兄弟是怎么认识你的？”

En: She isth a laedy most fair and juste!
Zh: 她是最为美丽和公正的女人！

En: I've been talking to myself long enough. Let's get back to it.
Zh: 我自己的事已经说的够久了。还是回到原来的话题吧。

En: Don't worry, it's only been four or five seconds. You've got this.
Zh: 别担心，只过了大概4、5秒钟。你可以的。

En: Let's return to Sunday night -- the sounds from my room
Zh: “我们说回周日晚上吧——就是我房间里的声音。”

En: I noticed your room is close to mine. I have a personal question.
Zh: “我发现你的房间跟我的很近。我有个私人问题。”

En: Good. Yes. This means she could have heard something. Like what you were doing before you blacked out.
Zh: 很好。没错。这就代表她可能听到了什么。比如说在晕过去之前，你在做什么。

En: You do *not* need to know that. The lieutenant taps on his notebook. "What you need is to ask normal police questions -- like..." He waits for you to finish the sentence.
Zh: “你*不需要*知道这个的。”警督轻轻拍了拍笔记本。“你要做的是问一些普通的警察工作有关的问题——比如……”他在等你接话。

En: Let's talk about Sunday night again -- the sounds from my room.
Zh: “我们再说说周日晚上吧——就是我房间里的声音。”

En: Now that Kim isn't here -- let's talk about Sunday night.
Zh: “现在金不在——我们说说周日晚上的事吧。”

En: Ah yes. She pours herself some more coffee. "The night before I saw you in the hallway -- and reminded you you're a police officer."
Zh: “啊，是啊。”她抿了一口咖啡。“就在我在走廊里看见你的前一晚——而且还提醒你，你是个警察。”

En: No problem, sir. She feeds herself another cigarette.
Zh: “没关系，先生。”她又抽了一口烟。

En: She feeds herself another cigarette.
Zh: 她又抽了一口烟。

En: Yes? She looks at the note in your hand.
Zh: “什么事？”她看着你手中的笔记。

En: Thank you. A few more questions...
Zh: “谢谢你。还有些问题……”

En: I ran the serial number on his boots. I know his name.
Zh: “我查过他靴子的序列号。我知道他的名字。”

En: She blows a long column of smoke and watches it disperse overhead. After a moment, she says: "Good for you."
Zh: 她吐出一道长长的烟柱，看着它在头顶消散。片刻之后，她说到：“对你有好处。”

En: His name was Ellis. Ellis Kortenaer.
Zh: “他的名字叫埃利斯。埃利斯·科顿艾尔。”

En: I bet you're playing aloof because you wanna know really, really bad.
Zh: “我敢打赌，你一定是装出一副冷漠的样子，因为你肯定非常非常想知道吧。”

En: Hmh... She doesn't smile. She taps the ash from her cigarette and shrugs. "Sounds oddly feminine..."
Zh: “嗯……”她没有笑。弹了弹烟灰，耸了耸肩。“听起来莫名有点女性化……”

En: You don't want to know?
Zh: “你不想知道？”

En: Not really, she says with complete uninterest. "I just think Lely's good enough. But go ahead. You look like you *want* to tell me."
Zh: “不太想，”她毫无兴趣地说到。“我觉得莱利已经很好了。不过你继续吧。看起来你*挺想*告诉我的。”

En: Not really. Curls of smoke leave her mouth with each word. "Lely's good enough for me."
Zh: “不太想。”她每说一个字，嘴里就冒出几缕轻烟。“我觉得莱利已经很好了。”

En: I'll respect that.
Zh: “我尊重这一点。”

En: Thank you. She smiles, takes a puff on her cigarette.
Zh: 她笑了，吸了一口烟。“谢谢。”

En: Ellis. Ellis Kortenaer was his name.
Zh: “埃利斯。他的名字叫埃利斯·科顿艾尔。”

En: You mentioned he liked showing off his tattoos -- how?
Zh: “你提到他喜欢炫耀自己的纹身——怎么讲？”

En: How? She leans back. "Imagine him lying in bed, *freakish* musculature laid out on the sheets. Scarred, of course. Tattooed. The sheets are dirty for some reason."
Zh: “怎么讲？”她向后靠了靠。“想象他躺在床上，*奇特*的肌肉组织陈列在床单上。当然了，有点吓人。还有纹身。床单那么脏是有原因的。”

En: Is this Oranjese lit?
Zh: 这是奥兰治文学吗？

En: Don't interrupt.
Zh: 别打岔。

En: You were the woman in this?
Zh: “你就是那个女人？”

En: He's smoking and drinking, of course. And his chest and shoulders and arms are studded with stars. Tens, hundreds of them. Maybe even thousands.
Zh: “当然，他会抽烟，会喝酒。而且他的胸膛，肩膀和胳膊上都布满了星星。十几个，上百个，甚至可能是上千个。”

En: And he says... She lowers her voice, comically. "'That was *too* hard core. Don't ask me about that.' So she goes: 'Okay. But what's this, baby?' And he's like: 'Saw some bad shit there, killed some loincloths.'"
Zh: “然后他说……”她滑稽地压低了声音。“‘那个*太过*硬核了。别问我那个。’接着她就会说：‘好吧。不过这是什么呢，宝贝？’然后他就会说：‘在那里看见一些破事，杀了几个野人。’”

En: Oh yeah. She nods.
Zh: “是啊。”她点点头。

En: And the woman goes like... She points at the air with her sharp-nailed finger, picking out an imaginary tattoo-star. "'What was this, baby?'"
Zh: “然后女人说……”她把长着尖利指甲的手指伸向空中，挑出想象中的星星纹身。“‘这是*什么*，宝贝？’”

En: And so it goes -- star after star, port after port, third world country after third world country. And he's done horrible things in every single one of them.
Zh: “就这样——星星，港口，第三世界国家，一个接着一个。他在每一个地方都做过可怕的事情。”

En: Is this Oranjese lit?
Zh: “这是奥兰治文学吗？”

En: Yes. This is the very *essence* of Oranjese lit -- a moment's respite. Dark and hopeless as the struggle itself. She leans even further back to demonstrate.
Zh: “是的。这就是奥兰治文学的*精华*——片刻的喘息。和挣扎本身一样的黑暗和绝望。”为了演示，她的身体更加靠后了。

En: She wouldn't, she doesn't have the Full Hoi in her.
Zh: 她不会的，她骨子里没有完全科霍依化。

En: This does not surprise her.
Zh: 她并没有为此感到惊讶。

En: Everything left to be clarified is in the column on the left.
Zh: 现在唯一有待澄清的就剩下左边那一栏了。

En: The last missing pieces of a puzzle of flesh.
Zh: 最后一块缺失的肉体拼图。

En: No love for Möther Oranje? Wasn't he a *söldier*?
Zh: 没有对祖国母亲奥兰治的热爱吗？他不是一个*士兵*吗？

En: Revachol chooses carefully.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖的选择很小心。”

En: True -- it did *not* love him.
Zh: “事实是——它*不爱*他。”

En: She understands what autopsies entail. It doesn't get her off-balance.
Zh: 她明白尸检需要什么。这不会让她失去平衡。

En: The lieutenant blinks -- his expression does not change.
Zh: 警督眨了眨眼——他的表情没有变化。

En: All right. Let's see where this is going.
Zh: 好吧。我们看看会发生些什么。

En: This is one of her manoeuvres. The others are fast asleep, as always... it's hard to say what the truth is.
Zh: 这是她得策略之一。其他的都在熟睡，像往常一样……很难说真相到底是什么。

En: What? Huh? The truth? All the world's a stage! Aren't we *all* just actors?
Zh: 什么？哈？真相？整个世界就是一个大舞台！我们不都*只是*演员吗？

En: This means his race was Occidental, which fits the statements of another witness, the lieutenant says, pleased.
Zh: “这就意味着他是奥西登人种，跟另一个目击者的陈述是相符的，”警督满意地说到。

En: Did he? A smile flits across her face. "I never said he was a good man. Or that he had good intentions -- only that he was never bad to me."
Zh: “他有吗？”她的脸上掠过一丝微笑。“我从没说过他是个好人。或者他的本意是好的——只是他对我一直都不坏。”

En: With a soft ring, as the porcelain meets the metal table.
Zh: 一阵轻柔的声音，就像瓷器碰到金属桌子一样。

En: Yeah... That was practically his pick-up line. She picks the cup back up.
Zh: “是啊……基本上可以算是他的搭讪台词。”她又把杯子端了起来。

En: Thank you for clearing that up, miss. He turns to you. "Whenever you're ready -- I'm interested to hear what Titus Hardie has to say now."
Zh: “感谢你的澄清，小姐。”他转向你。“等你准备好之后——现在我很想听听提图斯·哈迪会怎么说。”

En: She takes a very small sip of her coffee and smiles.
Zh: 她小小地抿了一口咖啡，笑了。

En: She takes a very small sip of her coffee and smiles.
Zh: 她小小地抿了一口咖啡，笑了。

En: When he said he was *done* and *done mentally* it didn't sound like a joke. Sounded like a deeply troubled man.
Zh: 当他说他*受够了*还有*精神失控*的时候，听起来不像是开玩笑。听起来像是一个很不安分的人。

En: Hold on, he said he was *mentally done*. That's sounds like a broken man to me.
Zh: “等等，他说他*精神失控*。在我听起来，很像一个绝望破碎的人。”

En: Well... She smiles. "Maybe I pieced him back together -- with my magical personality?"
Zh: “这个嘛……”她笑了。“也许我又把他拼回去了——用我神奇的个性？”

En: Yeah. She lights another cigarette. "Extremely dead."
Zh: “是啊。”她又点燃一支香烟。“死透了。”

En: A moment ago it was love... And, no, I don't think the Union is *communist*. They're a couple of shades pinker than that.
Zh: “刚才还是爱情……不，我不觉得工会是*康米派*。他们是更加左派的人。”

En: Is the Union *communist*? They're a couple of shades pinker than that, no? Social democrats.
Zh: “工会是不是*康米派*？他们是更加左派的人，不是吗？工人主义者。”

En: I think she's just tired. She doesn't know what you meant by that -- and can you blame her?
Zh: 我觉得她只是累了。她不知道你的话是什么意思——你能因此责怪她吗？

En: You could blame her *a little*. It was a pretty straight forward question that she refused to answer.
Zh: 你可以*稍微*责备她一下。她拒绝回答的是一个非常直截了当的问题。

En: From way out in the northwest. He told me.
Zh: “从遥远的西北方来。他告诉我的。”

En: Maybe he was wrong. Forget about it. Nothing miraculous is coming.
Zh: “也许他错了。算了吧。奇迹不会出现。”

En: Woob-woob-wob-wob. She performs the music of a descending tuba.
Zh: “嘟叭叭嘟嘟。”她模仿着降调大号的音乐声。

En: Another *fantastic* moment, the lieutenant grudgingly admits. "What next?"
Zh: “又是一个*异想天开*的时刻，”警督不情愿地承认到。“接下来是什么？”

En: Technically not? C'mon man. Listen. You can't trust them. It's just you and me.
Zh: 严格的意义上讲没有？拜托，老兄。听着。你不能相信他们。只有你和我了。

En: The Hardie boys gave me a recording. They say it proves the deceased's intention to commit rape.
Zh: “哈里兄弟给了我一份录音。他们说可以证明死者意图强暴你。”

En: It sounds like the boys would have preferred my saying it *did* happen. A sigh. "I didn't want to get caught up in this..."
Zh: “听起来哈迪兄弟更希望我说*确实*发生过强暴。”一声叹息。“我不想卷入这件事……”

En: We have listened to the recording. The deceased states he intends to commit rape.
Zh: “我们已经听过录音了。死者声称他打算实施强暴。”

En: Titus Hardie gave us a recording where the deceased states his intention to commit rape.
Zh: “提图斯·哈迪交给我们一份录音，死者声称说他打算实施强暴。”

En: I have listened to the recording. The deceased states his intention to commit rape.
Zh: “我听过录音了。死者声称他打算实施强暴。”

En: Titus Hardie gave me a recording. On it, the deceased says he intends to rape someone.
Zh: “提图斯·哈迪给了我一份录音。死者在里面说他打算强暴某个人。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? If it can handle the songs of the coming Apocalypse, I'm sure it can handle this too.
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？如果它能搞的定即将降临的末日之歌，那肯定也能搞定这个。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? It produces a raw, wall-piercing sound.
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？它能发出一种原始的、能穿透墙壁的声音。”

En: We are in the process of listening to it. But haven't -- yet.
Zh: “我们正在听。不过暂时还没有听完。”

En: I'm... in the process of listening to it, miss.
Zh: “我……我正在听呢，小姐。”

En: Just looking for a player.
Zh: “只是在寻找播放器。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? It produces stratospheric, bedazzling sound. All night long...
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？它能发出平流层那种令人眩晕的声音。整夜不停……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: Mine unfortunately only comes with a mimsy little radio.
Zh: “很不幸，我只有一台小破收音机。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your partner's room? It's... unapologetic when it comes to volume.
Zh: “我可以推荐你搭档房间里的音响系统吗？说到音量的话，那可不是……道歉就能解决的问题。”

En: She nods, exhaling a dense cloud of cigarette smoke.
Zh: 她点点头，吐出一团浓厚的烟雾。

En: She is not at all worried the tape will contradict her statement.
Zh: 她完全不担心那盘磁带会跟她的陈述互相矛盾。

En: Mine unfortunately only comes with a mimsy little radio.
Zh: “很不幸，我只有一台小破收音机。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? It goes up to Apocalypse.
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？它能直接带人走向世界末日。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? It produces a raw, wall-piercing sound.
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？它能发出一种原始的、能穿透墙壁的声音。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? It produces a stratospheric, bedazzling sound.
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？它能发出平流层那种令人眩晕的声音。”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? I'm sure it plays more than OO and the Sad Small Church Band...
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？我肯定它绝对不只能播招摇乐团和悲伤小教堂乐队的歌……”

En: May I suggest the stereo system in your room? It's... uncharacteristically unapologetic when it comes to volume.
Zh: “我可以推荐你房间里的音响系统吗？说到音量的话，那可不是……寻常的道歉就能解决的问题。”

En: What does that recording say?
Zh: “录音里说了什么？”

En: She puts her coffee cup down.
Zh: 她放下了咖啡杯。

En: On this tape he specifically identifies *you* as the target.
Zh: “这盘磁带里面，他明确指出*你*就是他的目标。”

En: Mmm. Where did they get this recording, exactly?
Zh: “嗯。他们到底是从哪里弄到这盘录音的？”

En: It's intercepted radio chatter of the deceased -- recorded via a de-encryption station. It's authentic enough.
Zh: “这是从死者那里截获到的无线电通信——通过一个反加密站录制的。真实性很可靠了。”

En: Does he says he's gonna do it *Soldier of the Apocalypse*-style? She arches her brow.
Zh: 她拱起眉头。“他是不是说要像*末日战士*那样做？”

En: Those are the exact words he used.
Zh: “他就是这么说的。”

En: Co Hoi *was* mentioned.
Zh: “他还*提到*了科霍依。”

En: He wasn't *actually* there -- he didn't do a tour, or at least didn't tell me he did. Would've been overkill anyway. He lived his own little Co Hoi. It was in his... *everything*.
Zh: “*事实上*他并不在那里——他没有旅行过，或者没告诉我他做过。不然会是一趟杀戮之旅吧。他生活在自己的小科霍依的世界里。影响着他的……*一切*。”

En: Oh no, I'm pretty sure he *did* all those things -- then integrated them into his idea of normalcy. To keep on living. Until they just... sort of turned into his... She thinks. "What's the word I'm looking for?"
Zh: “不，我很肯定他*做过*那些事——然后把那些都融入了他对常态的看法里面。为了能继续生活下去。直到它们……有点像是变成了他的……”她想了想。“我该用什么词好呢？”

En: Coping mechanism?
Zh: “应对机制？”

En: Running joke. I was gonna say running joke -- and it sounds like you didn't even get the good bits. Lely's punchlines got way, way funkier than that.
Zh: “笑柄。我打算说是笑柄——听起来你根本没明白精彩的部分。莱利的俏皮话远比那个要有意思多了。”

En: Catch phrase?
Zh: “口头禅？”

En: Weren't you afraid?
Zh: “你不害怕吗？”

En: And you spent time with this person? Romantically?
Zh: “你跟这个人在一起过吗？很浪漫的那种？”

En: Afraid of what? That tape the Hardie boys recorded? Your mother probably never told you this, but girls are *evil*.
Zh: “害怕什么？哈迪兄弟录的磁带吗？你的母亲可能永远都不会告诉你这个，但是女孩都是很*邪恶*的。”

En: He did refer to the atrocities in South Safre, yes.
Zh: “他的确提到了南萨弗里的暴行，没错。”

En: Why say things like that? Machismo?
Zh: “为什么要这么说？大男子主义吗？”

En: Why say things like that? Was he bragging?
Zh: “为什么要这么说？他是在吹牛吗？”

En: We're all scraping up any happiness we can find, officer. Going around with our little scouring sticks -- you, your first love, Mr. Co Hoi here...
Zh: “我们都在到处积攒所有能找到的幸福和快乐，警官。带着我们的小研磨棒到处游走，这里刮刮那里蹭蹭——你，你的初恋，还有这位科霍依先生……”

En: Did he tell you he had actually *done* any of those things -- here in Martinaise, I mean?
Zh: “他有没有告诉过你，自己实际上有没有*做过*那些事情——我的意思是说，在马丁内斯？”

En: No. We were too busy laying waste our own nervous systems to direct any of the *fury* outward. He seemed... She thinks. "He seemed happy, I guess. At ease. As much as a man like him could be."
Zh: “没有。我们忙着破坏自己的神经系统，把所有的*愤怒*向外发泄。他似乎……”她想了一会儿。“我觉得他似乎很高兴。自由自在。像他这样的男人能做多少就是多少。”

En: Yes. The word *whore* was used.
Zh: “是的。他用过*婊子*这个词。”

En: Sure, I can try. What do you need?
Zh: “当然，我可以试试。你需要什么？”

En: She doesn't care. If anything, she sounds amused.
Zh: 她不在乎。如果有什么的话，她好像觉得很好笑。

En: Intercepted radio chatter. It sounded authentic enough.
Zh: “拦截无线电通信。听起来已经足够真实了。”

En: Something to that effect, yes.
Zh: “反正差不多吧，没错。”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Then you didn't get the Greatest Hits, officers. She shakes her head slowly. "It's probably for the best."
Zh: “那你完全没明白这盘精选集，警官。”她慢慢摇了摇头。“这样也许最好吧。”

En: Then you didn't get the Greatest Hits, officer. She shakes her head slowly. "It's probably for the best... Hm. Hang on..."
Zh: “那你完全没明白这盘精选集，警官。”她慢慢摇了摇头。“这样也许最好吧……嗯。坚持下去……”

En: The phrase was used. The lieutenant checks his notes and nods.
Zh: 警督看了一眼笔记，点点头。“确实用过这个词。”

En: Did he say *whores* a lot?... Was he pretty much on the verge of *doing it Co Hoi-style*?
Zh: “他是不是说了很多次*婊子*？……说自己接近于要按*按科霍依的风格办事*了？”

En: A memory surfaces in her tired neocortex -- it's not entirely unpleasant.
Zh: 她疲惫的大脑皮层中浮现出一段记忆——也不能说完全不愉快。

En: Yeah, he said *whore*. So do I. Does it matter?
Zh: “是啊，他说过*婊子*。我也是。有什么关系吗？”

En: I don't see how your problems are relevant right now, officer, the lieutenant says in a quick, clipped tone.
Zh: “我现在看不出你的问题跟这个有什么关系，警官，”警督用一种快速又简短的语气说到。

En: It's not a problem. She touches his shoulder. "As for Co Hoi, officers..."
Zh: “这不是问题。”她碰了下他的肩膀。“至于科霍依，警官……”

En: She clearly enjoys siding with you.
Zh: 她显然很喜欢站在你这边。

En: He liked the way it *sounded* when he said it. As to Co Hoi... The young woman lights a new cigarette with the butt of her old one.
Zh: “他喜欢它*听起来*的那种感觉。至于科霍依……”年轻女子用旧烟蒂点燃一支新的烟。

En: It suits you, officer. You should say it more. As to Co Hoi...
Zh: “很适合你，警官。你应该多说一些。至于科霍依……”

En: She clearly enjoys telling you this.
Zh: 她显然很乐意告诉你这些。

En: Yes -- was he bragging?
Zh: “是的——他是在吹牛吗？”

En: She's quite observant about the human character -- trained to observe, even...
Zh: 她非常善于观察人性——专门训练过，甚至……

En: Did you learn this from studying Oranjese lit? Sounds psychological.
Zh: “你是研究奥兰治文学的时候学会的吗？听起来跟心理学有关。”

En: People who go to university know how to use words like *internalize* and *integrate*, officer... Now, what was that expression? She's still looking for it.
Zh: “上大学的人知道怎么使用*内在化*和*一体化*的词汇，警官……现在，那是什么表情？”她还在寻找。

En: Persona?
Zh: “伪装？”

En: There's more to this. She has an index in her head of pathologies and how to exploit them. I can almost see her turn the pages.
Zh: 远远不止这样。她的脑子里有一个精神异常索引，而且也知道如何利用。我几乎能看见她在脑子里翻动书页的样子。

En: You can trust Pillar-Bookhead, by the way. As far as I can tell, he's not singing to her tune... the *only* one besides me.
Zh: 顺便说一句，你可以相信榆木脑袋。据我所知，他没有附和她的步调……除了我之外，*只有*他。

En: He was like the Semenese conflict, the Co Hoi massacre, and the '36 famine in Yeesut all rolled into one person, then cast in Oranjese ceramic armour. Which he wore in bed *and* in the shower...
Zh: “他就像塞美尼冲突，科霍依大屠杀，还有伊苏特36年的大饥荒，全部都集中在了一个人身上，然后镶铸了奥兰治陶瓷盔甲。他在床上*还有*洗澡的时候都会穿在身上……”

En: You like this kind of stuff?
Zh: “你喜欢这种东西？”

En: Had I the physical robustness and social support I'd be *in* Co Hoi, *I* would be tearing it up *Soldier of the Apocalypse*-style...
Zh: “如果我拥有强壮的身体和社会支持，我也会*成为*科霍依，*我*也会像*末日战士*一样把它撕碎……”

En: There is a small measure of pride in her. That she could quell the rage in such a being.
Zh: 她有一点小小的骄傲。因为她能平息这样一个人的怒火。

En: Do you think he was trying to scare people?
Zh: “你觉得他是想吓唬别人吗？”

En: No, I'm pretty sure he *did* all those things -- and then had to internalize them to keep on living. Until they just... sort of turned into his... She thinks. "What's the word I'm looking for?"
Zh: “不，我很肯定他*做过*那些事——然后把它们全都内在化，这样才能继续生活下去。直到它们……有点像是变成了他的……”她想了想。“我该用什么词好呢？”

En: Did he tell you he had actually *done* any of those things? Here in Martinaise, I mean.
Zh: “他有没有告诉过你，自己实际上有没有*做过*那些事情——我的意思是说，在马丁内斯？”

En: Like -- for example -- his name?
Zh: “比如说——举个例子——他的名字？”

En: Actually, officer, I didn't know his name. I just called him Lely.
Zh: “警官，其实我不知道他的名字。我就叫他莱利。”

En: Actually, officer, I didn't know his name. I just called him Lely.
Zh: “警官，其实我不知道他的名字。我就叫他莱利。”

En: His name?
Zh: “他的名字？”

En: Lelystad. That's a good start... The lieutenant writes it down in his notebook...
Zh: “莱利斯塔德。这是个很好的开始……”警督在笔记本里记了下来。

En: ...then tears out a page and hands it to you. "We have a few questions you can help us with. A few things a field autopsy alone can't answer."
Zh: ……然后撕下一张，递给了你。“我们有些问题需要你的帮助。有些事情光靠现场尸检是无法回答的。”

En: I guess. He came from Lelystad -- it's short for that. And it was his *army name*, apparently. He said his real name wasn't *his*. I tried to pry it out of him, but it was no use.
Zh: “我猜是吧。他是从莱利斯塔德来的——这是缩写版。而且显然这也是他在*军队的名字*。他说自己的真名不是*他的*。我试过想撬开他的嘴，但是没有用。”

En: A nickname?
Zh: “昵称吗？”

En: A nickname?
Zh: “昵称吗？”

En: ...then starts tearing out a page, but stops mid-motion. "It just occurred to me, colleague," he says, turning to you. "We *still* haven't performed a field autopsy on the deceased."
Zh: ……然后开始撕扯下一页纸，不过中途停了下来。“我刚刚想起来，同事，”他转向你，说到。“我们*还没有*对死者进行现场尸检呢。”

En: The questions I have assume *some* kind of foreknowledge on our part. I suggest we go and perform it now and return once we've brought ourselves up to date.
Zh: “我的问题都是基于我们的*某种*预见假设出来的。我建议我们现在就去尸检，一旦掌握最新信息之后马上回来。”
