En: The young woman cranes her neck, trying to catch a glimpse of the page the lieutenant passed to you. On it is a list of autopsy observations, recorded neatly in blue ink.
Zh: 年轻女子伸长脖子，想看一眼警督递给你的那页纸。上面有一份尸检观察结果清单，用蓝色墨水整洁地记录着。

En: How old was he, miss?
Zh: “他多大，小姐？”

En: His eyes were blue?
Zh: “他的眼睛是蓝色的吗？”

En: His eye colour?
Zh: “他的眼睛颜色是？”

En: Tell me something odd -- did he enjoy his death?
Zh: “有件怪事需要你确认——他很享受死亡吗？”

En: I think we're finished with this line of questioning. (Hand the lieutenant back his notes.)
Zh: “我觉得这方面的问题已经差不多了。”（把笔记还给警督。）

En: Where is Lelystad? The place, I mean.
Zh: “莱利斯塔德在哪？我是说那个地方。”

En: Could it be love that *did him in*?
Zh: “难道是爱情让他*丢了性命*？”

En: Perhaps, miss, it was *communism* that killed him?
Zh: “小姐，也许是*康米主义*杀了他？”

En: Do you feel guilty?
Zh: “你感到内疚吗？”

En: Something miraculous is coming. He told me.
Zh: “某种奇迹就要发生了。他告诉我的。”

En: He had a tattoo -- what did it mean?
Zh: “他有个纹身——那是什么意思？”

En: We ordered a toxicology report. Any idea what that will show us?
Zh: “我们要求做一份毒理报告。你知不知道会出现什么样的结果？”

En: We requested a semen screening from Processing.
Zh: “我们要求分析部门做一次精液检查。”

En: Oh yes, the lieutenant suddenly remembers. "His hair -- if you can remember?"
Zh: “是的，”警督突然记起来了。“他的头发——你还记得吗？”

En: In Oranje, officer. It's a... I think *municipality* is the term? A nowhere-town there.
Zh: “在奥兰治，警官。那是一个……是不是有个术语叫*自治区*？那是一个凄凉的小城镇。”

En: The Lelystad municipality has few boroughs and even fewer cities -- it's made of agricultural plots near the border of Gottwald. Executive summary: cows, silos, and wheat.
Zh: 莱利斯塔德自治区下属的市镇很少，城市就更少了——它是由戈特伍德边界附近的农田组成的。概要：奶牛，粮仓和小麦。

En: You were right, officer. The lieutenant nods, sportsmanlike. "He *was* Occidental."
Zh: “你是对的，警官。”警督点点头，很有运动员精神。“他*是*奥西登人。”

En: You were *almost* right, officer. The lieutenant shakes his head, like you just missed a shot in darts. "That means his race was Occidental, not Mondial. I'll update the form."
Zh: “你*差不多*说对了，警官。”警督摇摇头，好像你刚刚扔飞了一支飞镖。“那就意味着他是奥西登人种，不是蒙迪人。我会更新一下表格。”

En: Okay, let's go.
Zh: “好吧，我们走。”

En: I have other questions first.
Zh: “我还有其他问题。”

En: No, I want to ask the questions *now*.
Zh: “不，我*现在*就要问。”

En: Miss, he says, turning back to the young woman. "Excuse my colleague -- he has recently suffered brain damage. We will now go and do our duty cleaning up the deceased."
Zh: “小姐，”他转回到那个年轻女子，说到。“请原谅我的同事——他最近遭受了脑损伤。我们现在就会离开，去履行清理死者的职责。”

En: Where in the GRIH is the lieutenant? Possibly speeding from one terminal to another in glorious solitude? Wherever he is, you need him -- your other questions will make little sense without him here.
Zh: 警督跑哪里去了？可能正在光辉的孤独中飞速从一个终点驶向另一个尽头？不管他在哪，你需要他——如果他不在这里，你的其他问题几乎毫无意义。

En: You won't have to look at that sight much longer, I promise. We will return soon. He turns to leave.
Zh: “我保证，你不用再看着那副景象了。我们很快就回来。”他转身准备离开。

En: There is no talking him out of this -- and no, the incident has not had a good effect on his opinion of you either.
Zh: 劝他别这么做是不可能的——而且不行，这件事不会让他对你的看法变好。

En: All right, but quickly. She has endured that sight long enough. He nods toward the yard. "It's time for us to do our duty."
Zh: “好吧，不过快一点。她已经忍受这幅景象很久了。”他朝着院子点点头。“是时候轮到我们履行自己的职责了。”

En: The young woman nods in silent approval. Then feeds herself another cigarette.
Zh: 年轻女子默默点头表示赞同。然后又抽了一口烟。

En: Excuse the delay, miss, he says with a nod toward the yard. "This situation will be addressed now. We'll be back soon."
Zh: “抱歉拖了这么久，小姐，”他一边朝院子里点点头，一边说到。“这种情况现在就会得到解决。我们马上回来。”

En: The young woman nods, then proceeds to feed herself another cigarette as you leave.
Zh: 年轻女子点点头，你离开的时候，她又抽了一口烟。

En: Hold on, Kim. I did not know this was a competition.
Zh: “等等，金。我都不知道竞争的事。”

En: (Look at Klaasje.) "You were both from Oranje?"
Zh: （看着卡拉洁。）“你们俩都是奥兰治来的？”

En: It isn't. Police work is a *cooperative* sport.
Zh: “没有。警察工作是一种*合作*运动。”

En: There is no challenge without competition. There must be an opponent.
Zh: 没有竞争就没有挑战。肯定是有对手的。

En: This is clearly a sport for him. Something like archery -- or darts?
Zh: 对他来说这显然就是一项运动。像是射箭——或者飞镖？

En: It's still about getting hits, right? And not missing?
Zh: “其实还是为了获得命中率的，对吗？还有不要失手？”

En: Then the competition is against *the world*?
Zh: “那这是在和*世界*竞争吗？”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: That's too *conceptual* for me, I just like to get autopsies right -- on the first try, if possible. He makes another note. "Where were we?"
Zh: “对我来说太*概念化*了，我只想做好验尸——如果可能的话，最好一次成功。”他又做了点笔记。“我们说到哪里了？”

En: He's lying.
Zh: 他在撒谎。

En: That's too *pinball* for me. I just like to get autopsies right on the first try. He makes another note. "Where were we?"
Zh: “对我来说有点太*弹球*了。我只想一次性把验尸做好。”他又做了点笔记。“我们说到哪里了？”

En: The young woman looks at you, then the lieutenant, then you. She enjoys the silence.
Zh: 年轻女子看了看你，转眼望向警督，然后又回头看你。她很享受这种沉默。

En: (Look at the autopsy paper.) "Moving on..."
Zh: （看着尸检报告。）“继续……”

En: Yes. We were compatriots.
Zh: “没错。我们是同胞。”

En: Her eyes follow yours on the piece of paper.
Zh: 她的目光追随着看着那张纸的你。

En: Did that bring you together?
Zh: “所以你们走到了一起？”

En: Did that bring you together?
Zh: “所以你们走到了一起？”

En: No. He was too old for that -- and from another part of Oranjenrijk. I didn't even understand his accent. What brought us together wasn't Oranje -- it was bad habits.
Zh: “不。他有点太老了——而且他来自奥兰治的另一边。我甚至都搞不懂他的口音。我们走到一起不是因为奥兰治——而是坏习惯。”

En: Sex, alcohol...
Zh: 性，还有酒精。

En: Speed -- probably also sildenafil.
Zh: 思必得——可能还有万艾可。

En: And violence.
Zh: 还有暴力。

En: No love for Mother Oranje? But wasn't he a *soldier*? This could be worth pursuing.
Zh: 没有对祖国母亲奥兰治的热爱吗？他不是一个*士兵*吗？这是个值得追究的点。

En: A military man -- but not a patriot?
Zh: “一个军人——但不是一个爱国者？”

En: No need to get political.
Zh: 没必要搞得这么政治。

En: No, he left the National Service after they taught him to do what he did on Semenine. He wasn't the flag-waving kind. He was the *making money killing people* kind.
Zh: “不，在他们教会他在塞美尼做的那些事之后，他就退役了。他不是那种沙文主义者。他是那种靠*杀人赚钱*的人。”

En: He was by no means a stupid man... She takes a long drag of her cigarette, then washes it down with coffee. "A people person, a small platoon leader. Certainly not a patriot."
Zh: “他绝对不是一个愚蠢的男人……”她抽了一大口烟，然后就着咖啡吞了下去。“他是个善于交际的人，一个小排长。肯定不是一个爱国者。”

En: You don't seem like much of a patriot yourself.
Zh: “你也不太像是一个爱国者。”

En: Mhm, she hums. "There is nothing on Mundi. The Old Old World is dead and we both knew it. Maybe Oranje *did* bring us together -- in loathing."
Zh: “嗯，”她嘟囔着。“蒙迪什么都没有。旧旧世界已经死了，我们所有人都知道这一点。也许*确实*是奥兰治让我们走到了一起——因为憎恶。”

En: I love Revachol, though. She looks around, the wind in her hair. "I hope she loves me too."
Zh: “不过我挺喜欢瑞瓦肖的。”她看了看周围，风在她的发丝之间穿梭。“我希望她也喜欢我。”

En: As an Oranjese yourself, didn't his lack of patriotism annoy you?
Zh: “作为一个奥兰治人，缺乏爱国热情的他不会让你感到生气吗？”

En: A disgraceful mercenary. The Oranjese state should have checked his breast for sublime patriotic rage before they trained him!
Zh: “一个可耻的雇佣兵。奥兰治国在训练他之前，应该先检查一下他是否具有崇高的爱国主义情怀！”

En: Had he passed that test, we wouldn't have gotten along. Come to think of it -- maybe it was our *hate* for Oranje that brought us together.
Zh: 她带着几分消遣的看着你拍打自己的胸脯。“如果他通过了那次测试，我们就没办法和睦相处了。现在想起来——也许是对奥兰治的*憎恨*让我们走到了一起。”

En: No, there is nothing on Mundi. The Old Old World is dead and we both knew it. Come to think of it, maybe Oranje did bring us together -- in loathing.
Zh: “不，蒙迪什么也没有。旧旧世界已经死了，也许确实是奥兰治让我们走到了一起——因为憎恶。”

En: A flash of rage comes over you, the words have already left your mouth:
Zh: 你勃然大怒，话语脱口而出：

En: The young woman is smiling -- still -- about their *bad habits*.
Zh: 年轻女子还在微笑——笑他们的*坏习惯*。

En: Oranje? Oranjese map of waterways? This fits with his tattoo.
Zh: 奥兰治？奥兰治水路地图？跟他的纹身是吻合的。

En: She doesn't mind this questioning. On the contrary, she enjoys thinking back to him now. This is a sort of wake for her.
Zh: 她不介意这次询问。相反，她很享受回想他的事情。对她来说，算是守灵吧。

En: He was forty-two.
Right again, officer. The lieutenant taps his blue pen at you, as though assigning another point.
Zh: “又说对了，警官。”警督用蓝色钢笔拍了拍你，好像指定了另一个目标点。

En: Looks like you were right, officer. The lieutenant taps on his notebook once. As though assigning some kind of *point*...
Zh: “看来你是对的，警官。”警督拍了一下自己的笔记本。好像在指定某种*目标点*……

En: We were slightly off then. Thank you for clearing it up. The lieutenant makes a correction in his notes.
Zh: “我们有些理解偏了。感谢你的澄清。”警督在笔记里做了订正。

En: Forty-two? Are you sure? I would have had him above fifty...
Zh: “42？你确定？我还以为他50多岁了……”

En: He had many scars that made him appear older. But no. The memory makes her smile.
Zh: “他有很多伤疤，让他看起来比较显老。不过不是的。”回忆让她笑了起来。

En: We even celebrated his birthday, like, some weeks ago... It was a funny two days. He had little reason to lie to me.
Zh: “我们甚至还给他庆祝了生日，大概，是几周之前的事……那两天挺有意思的。他没有跟我撒谎的理由。”

En: That means his birthday is around February 28th.
Zh: 这就意味着他的生日大约在2月28号。

En: A miss.
Zh: 一次失误。

En: The adversary won.
Zh: 对手赢了。

En: This feels so *just*. Like there is righteousness in the world.
Zh: 这感觉实在是太*公正*了。就好像这个世界存在着正义。

En: It's still about getting hits right -- and not missing?
Zh: “其实还是关于正中目标——不能打偏？”

En: Then the competition is against *the world*?
Zh: “那这是在和*世界*竞争吗？”

En: I did not know this was a competition, Kim.
Zh: “我都不知道竞争的事，金。”

En: That's too *conceptual* for me, I just like to get autopsies correct -- on the first try if possible. He makes another note. "Where were we?"
Zh: “对我来说太*概念化*了，我只想做好验尸——如果可能的话，最好一次成功。”他又做了点笔记。“我们说到哪里了？”

En: He's lying.
Zh: 他在撒谎。

En: Let it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: The young woman looks at you, then the lieutenant, then you. She's enjoying this.
Zh: 年轻女子看了看你，转眼望向警督，然后又回头看你。她在享受。

En: This is clearly sports for him. Something like archery -- or darts?
Zh: 对他来说这显然就是一项运动。像是射箭——或者飞镖？

En: That's too *pinball* for me, I just like to get autopsies right on the first try. He makes another note. "Where were we?"
Zh: “对我来说有点太*弹球*了。我只想一次性把验尸做好。”他又做了点笔记。“我们说到哪里了？”

En: There is no challenge without competition. There must be an opponent.
Zh: 没有竞争就没有挑战。肯定是有对手的。

En: It isn't. Police work is a *cooperative* sport.
Zh: “不是的。警察工作是一种*合作*运动。”

En: Points are good. Have one, you old dog! Before we all die...
Zh: 分数很好啊。来一点吧，你这个老家伙！在我们全死光之前……

En: Better not to mention it...
Zh: 最好还是别提……

En: The young woman looks at you, then the lieutenant, then you.
Zh: 年轻女子看了看你，转眼望向警督，然后又回头看你。

En: She's clearly sensing something. A spike in testosterone levels, perhaps.
Zh: 她明显感觉到了什么。也许，是睾丸素水平激增。

En: Yes, light blue. They were like... She stops, her eyes half closed, then continues: "Like little blue galaxies. It was strange, seeing those eyes in his fucked up face..."
Zh: “是的，浅蓝色。它们就像……”她停了下来，双眼半睁，然后继续说到：“就像蓝色的小星系。感觉很奇怪，他那张一塌糊涂的脸上居然长了一双那样的眼睛……”

En: Blue, light blue. They were like... She stops, her eyes half closed, then continues: "Like little blue galaxies, you know. It was strange, seeing those eyes in his fucked up face..."
Zh: “蓝色，浅蓝色。它们就像……”她停了下来，双眼半睁，然后继续说到：“就像蓝色的小星系。感觉很奇怪，他那张一塌糊涂的脸上居然长了一双那样的眼睛……”

En: Pardon the swearing. She takes a drag. "I do him an injustice -- he wasn't ugly. And he had a beautiful, soft voice. Very surprising, what with all the scarring. It was quite something, watching him speak."
Zh: “请原谅我的咒骂。”她抽了一口烟。“我对他不太公正——他不丑。而且他的嗓音很美，很温柔。挺让人惊讶的，特别是有那么多疤痕的情况下。看着他说话，其实挺不错的。”

En: He had a combat wound -- on his chin and mouth?
Zh: “他有一道搏斗留下的伤口——在下巴和嘴巴上？”

En: Yes. *Severe*. She seems to enjoy the word. "It made him look like half his face was cracking away in some strange smile. That and those eyes..."
Zh: “是的。很*严重*。”她好像很享受这个词。“让他看上去像是半张脸在奇怪的微笑中裂开了。还有那双眼睛……”

En: It's clear she was very much attracted to him -- and still is.
Zh: 她明显是被他深深吸引了——现在还是。

En: You found him attractive?
Zh: “你觉得他很有吸引力吗？”

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 自己保密吧。

En: Of course. He was the most strangely beautiful man I've ever been with. And I mean that... She shivers slightly.
Zh: “当然。他是我遇见的最奇怪的美男子。我是认真的……”她微微颤抖着。

En: She shivers slightly.
Zh: 她微微颤抖着。

En: And now he's... Her gaze drifts toward the yard, but she catches herself and her eyes fix on her reflection in the window instead. "Yeah."
Zh: “不过现在他……”她的目光飘向院子，但是很快控制住了自己，转而把目光固定在窗户上自己的倒影里。“是啊。”

En: Super dead.
Zh: “死透了。”

En: I'm sorry for your loss.
Zh: “请节哀顺变。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Dead.
Zh: “死了。”

En: Yeah. She lights another cigarette.
Zh: “是啊。”她又点燃一支香烟。

En: Yeah... She smiles bitterly. "I'll get over it. Once I don't have to look at him anymore."
Zh: “是啊……”她的笑容有些苦涩。“我会挺过去的。只要我不用再看见他就好。”

En: She tries to light another cigarette. It's windy, but she gets it done.
Zh: 她试着点燃另一支香烟。风有点大，不过她还是成功了。

En: Then adds: "And now he's dead. A pity."
Zh: 然后补充了一句：“现在他死了。很可惜。”

En: What kind of man *was* he? Before you go, ask for details. She seems okay to talk about it.
Zh: 他*曾经*是个怎样的男人？在你离开之前，再多问问细节。她好像挺愿意谈论这个的。

En: It was light brown, almost blond. He darkened it with brilliantine. Made it oily. Not nice to stroke. I couldn't convince him to leave it alone.
Zh: “浅棕色，几乎可以说是金黄色。他用发蜡把颜色弄深了。把它弄得油腻腻的。摸起来不太舒服。我没办法说服他别整自己的头发。”

En: Interesting -- I too have stroked his hair.
Zh: “有意思——我也摸过他的头发。”

En: Kim, I said to put the brilliantine on the form -- do I get a point?
Zh: “金，我说过要把发蜡记在表里——我能得一分吗？”

En: Let's get to the rest.
Zh: “我们说说剩下的部分吧。”

En: No, he answers dryly.
Zh: “不能，”他干巴巴的答道。

En: But I put it down, there... (Point to the red autopsy slip.)
Zh: “但是我把它记下来了，那里……”（指着红色的验尸报告。）

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I can see you've put *quite a few* things here -- they don't all give you a point.
Zh: “我看见你在这里记了*不少*东西——它们并不能为你加分的。”

En: There it is, in your rushed writing --right where the lieutenant is pointing: "FUCKY-FUCKY."\n\nYou should really get this questioning back on track now.
Zh: 有了，你潦草的字迹——就在警督指向的地方：“真他妈绝了。”\n\n你真的应该让询问回到正轨上去。

En: There it is, in your rushed writing --right where the lieutenant is pointing: "PIGS gonna have SEX."\n\nYou should really get this questioning back on track now.
Zh: 有了，你潦草的字迹——就在警督指向的地方：“猪也会有性生活。”\n\n你真的应该让询问回到正轨上去。

En: Okay. Sure. Here you go. What else are we missing, officer? he asks, trying to get the questioning back on track.
Zh: “好吧。当然。干得好。我们还遗漏了什么，警官？”他问道，试图让询问回归正轨。

En: Oily, isn't it... She says, unfazed. She draws on her cigarette and lets the smoke linger for a moment in her mouth. "We have that in common now."
Zh: “很油腻，不是吗……”她淡定地说到。她抽了一口烟，让烟雾在自己的嘴巴里逗留了一会儿。“现在我们有共同点了。”

En: The young woman nods.
Zh: 年轻女子点点头。

En: Oh. She smiles. "*That*."
Zh: “哦。”她笑了。“*那个*啊。”

En: It was an Oranjese Map of the Waterways?
Zh: “那是奥兰治的水路地图吗？”

En: It was a map of his service history?
Zh: “是他服役历史的地图吗？”

En: What did it represent --  do you know?
Zh: “它代表什么——你知道吗？”

En: It was a map. Of his life and the places he visited as a soldier. It was mostly for showing off to chicks though.
Zh: “那是一张地图。记录了他的生活，还有当兵时去过的地方。不过主要还是用来向女人炫耀的。”

En: For showing off to chicks? How so?
Zh: “向女人炫耀？怎么讲？”

En: Sure, service history... It was mostly for showing off to chicks though.
Zh: “当然，服役历史……不过主要还是用来向女人炫耀的。”

En: How? She leans back. "Imagine him lying in bed, *freakish* musculature laid out on the sheets. Scarred, of course. Tattooed. The sheets are dirty for some reason."
Zh: “怎么讲？”她向后靠了靠。“想象他躺在床上，*惊人*的肌肉在床单上展示着。当然，疤痕密布。还有纹身。床单会*弄脏*是有原因的。”

En: Is this Oranjese lit?
Zh: 这算奥兰治文学吗？

En: Is this Oranjese lit?
Zh: “这算奥兰治文学吗？”

En: Don't interrupt.
Zh: 别打岔。

En: Yes. This is the very *essence* of Oranjese lit -- a moment's respite. Dark and hopeless as the struggle itself. She leans even further back to demonstrate.
Zh: “是的。这就是奥兰治文学的*精华*——片刻的喘息。和挣扎本身一样的黑暗和绝望。”为了演示，她的身体越发靠后了。

En: You were the woman in this?
Zh: “你就是那个女人？”

En: Oh yeah. She nods.
Zh: “是啊。”她点点头。

En: And the woman goes like... She points at the air with her sharp-nailed finger, picking out an imaginary tattoo-star. "'What was *this*, baby?'"
Zh: “然后女人说……”她把长着尖利指甲的手指伸向空中，挑出想象中的星星纹身。“‘这是*什么*，宝贝？’”

En: Can you tell us precisely what these mean? (Hand her the photo.)
Zh: “能告诉我们这些到底是什么意思吗？”（把照片递给她。）

En: No thank you. She does not take it. "I've seen enough of him dead. I can tell you what they *meant* without looking at them." She pours herself some more coffee.
Zh: “不需要照片，谢谢。”她没有接过来。“他的死相我已经看够了。不用看我就能告诉你它们的*意思*。”她给自己又倒了些咖啡。

En: And he says... She lowers her voice, comically. "'That was *too* hard core. Don't ask me about that.' So she goes: 'Okay. But what's this, baby?' And he's like: 'Saw some bad shit there, killed some loincloths.'"
Zh: “然后他说……”她滑稽地压低了声音。“‘那个*太过*硬核了。别问我那个。接着她就会说：‘好吧。不过这是什么呢，宝贝？’然后他就会说：‘在那里看见一些破事，杀了几个野人。’”

En: And so it goes -- star after star, port after port, third world country after third world country. And he's done horrible things in every single one of them.
Zh: “就这样——星星，港口，第三世界国家，一个接着一个。他在每一个地方都做过可怕的事情。”

En: He's smoking and drinking, of course. And his chest and shoulders and arms are studded with stars. Tens, hundreds of them. Maybe even thousands.
Zh: “当然，他会抽烟，会喝酒。而且他的胸膛，肩膀和胳膊上都布满了星星。十几个，上百个，甚至可能是上千个。”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: He was a blue-eyed boy with thick arms. From a small town. He was also *poor*, and the government of Oranje needed some people killed, so they turned him into a grotesque killer -- for money.
Zh: “他是一个长着蓝眼睛的男人，手臂很粗壮。来自一个小城镇。他也很*贫穷*，奥兰冶政府需要一些人的命，所以他们把他变成了一个丑陋的杀手——为了赏金。”

En: He went to Killer Academy in Vredefort. Then he killed some people on the Semenine islands. And on other islands too -- all of the islands. After this he came to Revachol and got killed himself.
Zh: “他去了弗里德堡的杀手学校。然后在塞美尼群岛上杀了一些人。还有其他岛上——所有岛上都杀过人。在那之后，他就来到了瑞瓦肖，自己被杀了。”

En: Not a very fun story.
Zh: “这个故事并不好笑。”

En: Good story. Thanks.
Zh: “不错的故事。谢谢。”

En: It *is* when you're high. It can be very exciting then -- you have the tools to deal with it. It's not a very nice story to remember when you're sober. She smiles faintly. "A change of topic?"
Zh: “当你兴奋的时候，它*就是*的。可能会非常刺激——你也拥有能够应对的工具。当你清醒的时候，这可不是什么好故事。”她微微一笑。“换个话题？”

En: Change of topic, perhaps? She looks over her shoulder, to the sea. Then back at you, smiling faintly.
Zh: 她的目光越过你的肩膀，看向大海。然后她又看着你，微微一笑：“也许，我们可以换个话题？”

En: A column of air encircles her, brushing gently on the metallic silver fabric that covers her shoulders and her long, slender arms...
Zh: 一缕轻风将她环绕，轻柔地抚弄着覆盖在她肩膀和纤长胳膊上的金属银制布料……

En: Revachol loves everybody.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖爱所有人。”

En: Shake your head.
Zh: 摇摇头。

En: The feeling dissipates, the cold passes. The woman's eyes follow yours -- to the piece of notebook paper.
Zh: 感觉消散，寒冷过去。女人的眼睛跟随着你——落到那长笔记本的纸页上。

En: It didn't love him.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖不爱他。”

En: It's clear she *liked* it.
Zh: 很明显，她*喜欢*它。

En: You liked it?
Zh: “你喜欢吗？”

En: Quite a lot, yes.
Zh: “很喜欢，没错。”

En: Sure, waterways... It was mostly for showing off to chicks though.
Zh: “当然了，水路……不过主要还是用来向女人炫耀的。”

En: Thank you for clearing that up.
Zh: “感谢你的澄清。”

En: Why do you ask? She seems unsurprised.
Zh: “为什么这么问？”她好像并不吃惊。

En: A real rainbow splattering of pharmaceuticals, I bet. She grins. "Barbiturates, amphetamine, sildenaphil..."
Zh: “我敢打赌，药物会飞溅出来，就像一道真正的彩虹。”她咧嘴一笑。“巴比妥，安非他命，万艾可……”

En: Hear that? Sildenaphil! Just like you suspected. You have a *great* nose for this stuff!
Zh: 听见了吗？万艾可？跟你怀疑的一样。你对这类东西的嗅觉非常*灵敏*！

En: What is Sildenaphil?
Zh: 万艾可是什么？

En: Thanks.
Zh: 谢谢。

En: How much does that toxicology report cost the Police of Revachol? I can do it for *half* of that. Save you some money, make some myself.
Zh: “这份毒理报告花了瑞瓦肖警察多少钱？我可以负担*一半*的费用。给你省点钱，我来承担一些。”

En: It's for maintaining an erection. Uppers are vasoconstrictors, so that feat becomes problematic.
Zh: 是用来维持男性兴奋状态的。安非他命是血管收缩剂，所以那个功效就有点问题了。

En: Okay. And why do I know this?
Zh: 好吧。所以我为什么知道这个？

En: Because you're a *scientist*, of course.
Zh: 当然因为你是个*科学家*了。

En: Yeah, you got the *magic touch*...
Zh: 是啊，你有*魔力*……

En: It's quite expensive, miss. But we'll manage without your help for now.
Zh: “挺贵的，小姐。不过没有你的帮助，我们也能应付的。”

En: Please answer the question, miss. Did he enjoy the moment of his death?
Zh: “请回答问题，小姐。他享受自己死亡的那一刻吗？”

En: It's just a feeling I have. Indulge me.
Zh: “这只是我的一种感觉。满足我一下吧。”

En: Just have her answer the question. Don't give explanations.
Zh: 让她回答问题就好。不要解释。

En: It came to me in an *occult vision*.
Zh: “它以一种*神秘的幻象*出现在我脑海里。”

En: Cool. The woman nods.
Zh: “厉害。”女人点点头。

En: There is a sound. Something in the reeds, when the wind combs through them. On the inlet, on the little islands there between castaway tyres.
Zh: 有一个声音。当风吹拂过芦苇丛的时候，里面有什么东西。在入海口，小岛上废弃轮胎的中间。

En: There is something we have all forgotten.
Zh: “有些事我们全都忘记了。”

En: Her light brown eyes fix on yours, then disappear behind her bangs as she drops her chin.
Zh: 她用浅棕色的眼睛盯着你，不过当她垂低下巴的时候，它们就消失在了她的刘海后面。

En: She agrees.
Zh: 她同意了。

En: Shake it off.
Zh: 摆脱掉。

En: It's moving... The motes under your eyes stand still. Say something.
Zh: 它在动……你眼睛下面的微粒纹丝不动。说点什么。

En: It very well could be, yes.
Zh: “很有可能，是的。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: What *do* I mean? She raises an eyebrow. "I have no idea. I don't even know what *you* mean. Love did him in? What does that mean?"
Zh: “我*是*什么意思？”她扬起一边的眉毛。“我不知道。我甚至不明白*你*的意思。爱情杀了他？这是什么意思？”

En: Coolly, gracefully, she pours herself more coffee.
Zh: 她给自己又倒了些咖啡，镇静而优雅。

En: I don't think he *enjoyed* dying, officer. He had too much left to do -- too many third world conflicts. Couldn't tap out just yet.
Zh: “我不觉得他很*享受*死亡，警官。他还有很多事要做——太多第三世界国家的冲突了。现在离开还太早了。”

En: Everything checks out here. It's all A-Okay. Good answer.
Zh: 这里一切都检验完毕了。全都很好。不错的答案。

En: That's a strange thing to suggest. The laedy iseth faere and juste!
Zh: 这种说法很奇怪。这位女士美丽又公正！

En: Well, it's wrong. He had too many drinks to down and too many bad things to do. Couldn't tap out just yet.
Zh: “呃，这是不对的。他还有很多酒没喝，还有很多坏事要做。现在离开还太早了。”

En: Occult vision? Of course. I do not -- for one moment -- think it wasn't an occult vision. I do not doubt *the signs*, but maybe... She draws it out...
Zh: “神秘幻象？当然了。我一刻也没有觉得它不是个神秘幻象。我不怀疑那个*标志*，不过也许……”她开了口……

En: ...*mayyyybe* you're interpreting them wrong? He enjoyed life, you see. A little too much, if anything. Up to the point where other people had to pay for it.
Zh: “……*也——许*你理解错了？你看，他很享受生活。如果说有什么的话，是有点享受过头了。甚至到了其他人要为之付出代价的地步。”

En: Wow. A superstar *and* a mystic. How intriguing. I have no doubt this is all adequate and true. I do not doubt *the signs*, but maybe... She draws it out...
Zh: “哇哦。一个超级明星，*而且*还是个神秘主义者。真吸引人。我毫不怀疑这一切都是真实而充分的。我不怀疑那个*标志*，不过也许……”她开了口……

En: They're not hard core *in that way* is what I'm trying to say. What are we talking about anyway? This politics shit is *a lot* in the morning. *Is* it even morning?
Zh: “我想说的是，他们并不是在*那个方面*硬核。不过我们到底在说些什么？早上聊政治什么的实在是有点*太过了*。现在*还是*早上吗？”

En: Good for you.
Zh: “对你有好处。”

En: I don't know how a semen sample works, officers. How many days after intercourse does it have to be -- I don't even know if he had sex with someone else. We didn't *go steady*.
Zh: “我不知道精液样本是怎么回事，警官。不知道需要性关系发生后多少天——我甚至不知道他有没有跟别人上床。我们没有*稳定*下来。”

En: He told me -- love did him in.
Zh: “他告诉我——爱情杀了他。”

En: Okay then. Another question.
Zh: “那好吧。换个问题。”

En: That's not funny, officer.
Zh: “这一点也不好笑，警官。”

En: Her voice is like a slash through the air. Her shoulders tense up.
Zh: 她的声音就像一片划破空气的利刃。肩膀也变得紧绷起来。

En: There. She momentarily lost control over Straight-Back-Guy! It appears that she feels *guilty*.
Zh: 看见没。她因为那个直男一时失控了！她好像感到有些*内疚*。

En: Of course, she says quickly.
Zh: “当然，”她快速回应到。

En: Of course I do. I'm hung over. I feel guilty about everything.
Zh: “我当然有了。我喝醉了。我对一切都感到内疚。”

En: Among many other things, yes. I could have done something. There's always *something* you can do, right? She looks into her coffee. "Ask me something else."
Zh: “对于很多其他事情而言，是的，我感到内疚。我可能做过一些事情。你总能做点*什么*，对吧？”她看着咖啡。“问我点别的吧。”

En: Do you feel guilty about what happened to him?
Zh: “你对他身上发生的事感到内疚吗？”

En: What is this, an interrogation? You didn't tell her this was going to be an interrogation.
Zh: 这是干什么，审讯吗？你没说过要审讯她啊。

En: What do you think it will tell us?
Zh: “你觉得它会告诉我们什么？”

En: Is she avoiding anything? Technically not.
Zh: 她是在回避吗？从严格的意义上讲没有。

En: See? There's something off here. This one *must* know she's deploying flares and countermeasures.
Zh: 看见了吗？这里有些不对劲的地方。这一个人*肯定*知道她在放烟雾弹和想对策。

En: Alright. The lieutenant puts the slip back in his notes and observes the young woman for a moment.
Zh: “好吧。”警督把报告放回笔记本里，观察了年轻女子一会儿。

En: Yes, miss. I hope you don't mind. The lieutenant hands you his notes.
Zh: “是的，小姐。希望你不要介意。”警督把他的笔记递给了你。

En: There is a little slip of questions that keeps everything in *order*. The questions are inside a blue notebook. The notebook is inside the lieutenant's pocket. Before the questions leave your mouth, you realize he should be here for this.
Zh: 要想保证一切都*井然有序*，还存在一个小问题。问题就在一个蓝色的笔记本里。笔记本在警督的口袋里。在你问出这些问题之前，你意识到他也应该在场。

En: Unfortunately, it is completely busted.
Zh: “很不幸，它完全崩溃了。”

En: No can do.
Zh: “无能为力。”

En: Ah, another broken thing in a line of broken things. She takes a drag. "Well, perhaps you'll find one somewhere."
Zh: “啊，一连串破事里又多了一件。”她抽了一口烟。“好吧，也许你会在别的什么地方找到一个。”

En: No can do.
Zh: “无能为力。”

En: Unfortunately, it is completely busted.
Zh: “很不幸，它完全崩溃了。”

En: Ah, another broken thing in a line of broken things. She takes a drag. "Well, perhaps you'll find one elsewhere."
Zh: “啊，一连串破事里又多了一件。”她抽了一口烟。“好吧，也许你会在别的什么地方找到一个。”

En: Ah, something happened to it? Another broken thing in a line of broken things. Quite a shame. She takes a drag. "Well, perhaps you'll find another one."
Zh: “啊，它出什么事了吗？一连串破事里又多了一件。真遗憾。”她抽了一口烟。“好吧，也许你会在别的什么地方找到一个。”

En: Ah, something happened to it? Another broken thing in a line of broken things. Quite a shame. She takes a drag. "Well, perhaps you'll find another one."
Zh: “啊，它出什么事了吗？一连串破事里又多了一件。真遗憾。”她抽了一口烟。“好吧，也许你会在别的什么地方找到一个。”

En: Lely? The lieutenant takes out his notebook. "That was his name?"
Zh: “莱利？”警督掏出笔记本。“这是他的名字吗？”

En: As I already told your partner, I didn't actually know his name. I just called him Lely -- it's short for Lelystad, where he came from.
Zh: “我已经跟你的搭档说过了，我其实并不知道他的名字。我就叫他莱利——莱利斯塔德的缩写，他就是从那里来的。”

En: Yes, he said his real name wasn't *his*. I tried to pry it out of him, but he never gave it up -- no matter how hard I tried.
Zh: “是的，他说他的真名不是*他的*。我试过撬开他的嘴，但他永远也不肯放弃——不管我有多努力。”

En: It was his *army name*.
Zh: “这是他在*军队的名字*。”

En: She tries to light another cigarette. It's windy, but she gets it done.
Zh: 她试着点燃另一支香烟。风有点大，不过她还是成功了。

En: No, it doesn't check out. Can't you see she's clearly avoiding something here?
Zh: 不，并没有通过检验。你没看见她明显就是在回避什么吗？

En: Wow. A mystic. How intriguing. I have no doubt this is all adequate and true. I do not doubt *the signs*, but maybe... She draws it out...
Zh: “哇哦。一个神秘主义者。真吸引人。我毫不怀疑这一切都是真实而充分的。我不怀疑那个*标志*，不过也许……”她开了口……

En: Titus says he's got a tape where the deceased discusses committing rape. Tell me about it.
Zh: “提图斯说他有一盘磁带，死者在里面讨论了实施强暴的事。跟我说说吧。”

En: You haven't listened to it? She blows a column of smoke in the air. "You sure you don't want to hear it before questioning me?"
Zh: “你还没听过吗？”她朝空中吐出一道烟柱。“你确定在问我之前自己不先听听吗？”

En: This is my investigation. I run it my way.
Zh: “这是我的调查。我要用我自己的方式。”

En: I think I can rely on your honesty.
Zh: “我觉得我可以依赖你的诚实。”

En: I have no time for tapes. There's a murderer on the loose!
Zh: “我没时间听磁带。有一个杀手还逍遥法外呢！”

En: Of course, officer. I'll cooperate. She taps her cigarette as she nods.  "I'm guessing he tried to give you the Doorgunner Megamix, yeah?"
Zh: “当然了，警官。我会配合的。”她轻弹一下香烟，点了点头。“我猜他是想给你门枪手精选集，对吧？”

En: Of course, officer. No time to lose. She taps her cigarette as she nods. "I'm guessing he tried to give you the Doorgunner Megamix, yeah?"
Zh: “当然了，警官。没时间浪费了。”她轻弹一下香烟，点了点头。“我猜他是想给你门枪手精选集，对吧？”

En: Sure. You can trust me. She taps her cigarette as she nods. "I'm guessing he tried to give you the Doorgunner Megamix, yeah?"
Zh: “当然。你可以相信我。”她轻弹一下香烟，点了点头。“我猜他是想给你门枪手精选集，对吧？”

En: They've got him saying he'd like to rape and kill. Do Revachol *Soldier of the Apocalypse*-style. It really upset the boys. Ash falls from her cigarette as she shrugs. "I never said he was a good man -- only that he wasn't bad to me."
Zh: “他们让他亲口说出打算强暴和杀人。要让瑞瓦肖领教一下*末日战士的风格*。让那些男孩心烦意乱。”她耸耸肩，一些烟灰掉落下来。“我从没说过他是个好人——只是他对我一直不坏。”

En: That's about it, I think. It's not a very long tape. She takes another drag of her cigarette.
Zh: 她又抽了一口烟。“我想，说得大概就是这个。那盘磁带的内容并不是很长。”

En: And you spent time with this person? Romantically?
Zh: “你跟这个人在一起过吗？很浪漫的那种？”

En: A guy like that didn't scare you?
Zh: “你不害怕那种家伙吗？”

En: Why would I be scared? Your mother probably never told you this, but girls are *evil*.
Zh: “我为什么要害怕？你的母亲可能永远都不会告诉你这个，但是女孩都是很*邪恶*的。”

En: We're all scraping up any happiness we can find, officer. Going around with our little scouring sticks -- you, your first love, Mr. Soldier of the Apocalypse here...
Zh: “我们都在到处积攒所有能找到的幸福和快乐，警官。带着我们的小研磨棒到处游走，这里刮刮那里蹭蹭——你，你的初恋，还有这位末日战士……”

En: Did he tell you he had actually *done* any of those things -- here in Martinaise, I mean?
Zh: “他有没有告诉过你，自己实际上有没有*做过*那些事情——我的意思是说，在马丁内斯？”

En: You like this kind of stuff?
Zh: “你喜欢这种东西？”

En: There is a small measure of pride in her about this. That she could comfort and quell the rage in such a being.
Zh: 她有一点小小的骄傲。因为她能抚慰和平息这样一个人的怒火。

En: No. We were too busy laying waste to our own nervous systems to direct any of the *fury* outward. He seemed... She pauses. "He seemed happy, I guess. At ease. As much as a man like him could be."
Zh: “没有。我们忙着破坏自己的神经系统，把所有的*愤怒*向外发泄。他似乎……”她想了一会儿。“我觉得他似乎很高兴。自由自在。像他这样的男人能做多少就是多少。”

En: Had I the physical robustness and social support, *I* would be tearing it up *Soldier of the Apocalypse*-style...
Zh: “如果我拥有强壮的身体和社会支持，*我*也会像*末日战士*一样把它撕碎……”

En: Did he tell you he had actually *done* any of those things? Here in Martinaise, I mean.
Zh: “他有没有告诉过你，自己实际上有没有*做过*那些事情——我的意思是说，在马丁内斯？”

En: Can you say any more about what's on the tape?
Zh: “这盘磁带里的内容你还知道些什么吗？”

En: I'll ask one last time: Do you still deny that he assaulted you in any way?
Zh: “我再问你最后一次：你还是否认他以任何方式侵犯过你吗？”

En: That'll be all about the tape. Thank you for your time.
Zh: “磁带的事就说到这里吧。感谢你的配合。”

En: Any time, officer. She removes her cigarette from between her lips. Smoke trails from her mouth.
Zh: 她把香烟从双唇之间拿开。烟雾从她的嘴边袅袅升起。“随时为你效劳，警官。”

En: I'm quite sure he didn't, officer. The young woman looks at you, bored, but smiles politely.
Zh: 年轻女子看着你，似乎觉得有些无聊，不过还是礼貌地笑了。“我很肯定他没有，警官。”

En: The tape? Hm... She searches her memory. Slowly, she starts smiling a strange smile.
Zh: “磁带？嗯……”她在记忆里搜索着。慢慢的，她的脸上露出一个奇怪的笑容。

En: I think he said *whore* a lot... He said this wasn't Revachol. He said it was a fucking village with village elephants and that he was gonna do us all *Co Hoi-style*.
Zh: “我想他说过很多次*婊子*……他说这不是瑞瓦肖。他说那只是个该死的村子，里面有大象，而且还说要用*科霍依风格*对付我们所有人。”

En: Coping mechanism?
Zh: “应对机制？”

En: Running joke. I was gonna say running joke -- and that tape didn't even get the good bits. Lely's punchlines got way, way funkier.
Zh: “笑柄。我打算说是笑柄——听起来你根本没明白精彩的部分。莱利的俏皮话远比那个要有意思多了。”

En: Catch phrase?
Zh: “口头禅？”

En: Did you learn this from studying Oranjese lit? Sounds psychological.
Zh: “你是研究奥兰治文学的时候学会的吗？听起来跟心理学有关。”

En: People who go to the University know how to use words like *internalize* and *integrate*, officer... now what was that expression? She's still looking for it.
Zh: “上大学的人知道怎么使用*内在化*和*一体化*的词汇，警官……现在，那是什么表情？”她还在寻找。

En: There's more to this. She has an index in her head of pathologies and how to exploit them. You can almost see her turning the pages.
Zh: 远远不止这样。她的脑子里有一个精神异常索引，而且也知道如何利用。我几乎能看见她在脑子里翻动书页的样子。

En: Co Hoi, huh?
Zh: “科霍依，是吗？”

En: Why'd he say things like that? Machismo?
Zh: “他为什么要这么说？大男子主义吗？”

En: Why'd he say things like that? Was he bragging?
Zh: “他为什么要这么说？他是在吹牛吗？”

En: Was he bragging?
Zh: “他是在吹牛吗？”

En: He liked the way it *sounded* when he said it. As for Co Hoi... The young woman lights a new cigarette with the butt of her old one.
Zh: “他喜欢它*听起来*的那种感觉。至于科霍依……”年轻女子用旧烟蒂点燃一支新的烟。

En: Why did he say *whore* so much?
Zh: “他为什么老是说*婊子*？”

En: He wasn't actually there -- not from what he told me, at least. But he might as well have been. I could see he'd lived his own little Co Hoi. It was in his... *everything*.
Zh: “他其实并没有去过那里——至少没跟我说过。不过也可能去过。我能看出他生活在自己的小科霍依世界里。影响着他的……*一切*。”

En: Oh no, I'm pretty sure he *did* terrible things -- then integrated them into his idea of normalcy. To keep on living. Until they just... sort of turned into his... What's the word I'm looking for?
Zh: “不是，我很肯定他*做过*那些事——然后把那些都融入了他对常态的看法里面。为了能继续生活下去。直到它们……有点像是变成了他的……”她想了想。“我该用什么词好呢？”

En: He was like the Semenese conflict, the Co Hoi massacre, and the '36 famine in Yeesut all rolled into one person, then encased in Oranjese ceramic armour. Which he wore in bed *and* in the shower...
Zh: “他就像塞美尼冲突，科霍依大屠杀，还有伊苏特36年的大饥荒，全部都集中在了一个人身上，然后镶铸了奥兰治陶瓷盔甲。他在床上*还有*洗澡的时候都会穿在身上……”

En: No, I'm pretty sure he *did* terrible things -- and then had to internalize them to keep on living. Until they just... sort of turned into his... What's the word I'm looking for?
Zh: “不，我很肯定他*做过*那些事——然后把它们全都内在化，这样才能继续生活下去。直到它们……有点像是变成了他的……”她想了想。“我该用什么词好呢？”

En: Does it matter that he said *whore*? I said it too.
Zh: “他说*婊子*有什么关系吗？我也说啊。”

En: She's quite observant about the human character -- trained almost...
Zh: 她非常善于观察人性——甚至训练过……

En: It suits you, officer. You should say it more. As for Co Hoi...
Zh: “很适合你，警官。你应该多说一些。至于科霍依……”

En: It's not a problem. She touches his shoulder. "As for Co Hoi..."
Zh: “这不是问题。”她碰了下他的肩膀。“至于科霍依，警官……”

En: She clearly enjoys siding with you.
Zh: 她显然很喜欢站在你这边。

En: She clearly enjoyed saying that to you.
Zh: 她显然很乐意对你这么说。

En: Do you think he was trying to scare people?
Zh: “你觉得他是想吓唬别人吗？”

En: I don't see how your problems are relevant right now, officer, the lieutenant says in a quick, clipped, *annoyed* tone.
Zh: “我现在看不出你的问题跟这个有什么关系，警官，”警督用一种快速、简短又*窝火*的语气说到。

En: You can trust Pillar-Bookhead, by the way. As far as I can tell, he's not singing to her tune... the *only* one besides me.
Zh: 顺便说一句，你可以相信榆木脑袋。据我所知，他没有附和她的步调……除了我之外，*只有*他。

En: Persona?
Zh: “伪装？”

En: That's enough. I get the picture.
Zh: “够了。我明白了。”

En: Mhm. She looks down at her feet, still smiling slightly. "Anything else, officer?"
Zh: “嗯。”她低头看着双脚，脸上还挂着一丝微笑。“还有别的事吗，警官？”

En: Co Hoi, a village that was the site of infamous war crimes in the South Safre conflict, the ongoing proxy war between Graad and Safre. It has been hot for 12 years, with atrocities piling up daily. Mostly committed by Graad.
Zh: 科霍依，南萨弗里冲突中一个恶名昭彰的战争罪行现场，当时格拉德和萨弗里正在进行代理战争。战火在那里持续燃烧了整整12年，暴行与日俱增。主要是格拉德犯下的。

En: Oh, come on -- you know better than that!
Zh: 拜托——你知道的远不止这些吧！

En: A memory surfaces in her damaged neocortex -- it's not entirely unpleasant.
Zh: 她受损的大脑皮层中浮现出一段记忆——也不能说完全不愉快。

En: Wait! Where's Kim? The man with all the notes -- he *needs* to be here for this.
Zh: 等等！金在哪里？那个带着所有笔记的男人——这里*需要*他。

En: You should record her responses, but you do not have a notebook. Kim, on the other hand, does.
Zh: 你应该记录下她的回应，不过你没有笔记本。但是金呢，他有。

En: Hold up. Don't go it alone -- it's a marathon, and you'll need support. Get Kim before you go any further.
Zh: 等一下。不要单打独斗——这是一场马拉松，你需要支持。在你继续之前，还是先把金找来吧。

En: Hm? Oh -- I'm guessing your partner should be here too? She taps the ash from the end of her cigarette while you stand there in silence.
Zh: 她弹了弹烟头上的烟灰，而你沉默地站在原地。“嗯？哦——我猜你的搭档也应该一起是吧？”

En: It isn't much closure, but it is enough. She is ready to talk about him now.
Zh: 这算不是结束，不过也够了。现在她已经准备谈论他的事情了。

En: He went to Killer Academy in Vredefort. Then he killed some people on the Semenine islands. And on other islands too -- all of the islands. After this he came to Revachol and got killed himself.
Zh: “他去了弗里德堡的杀手学校。然后在塞美尼群岛上杀了一些人。还有其他岛上——所有岛上都杀过人。在那之后，他就来到了瑞瓦肖，自己被杀了。”
